persian_text: او اگر دیوانه است و فتنهکاو داروی دیوانه باشد کیر گاو
english_translation: If he is mad and bent on mischief the cure for a madman is an oxhide whip If he is mad and bent on mischief the cure for a madman is a whip an oxs penis

persian_text: این در آید سر نهند او را بتان آن در آید سر نهد چون امتان
english_translation: This one enters the idols lay down their heads before him that one enters and lays down his head  like the peoples

persian_text: بادهای که آن بر سر شاهان جهد تاج زر بر تارک ساقی نهد
english_translation: a wine that would fly to the head of kings and put a golden tiara on the crown of the cupbearers head

persian_text: همچو خورشید و چو ماه پاکباز آنچ گیرند از ضیا بدهند باز
english_translation: like the sun and the spendthrift moon they give back the radiance that they receive

persian_text: تو نتوانی ابروی من ساختن چون توانی جان من بشناختن
english_translation: Thou canst not fashion my eyebrow how canst thou know my soul

persian_text: کعبهی درویش بودی کوی او آمدندی مستمندان سوی او
english_translation: His abode was the Kaba of the poor the distressed were coming to him

persian_text: هر کجا تابم ز مشکات دمی حل شد آن جا مشکلات عالمی
english_translation: Wheresoever I shine forth from the lampniche of a breath  there the difficulties of a world are resolved

persian_text: گوش داری تو به گوش خود شنو گوش گولان را چرا باشی گرو
english_translation: you have an ear hearken with your own ear why be dependent on the ears of blockheads

persian_text: آن سرش گوید سمعنا ای صبا پای او گوید عصینا خلنا
english_translation: Its head says We obey O zephyr Its foot says We refuse to obey let us alone

persian_text: من نمیگویم مرا هدیه دهید بلک گفتم لایق هدیه شوید
english_translation: I do not bid you bestow gifts on me nay I bid you be worthy of the gifts

persian_text: مایهی خونابه و زردآبه را جوش دادن از برای لابه را
english_translation: To set boiling the source of bloody tears for the sake of humble entreaties

persian_text: نیست اندر عنصرش حرص و هوا نور مطلق زنده از عشق خدا
english_translation: In his original nature is no concupiscence and sensuality he is absolute light living through love of God

persian_text: گفت همره را گرو نه پیش من ور نه قربانی تو اندر کیش من
english_translation: He said Place thy comrade with me as a pledge otherwise thou art a sacrifice according to my law

persian_text: لیک در سیمای آن در یتیم دیدهام آثار لطفت ای کریم
english_translation: But in the countenance of that unique Pearl I have beheld the signs of Thy grace O Bounteous One

persian_text: لا یعد این داد و لا یحصی ز تو من کلیلم از بیانش شرمرو
english_translation: These gifts of Thine are not to be numbered or computed in setting them forth I am tonguetied and shamefaced

persian_text: وانکه اندر وهم او ترک ادب بیادب را سرنگونی داد رب
english_translation: And him in whose judgement there is irreverence the Lord hath doomed the irreverent to fall headlong

persian_text: لیک حیوانی که چوپانش خداست گرگ را آنجا امید و ره کجاست
english_translation: But the animal whose shepherd is Godhow hath the wolf hope or way there

persian_text: این رها کن عشقهای صورتی نیست بر صورت نه بر روی ستی
english_translation: Give up this  Loves for what is endued with form have not as their object the form or the ladys face

persian_text: خیک اشکم گو بدر از موج آبگر بمیرم هست مرگم مستطاب
english_translation: Let the waterskin my belly be burst by the waves of the water if I die my death is acceptable

persian_text: آفتابی که بگرداند قفاش از برای غصه و قهر خفاش
english_translation: The Sun who turns his back on account of the rage and violence of the bat

persian_text: بار دیگر سوخت هم واپس بجست باز کردش حرص دل ناسی و مست
english_translation: Once more he is scorched and recoils again the greed of his heart makes him forgetful and intoxicated

persian_text: این همه مردن نه مرگ صورتست این بدن مر روح را چون آلتست
english_translation: All this dying is not the death of the form this body is like an instrument for the spirit

persian_text: مشفقی مسکیننوازی عادلی جوهری زربخششی دریادلی
english_translation: a compassionate man kind to the poor and just a jewel  goldlavishing oceanhearted

persian_text: زین سپس من نشنوم آن دمدمه بانگ دیوان است و غولان آن همه
english_translation: After this I will not hearken to their palaver all that is the cry of demons and ghouls

persian_text: زو نیابی سود و مایهگر خرد نبودش خود قیمت عقل و خرد
english_translation: Thou wilt not get interest or capital from him if he purchase  in sooth he has not the price for reason and intellect

persian_text: پنج حس ظاهر و پنج اندرون ده صفاند اندر قیام الصافون
english_translation: The five external and the five internal senses are in ten ranks in the standing posture of the ranked

persian_text: چون نماند واسطهی تن بیحجاب همچو موسی نور مه یابد ز جیب
english_translation: When the bodily medium is removed he perceives without screen like Moses the light of the Moon from bosom

persian_text: آدمی منکر ز تسبیح جماد و آن جماد اندر عبادت اوستاد
english_translation: Man disbelieves in the glorification uttered by inanimate things but those inanimate things are masters in worship

persian_text: از حریصی وز هوای سروری در نظر کند و بلافیدن جری
english_translation: He  because of covetousness and desire for eminence is dull as regards vision and bold in prating

persian_text: حق همیدید آن ولی ستارخوست لیک چون از حد بری غماز اوست
english_translation: God saw it but He is disposed to cover up  yet when you carry beyond bounds He is a telltale

persian_text: میوهای که بر شکافیدی ز زور همچو آب از میوه جستی برق نور
english_translation: From the fruit that was bursting forcibly flashes of light would spurt forth like juice

persian_text: تا دم عیسی ترا زنده کند همچو خویشت خوب و فرخنده کند
english_translation: That the breath of Jesus may revive thee and make thee fair and blessed as itself

persian_text: آن رخام خوب و آن سنگ شریف برج زندان را بهی بود و الیف
english_translation: The beautiful marble and the noble stone of the prisonbastion were pleasing and agreeable

persian_text: بود از مستی شهوت شادمان در فرو بست و همیگفت آن زمان
english_translation: Propter ebrietatem libidinis gaudebat she shut the door saying meanwhile She was happy from the intoxication of lust she shut the door saying meanwhile

persian_text: بلک خود را در صفا گوری کنی در منی او کنی دفن منی
english_translation: Nay but by digging for yourself a grave in purity and burying egoism in His egoism

persian_text: سنگ را هرگز بگوید کس بیا از کلوخی کس کجا جوید وفا
english_translation: One never says Come to a stone how should any one request a brickbat to keep faith

persian_text: اندرین بود او که شیخ نامدار زود پیش افتاد بر شیری سوار
english_translation: He was in this when suddenly the renowned Shaykh appeared before him riding on a lion

persian_text: صورت دیوار و سقف هر مکان سایهی اندیشهی معمار دان
english_translation: Know that the form of the walls and roof of every dwellingplace is a shadow of the thought of the architect

persian_text: چون جمال احمدی در هر دو کون کی بدستای فر یزدانیش عون
english_translation: When has there been in the two worlds a beauty like that of Ahmad  Oh may the Divine Glory aid him

persian_text: این سفال و این پلیته دیگرست لیک نورش نیست دیگر زان سرست
english_translation: This earthenware lamp and this wick are different but its light is not different it is from Yonder

persian_text: با منجم این همه انجم به جنگ کای رها کرده تو جان بگزیده رنگ
english_translation: All these stars are at war with the astronomer saying O thou who hast let the spirit go and hast preferred colour

persian_text: آن دلی کو عاشق مالست و جاه یا زبون این گل و آب سیاه
english_translation: Is that the heart that is enamoured of riches and power or is submissive to this black earth and water

persian_text: گر ترازو نبود آن خصم از جدال کی رهد از وهم حیف و احتیال
english_translation: If there be no pair of scales how shall the litigant escape from disputing when he suspects fraud and deceit

persian_text: که بهاران خطهی ده خوش بود کشتزار و لالهی دلکش بود
english_translation: For in spring the countryside is pleasant there are sown fields and lovely anemones

persian_text: زانک چون مغزش در آگند و رسید پوستها شد بس رقیق و واکفید
english_translation: Because inasmuch as its kernel had filled and had reached maturity the rinds became very thin and burst

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر که آری لیک ما بر سر راهیم و بر عزم غزا
english_translation: The Prophet said  Yes  but we are intending to march and starting on a campaign

persian_text: کان نظر شیرین کنندهی رنجهاست ساحران را خونبهای دست و پاست
english_translation: For that look makes pains sweet it is the bloodprice to the magicians for their hands and feet

persian_text: جان چو خفته در گل و نسرین بود چه غمست ار تن در آن سرگین بود
english_translation: When the spirit is lying at rest amidst roses and eglantines what does it matter if the body is in that dung

persian_text: من دعا کورانه کی میکردهام جز به خالق کدیه کی آوردهام
english_translation: When have I been praying in the fashion of the blind When have I begged of any one except the Creator

persian_text: گر خر اول توبه و سوگند خورد عاقبت هم از خری خبطی بکرد
english_translation: If at first the ass repented and swore  in the end because of his asininity he made a lapse

persian_text: این چه صبر است این صبوری از چه روست یا مگر پیش تو این جانت عدوست
english_translation: What patience is this For what reason is this great fortitude Or perchance in your opinion this life of yours is an enemy

persian_text: زانک از عزت به خواری آمدن همچو قطع عضو باشد از بدن
english_translation: For to come from high to low estate is like the amputation of a limb from the body

persian_text: طالبست و غالبست آن کردگار تا ز هستیها بر آرد او دمار
english_translation: The Agent is seeking and prevailing  to the end that He may utterly destroy existences

persian_text: هر جمادی که کند رو در نبات از درخت بخت او روید حیات
english_translation: Every mineral that sets its face towards the plant life grows from the tree of its fortune

persian_text: اسپ داند بانگ و بوی شیر راگر چه حیوانست الا نادرا
english_translation: The horse though it is an animal knows the roar and smell of the lion except in rare instances

persian_text: آفتابی دید او جامد نماند روغن گل روغن کنجد نماند
english_translation: He beheld a Sun and remained frozen no more the oil of roses was no longer oil of sesame

persian_text: کاو گرسنه خفته باشد بیخبر و آن دو پستان میخلد زو مهر در
english_translation: For it may have fallen asleep hungry unawares and those two breasts are tingling in love for it

persian_text: بانگ بر رسته ز بر بسته بدان تاج شاهان را ز تاج هدهدان
english_translation: Know the natural cry from the artificial one the crown of kings from the crown of hoopoes

persian_text: دید قومی در نظر آراسته قوم دیگر منتظر برخاسته
english_translation: He beheld some people arrayed in the sight  and others who had risen to their feet waiting

persian_text: آنچ این فرعون میترسد ازو هست شد این دم که گشتم جفت تو
english_translation: That of which this Pharaoh is afraid came into being at the moment when I lay with thee

persian_text: کلوخ انداختن تشنه از سر دیوار در جوی آب
english_translation: How the thirsty man threw bricks from the top of the wall into the stream of water

persian_text: بنده گوید آنچ فرمودی بیان صد چنانم صد چنانم صد چنان
english_translation: The servant answers I am a hundred hundred hundred times as much as that which Thou hast declared

persian_text: قهر را از لطف داند هر کسی خواه دانا خواه نادان یا خسی
english_translation: Every one knows mercy from vengeance whether he be wise or ignorant or vile

persian_text: ور بدانستند لحن همدگر فهم آن چون کرد بینطقی بشر
english_translation: And if they knew each others accents how should man understand it  without any articulation

persian_text: نه نیاز و نه جمالی بهر ناز تو بتویش گنده مانند پیاز
english_translation: Neither humble supplication nor any beauty to show pride his  coat on coat is stinking like an onion

persian_text: خانه آن تست هر چت میل هست بر گزین خود هر دو عالم اندکست
english_translation: The house is thine choose anything thou desirest in truth the two worlds are little

persian_text: قبض و بسط چشم دل از ذوالجلال دم به دم چون میکند سحر حلال
english_translation: Inasmuch as the closing and opening of the eye of the heart by the Almighty is continually working lawful magic

persian_text: چون رسید اندر سبا این نور شرق غلغلی افتاد در بلقیس و خلق
english_translation: When this orient light reached Sabá a tumult arose in Bilqís and her people

persian_text: مرد دق باشد چو یخ هر لحظه کم لیک پندارد بهر دم بهترم
english_translation: The man suffering from phthisis dwindles incessantly like ice but at every moment he thinks he is better

persian_text: طبع من دیگر نگشت و عنصرم تیغ حقم هم به دستوری برم
english_translation: My nature and element have not changed I am the sword of God and by leave I cut

persian_text: تعجب کردن آدم علیهالسلام از ضلالت ابلیس لعین و عجب آوردن
english_translation: How Adam on whom be peace marvelled at the perdition of the accursed Iblís and showed vanity

persian_text: تا که در هر گوش ناید این سخن یک همیگویم ز صد سر لدن
english_translation: In order that this subject may not come to every ear I am telling one out of a hundred esoteric mysteries

persian_text: ای اخی دست از دعا کردن مدار با اجابت یا رد اویت چه کار
english_translation: O comrade do not refrain from invoking  what business do you have with His acceptance or rejection

persian_text: یا چو اسمعیل صبار مجید پیش عشق و خنجرش حلقی کشید
english_translation: Or like the muchenduring and glorious Ismáíl  offered a throat to Love and his dagger

persian_text: با دو پر بر میپرد چون جبرئیل بیگمان و بی مگر بی قال و قیل
english_translation: He flies aloft with two wings like Gabriel without opinion and without peradventure and without disputation

persian_text: تا همه زوار گرد او تنند کعبهی او را همه قبله کنند
english_translation: In order that all the pilgrims might gather round him and might all turn in worship to his Kaba

persian_text: ور نخواهی خدمت ابنای جنس در دهان اژدهایی همچو خرس
english_translation: And if you are unwilling to serve the men of kind you are in the dragons mouth like the bear

persian_text: هر دمت طوفان و کشتیای مقل با غم و شادیت کرد او متصل
english_translation: At every moment O man of little means He has conjoined with your grief and gladness an ark and a flood

persian_text: بر در و دیوار جسم گلسرشت حق ز غیرت نقش صد صوفی نبشت
english_translation: Upon the door and wall of the body moulded of clay God in His jealousy traced the figures of a hundred Súfís

persian_text: ملاقات پادشاه با آن ولی که در خوابش نمودند
english_translation: The meeting of the king with the saint whose coming had been shown to him in a dream

persian_text: نه ز بغداد و نه موصل نه طراز در کشی در نی و نی راه دراز
english_translation: Not from Baghdád and not from Mosul and not from Tiráz you will draw out a long journey in not and not

persian_text: اشتهای گولگردی آمدت یا ملولی وطن غالب شدت
english_translation: Did you feel a craving to indulge in foolish sightseeing or were you overcome by disgust with your home

persian_text: گریه چون از حد گذشت وهایهای زو که بیگریه بد او خود دل ربای
english_translation: When the tears and sobs passed beyond boundsfrom her who was fascinating even without tears

persian_text: گنجهای خاک تا هفتم طبق عرضه کرده بود پیش شیخ حق
english_translation: God had offered to the Shaykh the treasures of the earth down to the seventh tier

persian_text: تا نتاند شیر علم دین کشید تا نگردد گرد آن قصر مشید
english_translation: That he might not be able to drink the milk of knowledge of religion and that he might not roam around that lofty castle

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر که دستت هر چه برد بایدش در عاقبت واپس سپرد
english_translation: The Prophet said that whatever your hand has taken must in the end be restored

persian_text: یا نبود آن کار او را خود گهر یا نیامد وقت پاداش از قدر
english_translation: Either the essential thing was not in his work at all or by decree the hour for the reward has not yet arrived

persian_text: دست را مسپار جز در دست پیر حق شدست آن دست او را دستگیر
english_translation: Do not surrender thy hand save to the hand of the Pír  God hath become the aider of his hand

persian_text: مدتی دندانکنان این میکشید نارسیده عمر او آخر رسید
english_translation: For a long time gnashing his teeth he suffered this  ere he attained his life reached its end

persian_text: بعد از آن کودک به کوسه بنگریست گفت او با آن دو مو از غم بریست
english_translation: After that the boy looked at the youth and said He is quit of trouble by reason of the two hairs

persian_text: دیر گیرد سخت گیرد رحمتش یک دمت غایب ندارد حضرتش
english_translation: His Mercy is long in gripping grips tight His Presence does not keep you absent for one moment

persian_text: ستر چه در پشم و پنبه آذرست تا همیپوشیش او پیداترست
english_translation: What covering  The fire is in the wool cotton whilst thou art covering it up it is more manifest

persian_text: چون عمر بر آسمان مه را ندید گفت کاین مه از خیال تو دمید
english_translation: As Umar did not see the moon in the sky he said This moon has risen from thy phantasy

persian_text: امردی و کوسهای در انجمن آمدند و مجمعی بد در وطن
english_translation: A beardless boy and a youth with a few hairs on his chin came to a festive gathering for there was an assemblyplace in the town

persian_text: آب نیلست این حدیث جانفزا یا ربش در چشم قبطی خون نما
english_translation: This spiritaugmenting discourse is the water of the Nile O Lord let it seem blood to the eye of the Egyptian

persian_text: میشود مبدل بسوز مریمی شاخ لب خشکی به نخلی خرمی
english_translation: A drylipped bough is changed into a flourishing palmtree by the burning of a Mary

persian_text: آنکه از بادی رود از جا خسی است ز آنکه باد ناموافق خود بسی است
english_translation: That which is removed from its place by a wind is rubbish for indeed the contrary winds are many

persian_text: ز آنکه در الاست او از لا گذشت هر که در الاست او فانی نگشت
english_translation: Because he is in except he has transcended not  whosoever is in except has not passed away

persian_text: رجوع کردن به حکایت آن شخص و آمدن او به امید عنایت آن محتسب
english_translation: Return to the Story of the man and his coming in hope of the favour of the Inspector of Police

persian_text: آن کسی که بانگ موران بشنود هم فغان سر دوران بشنود
english_translation: He that hears the voice of ants will also hear the cry from the inmost soul of them that are afar

persian_text: حالت صورتپرستان این بود سنگشان از صورتی مومین بود
english_translation: This is what happens to formworshippers their stone is waxen by a form

persian_text: پس چرا لابه کند او یا دعا که بگردان ای خداوند این قضا
english_translation: Then why should he make entreaty and cry in prayer O God avert this destiny

persian_text: که ازین شاگردی و زین افتقار سوزنی باشی شوی تو ذوالفقار
english_translation: For through this pupilage and this poverty  thou be a needle thou wilt become Dhu lfaqár

persian_text: جز مگر مفتاح خاص آید ز دوست که مقالید السموات آن اوست
english_translation: unless perchance the special key come from the Friend to whom belong the keys of the heavens

persian_text: نطق موسی بد بر اندازه و لیک هم فزون آمد ز گفت یار نیک
english_translation: The speech of Moses was in measure but even so it exceeded the words of his good friend

persian_text: گر نبینی رفتن آب حیات بنگر اندر جوی و این سیر نبات
english_translation: If you see not the flow of the Water of Life look at the stream and at this movement of the weeds

persian_text: باز آمد سوی آن طفل رشید مصطفی را بر مکان خود ندید
english_translation: She came back towards that righteous child she did not see Mustafá in his place

persian_text: بس تانی دارد و صبر و شکیب چشمسیر و مثرست و پاکجیب
english_translation: He hath much deliberation and patience and longsuffering he is contented and unselfish and pure of heart

persian_text: رسته و بر بسته پیش او یکیستگر یقین دعوی کند او در شکیست
english_translation: What has grown and what has been tied on is one to him though he may claim intuitive certainty he is in a doubt

persian_text: او بر آرد از کدورتها صفا مر جفاهای ترا گیرد وفا
english_translation: He brings forth purity from defilements He takes your acts of wrong as faithful performance

persian_text: صورت نفس ار بجویی ای پسر قصهی دوزخ بخوان با هفت در
english_translation: O son if you seek the form of the self read the story of Hell with its seven gates

persian_text: ز آنکه اول سمع باید نطق را سوی منطق از ره سمع اندر آ
english_translation: Since in order to speak one must first hear do thou come to speech by the way of hearing

persian_text: کافرک را هیکلی بد یادگار یاوه دید آن را و گشت او بیقرار
english_translation: The wretched infidel had an amulet as a keepsake He observed that it was lost and became distracted

persian_text: از ذکر باقی نطفه میچکید ران و زانو گشت آلوده و پلید
english_translation: Seminis quod reliquum erat e pene stillabat femur genuque inquinata et spurca evaserant Remnants of sperm were dripping from penis his thighs and knees had become soiled and filthy

persian_text: مدت شش سال در هجران شاه شد وزیر اتباع عیسی را پناه
english_translation: During six years in separation from the king the vizier became a refuge for the followers of Jesus

persian_text: دید ناگه باز را در دود و گرد شه بر او بگریست زار و نوحه کرد
english_translation: Suddenly he espied the falcon amidst smoke and dust the King wept sorely over it and made lament

persian_text: گبر گوید هست عالم نیست رب یا ربی گوید که نبود مستحب
english_translation: The infidel says The world exists there is no Lord he says that O my Lord is not to be approved

persian_text: این همه عالم طلبکار خوشند وز خوش تزویر اندر آتشند
english_translation: All the world are seekers of happiness and on account of a false happiness they are in the fire

persian_text: بیطلب تو این طلبمان دادهای گنج احسان بر همه بگشادهای
english_translation: Without seeking Thou hast given us this search Thou hast opened to all the treasure of beneficence

persian_text: ز آب هر آلوده کاو پنهان شود الحیاء یمنع الإیمان بود
english_translation: Every defiled one who hides from the water is Shame hinders Faith

persian_text: چونک نگذارد سگ آن نعرهی سقم من مهم سیران خود را چون هلم
english_translation: Since the dog will not leave off his pestilent howling I am the moon how should I abandon my course

persian_text: گر بگوید چشم را کو را فشار درد چشم از تو بر آرد صد دمار
english_translation: If He say to the eye Squeeze him eyeache will wreak upon you a hundred vengeances

persian_text: باخبر شدن آن غریب از وفات آن محتسب و استغفار او از اعتماد بر مخلوق
english_translation: How the poor stranger was informed of the Inspectors death and begged God to pardon him for having relied upon a created being

persian_text: گفت شاهد زان به جای دیده است کو بدیدهی بیغرض سر دیده است
english_translation: The words of the Witness are equivalent to the eye because he has seen the secret with a disinterested eye

persian_text: در من آویزید تا نازان شویدگر چه جغدانید شهبازان شوید
english_translation: Cling to me that ye may become exultant  ye may become royal falcons although ye are owls

persian_text: از قرین بیقول و گفت و گوی او خو بدزدد دل نهان از خوی او
english_translation: Thy heart secretly steals its disposition from the disposition of thy companion without speech and talk on his part

persian_text: آمد اندر انجمن آن طفل خرد آب روی مرد لافی را ببرد
english_translation: The little child came into the company and took away the prestige of the boastful man

persian_text: گر ندیدندی هزاران بار بیش عاقلان کی جان کشیدندیش پیش
english_translation: Unless the intelligent had experienced more than a thousand times how should they have betaken themselves to Him

persian_text: فالق الإصباح اسرافیلوار جمله را در صورت آرد ز ان دیار
english_translation: Like Isráfíl  He who causes the dawn to break brings them all from those lands into form

persian_text: مومنان گویند که آثار بهشت نغز گردانید هر آواز زشت
english_translation: the true believers say that the influences of Paradise made every unpleasant sound to be beautiful

persian_text: ای بسا کس را که صورت راه زد قصد صورت کرد و بر الله زد
english_translation: Oh many a one whom the form waylaid he aimed at the form and struck at God

persian_text: سیزده پیغامبر آنجا آمدند گمرهان را جمله رهبر میشدند
english_translation: Thirteen prophets came thither all were ready to guide those who had lost the way

persian_text: چونک دست خود به دست او نهی پس ز دست آکلان بیرون جهی
english_translation: When thou layest thy hand in his then thou wilt escape from the hand of the devourers

persian_text: ز آنکه داند هر که چشمش را گشود کآن کشنده سخرهی تقدیر بود
english_translation: Because every one whose eyes He hath opened would know that the slayer was constrained by predestination

persian_text: پیش شیخی در بخارا اندری تا به خواری در بخارا ننگری
english_translation: In the presence of a Shaykh you are in Bukhárá see that you do not look on Bukhárá as lowly

persian_text: گر نبودی او مسیح بطن نون حبس و زندانش بدی تا یبعثون
english_translation: If he had not glorified  the fishs belly would have been his gaol and prison until they shall be raised

persian_text: لا أحب الآفلین گفت آن خلیل کی فنا خواهد از این رب جلیل
english_translation: That Friend said I love not them that set how should the glorious Lord mean transiency by this

persian_text: بارها گفتند زر را وا بریم سوی مخزن ما چه بیگار اندریم
english_translation: times they said Let us take the gold back to the treasury what a quest are we in

persian_text: تی شو و خوش باش بین اصبعین کز میلا این سرمستست این
english_translation: Become empty and stay happily between two fingers for where is intoxicated with the wine of nowhere

persian_text: سینه کوبان آنچنان بگریست خوش که اختران گریان شدند از گریهاش
english_translation: Beating her breast she wept so well that the stars were made to weep by her weeping

persian_text: چون یکی حس غیر محسوسات دید گشت غیبی بر همه حسها پدید
english_translation: When one sense has perceived things that are not objects of senseperception that which is of the invisible world becomes apparent to all the senses

persian_text: این سلاح عجب من شد ای فتی عجب آرد معجبان را صد بلا
english_translation: These are the weapons of my pride O noble sir pride brings a hundred afflictions on the proud

persian_text: تو ز تلخی چونکه دل پر خون شوی پس ز تلخیها همه بیرون روی
english_translation: When from bitterness  thy heart is filled with blood  then thou wilt escape from all bitternesses

persian_text: کز برای من بدش سجدهی ملک وز پی من رفت بر هفتم فلک
english_translation: Since the worship of the angels was to him for my sake and he ascended to the Seventh Heaven on my account

persian_text: گر بریزی بحر را در کوزهای چند گنجد قسمت یک روزهای
english_translation: If thou pour the sea into a pitcher how much will it hold One days store

persian_text: اول و آخر هبوط من ز زن چونک بودم روح و چون گشتم بدن
english_translation: my first and my last fall were caused by woman since I was spiritand how have I become body

persian_text: میر دیدی خویش راای کم ز مور زان ندیدی آن موکل را تو کور
english_translation: you O less than an ant deemed yourself a prince hence blind  you did not see that custodian

persian_text: قصهی سبحانی ما اعظم شانی گفتن ابویزید
english_translation: Story of Báyazíds may God sanctify his spirit saying Glory to me How grand is my estate

persian_text: پس چه باشد مکه و شام و عراق که نماید او نبرد و اشتیاق
english_translation: What then would Mecca and Syria and Iráq be  that he should show fight and longing

persian_text: گفت خرگر در غممگر در ارم قسمتم حق کرد من زان شاکرم
english_translation: The ass replied Whether I am in pain or in Iram God has made it my portion and I am grateful for it

persian_text: مصریان را جمع آرید این طرف تا در آید آنک میباید بکف
english_translation: Bring the Egyptians together on this side in order that he who is wanted may come to hand

persian_text: احتیاطش کرد از سهو و خباط چون قضا آید چه سود است احتیاط
english_translation: He took precaution for it against neglect and craziness when the destiny comes to pass of what avail is precaution

persian_text: پس چرا شش روز آن را درکشید کل یوم الف عامای مستفید
english_translation: Why then O seeker of instruction did He extend for it to six days every day a thousand years

persian_text: در معاصی قبضها دلگیر شد قبضها بعد از اجل زنجیر شد
english_translation: The contractions in sins affect the heart after death contractions become chains

persian_text: اسم هر چیزی تو از دانا شنو سر رمز علم الاسما شنو
english_translation: Do thou hear the name of every thing from the knower hear the inmost meaning of the mystery of He taught the Names

persian_text: ریختم سرمایه بر پاک و پلیدای شه سرمایهده هل من مزید
english_translation: I poured the capital over pure and impure  O King who givest the capital is there any more

persian_text: اندرین محضر خردها شد ز دست چون قلم اینجا رسیده شد شکست
english_translation: In this place of presence minds are lost beyond control when the pen reaches this point it breaks

persian_text: ور سخنکش یابم آن دم زن به مزد میگریزد نکتهها از دل چو دزد
english_translation: And if at that time I find the scoundrel who kills the discourse the deep sayings will flee like a thief from my heart

persian_text: پس قبایت تنگ آید باز پس این کند با خویشتن خود هیچکس
english_translation: then after that the coat will be tight for you does any one practice this on himself

persian_text: شید آوردی که تا جغدان ما مر ترا سازند شاه و پیشوا
english_translation: Thou hast employed hypocrisy in order that our owls may make thee king and leader

persian_text: هر منافق مصحفی زیر بغل سوی پیغمبر بیاورد از دغل
english_translation: Every Hypocrite by way of fraud brought a Qurán under his arm to the Prophet

persian_text: ور نه موسی کی روا دارد که من پیش تو یاد آورم از هیچ تن
english_translation: Otherwise how should Moses deem it right that I make mention of anybody before thee

persian_text: موذن آمد از یکی لفظی بجست کای موذن بانگ کردی وقت هست
english_translation: the muezzin came from one of them fell a remark O muezzin have you given the call to prayers Is it time

persian_text: چون سلاحت هست رو صیدی بگیر تا بدوشانیم از صید تو شیر
english_translation: Since thou hast the weapons go catch some game in order that we may get milk from thy prey

persian_text: آنک او بعد از رئیسی خوار شد وآن توانگر هم که بیدینار شد
english_translation: him who was made lowly after having been a chief and the rich man too who became impecunious

persian_text: بر امید وصل تو مردن خوشست تلخی هجر تو فوق آتشست
english_translation: Tis sweet to die in hope of union with thee the bitterness of banishment from thee is worse than fire

persian_text: سوی شامست این نشان و این خبر در ره قدسش ببینی در گذر
english_translation: This sign and this information lies in the direction of Syria you will see it as you pass on the way to Jerusalem

persian_text: گفت یک خاصیتم در بازو است که زنم من نقبها با زور دست
english_translation: Another said My specialty lies in my arm I make tunnels by strength of hand

persian_text: لیک بیرون از جهاد و فعل خویش از ورای خیر و شر و کفر و کیش
english_translation: But outside of my own exertion and action beyond good and evil and religion and infidelity

persian_text: هر زمان نخود بر آید وقت جوش بر سر دیگ و برآرد صد خروش
english_translation: At the time of its being boiled the chickpea comes up continually to the top of the pot and raises a hundred cries

persian_text: لاجرم آن فتنهها کرد ای عزیز بیست همچندان که ما گفتیم نیز
english_translation: Inevitably it wrought those mischiefs my dear friend too twenty times as many as we have told

persian_text: زلف جعد مشکبار عقلبر آخرا چون دم زشت خنگ خر
english_translation: The curly lock that sheds musk and takes away the reasonat last it is like the ugly white tail of a donkey

persian_text: فکر زنبورست و آن خواب تو آب چون شوی بیدار باز آید ذباب
english_translation: thoughts are hornets and thy sleep is the water  when thou awakest the flies come back

persian_text: هم ز نساخی بر آمد هم ز دین شد عدوی مصطفی و دین به کین
english_translation: He abandoned both his work as a scribe and the Religion  he became the malignant foe of Mustafá and the Religion

persian_text: نقص عقل است آنکه بد رنجوری است موجب لعنت سزای دوری است
english_translation: Tis deficiency of mind that is the bad disease it is the cause of curse and merits banishment

persian_text: آمد آن یک در تفحص در پیش دید کافر را به بالای ویش
english_translation: One of them went after him to investigate he found the infidel on the top of him

persian_text: کی رسدتان این مثلها ساختن سوی آن درگاه پاک انداختن
english_translation: How is it seemly for you to make these similitudes and cast them at that holy Court

persian_text: این چه دم اژدها افشردنست هیچ دانی چه خبر آوردنست
english_translation: What squeezing of the dragons tail is this Dost thou know at all what the announcement is

persian_text: دیده آ بر دیگران نوحهگری مدتی بنشین و بر خود میگری
english_translation: O eye thou makest lament for others sit down awhile and weep for thyself

persian_text: تا ببینم کاهلی هر یکی تا بدانم حال هر یک بیشکی
english_translation: That I may perceive the laziness of each and know beyond any doubt the case of every one

persian_text: قصهی ایاز و حجره داشتن او جهت چارق و پوستین
english_translation: The Story of Ayáz and his having a chamber for his rustic shoon and sheepskin jacket

persian_text: هیچ این سودا نمیرفت از سرش پندها میداد چندین کافرش
english_translation: Never would this passion go out of her head so many infidels were exhorting her

persian_text: گاو نفس خویش را زوتر بکش تا شود روح خفی زنده و بهش
english_translation: Kill most quickly the cow your fleshly soul so that the hidden spirit may become alive and conscious

persian_text: گوید ای یزدان مرا در تن مبر تا درین گلشن کنم من کر و فر
english_translation: And says O God do not bring me to my body in order that I may walk as a prince in this garden

persian_text: هر محدث را خسان باذل کنند حرفش ار عالی بود نازل کنند
english_translation: Worthless folk humiliate every storyteller if his words are lofty they make them low

persian_text: تو هم از دشمن چو کینی میکشی ای زبون شش غلط در هر ششی
english_translation: So when you O slave to the six  wreak vengeance on your enemy you are in error concerning all the six

persian_text: سایهی آن را نمیدیدند هیچ صد تفو بر دیدههای پیچ پیچ
english_translation: And not seeing the shade of those at all A hundred spittings on distorted eyes

persian_text: آنچ کاری بدروی آن آن تست ورنه این بیداد بر تو شد درست
english_translation: For you reap what you sow that is yours Otherwise this act of injustice is proved against you

persian_text: راز جز با رازدان انباز نیست راز اندر گوش منکر راز نیست
english_translation: The secret is partner with none but the knower of the secret in the sceptics ear the secret is no secret

persian_text: نیستی طاوس با صد نقشبند که به نقشت چشمها روشن کنند
english_translation: Nor a peacock painted with a hundred designs so that eyes should be illumined by the picture which you present

persian_text: آن قیاس حال گردون بر زمین راست ناید فرق دارد در کمین
english_translation: The analogy between the state of Heaven and the earth is inexact it has a concealed difference

persian_text: زانک محسوسست ما را اختیار خوب میآید برو تکلیف کار
english_translation: Inasmuch as our power of choice is perceived by the sense responsibility for actions may well be laid upon it

persian_text: چونک حاکم این کند اندر گزین چون کند حکم احکم این حاکمین
english_translation: Since a judge does this in the case of a virtuous man how will the most Just of these judges give judgement

persian_text: تا هماره دوست بینی در نظر در دلت ناید ز کین ناخوش صور
english_translation: That you may always see friendly in your sight and that ugly ideas arising from hatred may not come into your heart

persian_text: هین حکایت کن از آن احوال خوش خاک بر احوال و درس پنج و شش
english_translation: Hark tell the story of those goodly states dust upon the states and lessons of the five and the six

persian_text: چون بسی میکرد فن و آن میفتاد گفت صد لعنت بر آن ابلیس باد
english_translation: Since they always dropped off though she tried many an artifice she exclaimed A hundred curses on Iblís

persian_text: لیک آدم چارق و آن پوستین پیش میآورد که هستم ز طین
english_translation: But Adam brought forward the rustic shoon and sheepskin jacket saying I am of clay

persian_text: گردن این مثنوی را بستهای میکشی آن سوی که دانستهای
english_translation: Thou hast bound the neck of this Mathnawí thou art drawing it in the direction known to thee

persian_text: خون آنها خلق را باشد سبیل ز انکه وحشیاند از عقل جلیل
english_translation: Their blood is free to mankind since they are estranged from the august Reason

persian_text: گفت یارب بارها برگشتهام توبهها و عهدها بشکستهام
english_translation: He cried O Lord many a time have I turned away and broken my vows of penitence and my promises

persian_text: گفت من هم نیز خوابش دیدهام وز روان شیخ این بشنیدهام
english_translation: He said I have also seen him in a dream and have heard this from the spirit of the Shaykh

persian_text: بینوا گردی ز یاران وابری خوار گردی و پشیمانی خوری
english_translation: You will become destitute you will be cut off from friends you will be despised you will feel sorry

persian_text: مشت بر هم میزدند از ابلهی پر بدند از جهل و از دانش تهی
english_translation: In their folly they smote each other with their fists they were full of ignorance and empty of knowledge

persian_text: تا نمیری نیست جان کندن تمام بیکمال نردبان نایی به بام
english_translation: Your agony is not finished till you die you cannot reach the roof without completing the ladder

persian_text: هیچ ماند آب آن فرزند را هیچ ماند نیشکر مر قند را
english_translation: Does the semen at all resemble that child Does the sugarcane at all resemble the candy

persian_text: گفت اگر کشتم بتو چون آمدم چون به پای خود درین خون آمدم
english_translation: He replied If I have killed him how have I come to thee How have I come with my own feet into this blood

persian_text: دیده را بر جستن عمر گماشت رخت را و اسب را ضایع گذاشت
english_translation: He fixed his eye on seeking Umar he let his baggage and horse be lost

persian_text: امتحانشگر کنی در راه دین هم تو گردی ممتحنای بییقین
english_translation: If you make trial of him in the way of religion you will be tried  O man without faith

persian_text: بیان حسد وزیر
english_translation: Explanation of the envy of the vizier

persian_text: مرکب اعناق مردم را مپا تا نیاید نقرست اندر دو پا
english_translation: Do not be perpetually riding on the necks of people lest gout attack your feet

persian_text: عذر گفتن خرگوش
english_translation: The hares apology

persian_text: ای ز تو مر آسمانها را صفاای جفای تو نکوتر از وفا
english_translation: Oh tis from thee the heavens have purity oh thy unkindness is better than kindness

persian_text: نام احمد نام جمله انبیاست چون که صد آمد نود هم پیش ماست
english_translation: The name of Ahmad is the name of all the prophets when the hundred comes  ninety is with us as well

persian_text: سخت گیرد خامها مر شاخ را زانک در خامی نشاید کاخ را
english_translation: The unripe cling fast to the bough because during immaturity they are not meet for the palace

persian_text: گر دو سه ابله ترا منکر شدند تلخ کی گردی چو هستی کان قند
english_translation: If two or three fools have disbelieved in thee how shouldst thou be soured when thou art a mine of candy

persian_text: چون زند شهوت درین وادی دهل چیست عقل تو فجل ابن الفجل
english_translation: When lust beats the drum in this vale what is thy reason A radish and the son of a radish

persian_text: صورت بر هم زدن از جسم تنگ اختلاط جانها در صلح و جنگ
english_translation: The appearance of collision from the narrow body is the spirits being intermingled in peace or war

persian_text: ای ضیاء الحق حسامالدین بیاای صقال روح و سلطان الهدی
english_translation: Come O Radiance of God Husámuddín O polisher of the spirit and sovereign of the Way to salvation

persian_text: چون صفت با جان قرین کردست او پس مناسب دانش همچون چشم و رو
english_translation: Inasmuch as He hath joined the quality to the soul know that it is congruous like eyes and face

persian_text: لیک ازو مقصود این بالش نبود علم بود و دانش و ارشاد سود
english_translation: But this pillow was not its object it was learning and knowledge and right guidance and profit

persian_text: گفت آن آن فلان میر اجل گفت طالب را چنین باشد عمل
english_translation: He answered It belongs to soandso the most honourable Amír Said he Is the seekers work like this

persian_text: هرکه درخلوت ببینش یافت راه او ز دانشها نجوید دستگاه
english_translation: No one who in solitude has found the way to vision will seek power by means of the kinds of knowledge

persian_text: خلوت از اغیار باید نه ز یار پوستین بهر دی آمد نه بهار
english_translation: One must seclude ones self from strangers not from the friend the furcoat is for winter not for spring

persian_text: در نگنجد عشق در گفت و شنید عشق دریاییست قعرش ناپدید
english_translation: Love is not contained in speech and hearing Love is an ocean whereof the depth is invisible

persian_text: چغز جان در آب خواب بیهشی رسته از موش تن آید در خوشی
english_translation: When the froglike soul escapes from the mouselike body into the water the sleep of unconsciousness it enters into a happy state

persian_text: حکم چون در دست گمراهی فتاد جاه پندارید در چاهی فتاد
english_translation: When authority falls into the hands of one who has lost the way he deems it to be a high position  he has fallen into a pit

persian_text: خلق دیوانه شدند از شوق او از فراق حال و قال و ذوق او
english_translation: The people became mad from longing for him and on account of being separated from his feeling and discourse and intuition

persian_text: سجده میکرد او که هم فرمان تراست غیر حق بر ملک حق فرمان کراست
english_translation: He was prostrating himself in prayer saying It beseems Thee to command to whom but God belongeth the command over Gods kingdom

persian_text: چون بیابیاش نمانی منتظر هم هویدا او بود هم نیز سر
english_translation: When thou findest him thou wilt not remain in expectation  he is both the manifest and also the mystery

persian_text: بس دراز است این حدیث خواجه گو تا چه شد احوال آن مرد نکو
english_translation: This is very long Tell the story of the merchant that we may see what happened to that good man

persian_text: بشنو الفاظ حکیم پردهای سر همانجا نه که باده خوردهای
english_translation: Hearken to the words of the Sage who lived in seclusion Lay thy head in the same place where thou hast drunk the wine

persian_text: حکایت مریدی کی شیخ از حرص و ضمیر او واقف شد
english_translation: Story of the disciple of whose greediness and secret thoughts his Shaykh became aware

persian_text: فاش و مطلق گفتنم دستور نیست لیک راز از پیش چشمم دور نیست
english_translation: It is not permitted to me to speak openly and freely yet the mystery is not far from mine eye

persian_text: در سه تاریکی شناسی باد خر چون ندانی مر مراای خیرهسر
english_translation: Amidst three darknesses thou knowest the wind of the ass how dost not thou know me O giddyhead

persian_text: وای اگر بر عکس بودی این مطار پیش تو گلزار و پیش خویش راز
english_translation: Alas if the case had been reversed a rosegarden in your view and miserable in my own

persian_text: در دعا میخواستی جانم از او کش بیابم مار بستانم از او
english_translation: My soul was desiring of Him in prayer that I might find him and take the snake from him

persian_text: هر سه با هم اندر آن صحرای ژرف صیدها گیرند بسیار و شگرف
english_translation: And all three together might seize much and great quarry in that deep wilderness

persian_text: در فره دادن شنیده در کمون حکمت لولا رجال مومنون
english_translation: In giving the advantage they have hearkened in secret to the wisdom of  Had not there been truebelieving men

persian_text: آتش از استیزه افزون میشدی میرسید او را مدد از بیحدی
english_translation: out of spite the fire was increasing aid was coming to it from One who is infinite

persian_text: اتحاد یار با یاران خوش است پای معنی گیر صورت سرکش است
english_translation: Sweet is the oneness of the Friend with His friends catch the foot of spirit Form is headstrong

persian_text: صنع بیصورت بکارد صورتی تن بروید با حواس و آلتی
english_translation: The formless working sows a form  there grows up a body endowed with senses and a faculty

persian_text: میل و رغبت کان زمام آدمیست جنبش آن رام امر آن غنیست
english_translation: The inclination and desire which is Mans nosereinits movement is subject to the command of that Selfsufficient One

persian_text: از نظر چون بگذری و از خیال کشته باشی نیم شب شمع وصال
english_translation: When you relinquish sight and phantasy  you will have put out the candle of union at midnight

persian_text: بر زن و بر فعل زن دل مینهید عقل ناقص و آن گهانی اعتماد
english_translation: Will ye set your hearts on woman and the deeds of woman weak mind and then trust

persian_text: نیست مخفی در نماز آن مکرمت در گنه خلعت نهد آن مغفرت
english_translation: That bounty is not mysterious in piety the Forgiveness bestows a robe of honour in sin

persian_text: هست معذوریش معذوری من هست رنجوریش رنجوری من
english_translation: His excusability is My excusability his sickness is My sickness

persian_text: آدمی را اندکاندک آن همام تا چهل سالش کند مرد تمام
english_translation: Little by little till forty years that Potentate makes the human being a complete man

persian_text: گر نبودی کور از او بگداختی گرمی خورشید را بشناختی
english_translation: Were it not blind it would have been melted by Him it would have known the heat of sun

persian_text: گر چه شویم آگه است و پر فن است لیک گوهر را هزاران دشمن است
english_translation: Although my husband is shrewd and artful yet the pearl has thousands of enemies

persian_text: چون بشد درد و شدت آن حرص زفت همچو سگ سودای خانه از تو رفت
english_translation: when the pain is gone and your greed has grown stout the desire for the house departs from you just as the dog

persian_text: با همه تمکین خدا روزی او کرده باشد بسته اندر جست و جو
english_translation: Notwithstanding all majesty God must have made his livelihood to be bound up with seeking and endeavour

persian_text: همچنان دوغی ترش در معدنی خود نگردی زو مخلص روغنی
english_translation: Still thou art sour buttermilk in the churn in sooth thou hast not extracted any oil from it

persian_text: چون دو کس بر هم زند بیهیچ شک در میانشان هست قدر مشترک
english_translation: When two persons come into touch with each other without any doubt there is something in common between them

persian_text: آن یکی موری گرفت از راه جو مور دیگر گندمی بگرفت و دو
english_translation: One ant picks up barley on the road another ant picks up a grain of wheat and runs away

persian_text: چونک قدرت نیست خفتند این رده همچو هیزمپارهها و تنزده
english_translation: When there is no power  this troop are asleep and silent like faggots

persian_text: قعر چه بگزید هر کی عاقل است ز آنکه در خلوت صفاهای دل است
english_translation: Every one who is wise chose the bottom of the well  because spiritual joys are in solitude

persian_text: مروحهی تقدیر ربانی چرا پر نباشد ز امتحان و ابتلا
english_translation: Why should not the fan of Divine foreordainment be fraught with trial and probation

persian_text: از هوا شد سوی بالا او بسی لیک بر گردون نپرد کرکسی
english_translation: by selfwill he often went upward but no vulture can fly to heaven

persian_text: شد ز عیسی زنده لیکن باز مرد شاد آنکو جان بدین عیسی سپرد
english_translation: He was made living by Jesus but died again Happy is he that gave up his life to this Jesus

persian_text: آنچ لحیانی به خانهی خود ندید هست بر کوسه یکایک آن پدید
english_translation: That which the bushybearded man never saw in his own house is apparent at once to him who has but a few hairs on his chin

persian_text: چون شود بیدار از خواب او سحر باز آید سوی او آن خیر و شر
english_translation: At dawn when he wakes from slumber that good and evil will come back to him

persian_text: آنک تنها در رهی او خوش رود با رفیقان سیر او صدتو شود
english_translation: He who cheerfully goes alone on a journey with companions his progress is increased a hundredfold

persian_text: دعوت مکارشان اندر کشید الحذر از مکر شیطانای رشید
english_translation: The invitation of the deceiver inveigled them O righteous man beware of the deceit of the Devil

persian_text: در چه اندازم کنون تیغ و مجن کین سلاح خصم من خواهد شدن
english_translation: I now throw my sword and shield into the well for they will become the weapons of my adversary

persian_text: نبود آن دشنام او بیفایده نبود آن مهمانیش بیمایده
english_translation: Its revilement is not without use its hospitality is not without a table

persian_text: مرد افسونگر ز حرص کسب و کار در نیابد آن زمان افسون مار
english_translation: The charmer from greed of getting and making  is not conscious of the snakes spell at the time

persian_text: زانک بیلذت نروید لحم و پوست چون نروید چه گدازد عشق دوست
english_translation: For without pleasure flesh and skin do not grow and unless they grow what shall the love of the Friend consume

persian_text: شهر را بفریبد الا شاه را ره نتاند زد شه آگاه را
english_translation: He deceives the city except the king he cannot waylay the sagacious king

persian_text: آن امینان جمله در عذر آمدند همچو سایه پیش مه ساجد شدند
english_translation: All those trusted began to excuse themselves they fell prostrate like a shadow in the presence of the moon

persian_text: یا به علم نقل کم بودی ملی علم وحی دل ربودی از ولی
english_translation: Or that he had not been filled with traditional knowledge had carried away from a saint the knowledge divinely revealed to the heart

persian_text: آن به نسبت با کمال تو رواست ملک اکمال فناها مر تراست
english_translation: That is proper in reference to Thy perfection Thine is the power of perfecting mortalities

persian_text: پرس پرسان میشد اندر افتقاد چیست این غم بر که این ماتم فتاد
english_translation: He went along asking many questions in his searchWhat is this sorrow Whose death has occasioned this mourning

persian_text: آمنست از فوت و از یاغی که او میشناسد قهر شه را بر عدو
english_translation: He hath no fear of missing his chance or of the enemy for he recognises the Kings dominion over the enemy

persian_text: وانگهی بنمودشان یک روز هم که به تن باز آمد ارواح از عدم
english_translation: And then did it seem to them one day even when their spirits came back from nonexistence into their bodies

persian_text: عاشقان اندر عدم خیمه زدند چون عدم یکرنگ و نفس واحدند
english_translation: Lovers have pitched their tents in nonexistence they are of one colour and one essence like nonexistence

persian_text: در صفت پیر و مطاوعت وی
english_translation: Concerning the qualities of the Pír and obedience to him

persian_text: بس صفای بیحدودش داد او وانگه از ظلمت ضدش بنهاد او
english_translation: So He endowed him with infinite purity  and then set up against him a contrary of darkness

persian_text: هر دمش صد نامه صد پیک از خدا یا ربی زو شصت لبیک از خدا
english_translation: At every moment to him from God a hundred missives a hundred couriers from him one O my Lord and from God a hundred Labbayka

persian_text: نیست حاکم تا کند تیمار او کار خواجهی خود کند یا کار او
english_translation: He possesses no authority that he should care for him shall he do his own masters business or his

persian_text: خواجهاش میداشتی در کار پیش بهترش دیدی ز فرزندان خویش
english_translation: His master used to prefer him in the work and deem him better than his own sons

persian_text: یا منم دیوانه و خیره شده دیو چیزی مرا مرا بر سر زده
english_translation: Or have I become mad and crazy Has the Devil cast something upon my head

persian_text: تا ملک بیخود شد از تدریس او قدس دیگر یافت از تقدیس او
english_translation: So that the angels became beside themselves at his teaching and gained from his glorification a holiness other

persian_text: هین نگهدارای دل اندیشه جو دل ز اندیشهی بدی در پیش او
english_translation: Beware Guard thyself O heart disposed to thinking from any evil thought in his presence

persian_text: هیچکس را خود ز آدم تا کنون کی بدست آواز صد چون ارغنون
english_translation: When has any one even from Adam till now had a hundred voices like an organ

persian_text: بس که خود را کردهای بندهی هوا کرمکی را کردهای تو اژدها
english_translation: Inasmuch as thou hast made thyself the slave of sensuality thou hast made a petty worm into a dragon

persian_text: چون رسید اینجا بیانم سر نهاد محو شد والله اعلم بالرشاد
english_translation: Now that my exposition has reached this point it lays down its head and expires and God best knoweth the right course

persian_text: دیگری آمد که بگذشت آن صفر گفت عکاشه ببرد از مژده بر
english_translation: Some one else came saying Safar is past He said Ukkásha has borne away the fruit for the good news

persian_text: راست میکرد او به دست آن تاج را باز کژ میشد برو تاجای فتی
english_translation: He was putting the tiara straight with his hand the tiara always became awry for him again O youth

persian_text: گفت بد موقوف این لت لوت من آب حیوان بود در حانوت من
english_translation: He said My food depended on these blows the Water of life was in my shop

persian_text: کی توان نوشید این میزیردست مییقین مر مرد را رسواگرست
english_translation: How is it possible to quaff this wine underhand  Certainly wine is the exposer of man

persian_text: نیست قدرت هر کسی را سازوار عجز بهتر مایهی پرهیزکار
english_translation: Power is not suitable to every one weakness is the best stockintrade for the devout

persian_text: آمدی اندر جهانای ممتحن هیچ میبینی طریق آمدن
english_translation: Thou camest into the world O afflicted one dost thou ever see the way of thy coming

persian_text: و آن جماعت در پی او در قیام اینت زیبا قوم و بگزیده امام
english_translation: While that company stood up behind him Look you a goodly company and an elect Imám

persian_text: چند در آتش نشستی همچو عود چند پیش تیغ رفتی همچو خود
english_translation: you have sathow oftenin the fire like aloeswood that you have gonehow oftento meet the sword like a helmet

persian_text: آن یکی را بیگهان آمد قنق ساخت او را همچو طوق اندر عنق
english_translation: A guest came to a certain man at a late hour he made him like a collar on the neck

persian_text: خام را جز آتش هجر و فراق کی پزد کی وا رهاند از نفاق
english_translation: Save the fire of absence and separation who will cook the raw one Who will deliver him from hypocrisy

persian_text: ور وی آنست این بدنای دوست چیستای عجب زین دو کدامین است و کیست
english_translation: And if he is that  what is this body my friend Oh I wonder which of these twain he is and who

persian_text: آدمی بر قد یک طشت خمیر بر فزود از آسمان و از اثیر
english_translation: Man no bigger than a kneadingtrough  has surpassed the heavens and the aether

persian_text: گر ز زندانم برانی تو به رد خود بمیرم من ز تقصیری و کد
english_translation: If thou wilt drive me from the prison and turn me out verily I shall die of destitution and beggary

persian_text: که چرا کشتی کنی و پروری چون کمالی یافت آن را میبری
english_translation: Crying Why dost thou sow and tend some seedcorn and art cutting it when it has attained to perfection

persian_text: پیش مومن کی بود این غصه خوار قدر عشق گوش عشق گوشوار
english_translation: How should this anguish be lightly esteemed by the true believer Love for the earring is in proportion to love for the ear

persian_text: شاه آن دان کاو ز شاهی فارغ است بیمه و خورشید نورش بازغ است
english_translation: Regard as a king him that is unconcerned with kingship him whose light shines forth without moon or sun

persian_text: او عوان را در دعا در میکشید کز عوان او را چنان راحت رسید
english_translation: He was bringing the policeman into the prayer  because such solace had come to him from the policeman

persian_text: آن کرم که اندر جفا آنهات داد در وفا بنگر چه باشد افتقاد
english_translation: That bounty which gave thee those things in thy unrighteousnessconsider what will be care in thy faithfulness

persian_text: چشمبسته عالمی میدیدمی ورد و ریحان بیکفی میچیدمی
english_translation: With eye shut I would be seeing a world without a hand I would be gathering roses and basil

persian_text: بحر علمی در نمیپنهانشده در سه گز تن عالمی پنهانشده
english_translation: Thou art the sea of knowledge hidden in a dewdrop thou art the universe hidden in a body three ells long

persian_text: گر بگویم آن سبب گردد دراز که چرا بودش به تخت آن عشق و ساز
english_translation: If I explain the reason why she had that love and complaisance to her throne it will become long

persian_text: هین که اسرافیل وقتاند اولیا مرده را ز یشان حیات است و حیا
english_translation: Hark for the saints are the Isráfíls of the time from them to the dead comes life and freshness

persian_text: ور برد کفشت مرو در سنگلاخ ور دو شاخ استت مشو تو چار شاخ
english_translation: And if He take away your shoes do not go into stony ground and if you have two spikes dont become fourspiked

persian_text: چون عمل کردی شجر بنشاندی اندر آخر حرف اول خواندی
english_translation: When you have done work planted the treeat the end you read the first words

persian_text: سجده نتوان کرد بر آب حیات تا نیابم زین تن خاکی نجات
english_translation: Tis impossible to perform the prostration on the Water of Life until I gain deliverance from this earthly body

persian_text: دست بر سبلت نهادی در نوید رمز یعنی سوی سبلت بنگرید
english_translation: He would gaily put his hand on his moustache as a sign meaning Look at my moustache

persian_text: بادهشان کم بود و گفتا ای غلام رو سبو پر کن به ما آور مدام
english_translation: they had no wine so he said to his slave Go fill the jug and fetch us wine

persian_text: امر و نهی و خشم و تشریف و عتاب نیست جز مختار راای پاکجیب
english_translation: Command and prohibition and anger and conferment of honour and rebuke concern him who possesses the power of choice O purebosomed one

persian_text: در سخای آن شه و سلطان جود مر ترا کفشی و شلواری نبود
english_translation: In the generosity of that king and sultan of munificence was there not a pair of shoes and trousers for thee

persian_text: این حدیث آخر ندارد باز ران جانب هاروت و ماروتای جوان
english_translation: This topic is endless Speed back O youth to Hárút and Márút

persian_text: دست گوید من چنین دزدیدهام لب بگوید من چنین پرسیدهام
english_translation: His hand will say I have stolen such and such his lip will say I have asked such and such questions

persian_text: این حروف حالهات از نسخ اوست عزم و فسخت هم ز عزم و فسخ اوست
english_translation: These letters thy states are of His inditing thy forming a purpose and changing it is just from His forming a purpose and changing it

persian_text: از چه نام برگ را کردی تو مرگ جادوی بین که نمودت مرگ برگ
english_translation: Wherefore have you given the name death to provision  Observe the sorcery that has caused the provision to seem to you death

persian_text: گفت اوگر ابلهم من در ادب زیرکم اندر وفا و در طلب
english_translation: He said If I am foolish in manners I am wise in respect of faithfulness and pursuit

persian_text: شکر طوفان را کنون بگماشتی واسطهی اطلال را بر داشتی
english_translation: Thanks  Now Thou hast let loose the Flood and removed the ruins which stood between

persian_text: جفت شد با او به شهوت آن زمان متحد گشتند حالی آن دو جان
english_translation: Protinus cum eo magna cupidine coivit illae duae animae statim unitae evaserunt She joined with him in that moment with lust those two souls immediately became united

persian_text: آب روح شاه اگر شیرین بود جمله جوها پر ز آب خوش شود
english_translation: If the water of the Kings spirit be sweet all the riverbeds are filled with the sweet water

persian_text: ترک دنیا هر که کرد از زهد خویش بیش آید پیش او دنیا و پیش
english_translation: If any one abandons the world by his own renunciation the world comes to him more and more

persian_text: یکقدم چون رخ ز بالا تا نشیب یکقدم چون پیل رفته بر وریب
english_translation: one step like the rook from top to bottom  one step he goes crossways like the bishop

persian_text: چون پی یسکن الیهاش آفرید کی تواند آدم از حوا برید
english_translation: Inasmuch as He created her that he might take comfort in her how can Adam be parted from Eve

persian_text: فرض میآری به جاگر طایفی بر سهیلی چون ادیم طایفی
english_translation: Thou wilt perform thy bounden duty if thou go round about Suhayl as Táif hide

persian_text: نیست با ایشان سلاح و لشکری جز عصا و در عصا شور و شری
english_translation: There is not with them any weapons or soldiers nothing but a rod and in the rod is a calamity and bane

persian_text: آن زمان که خفته باشد آن حکیم آن عصا را قصد کن بگذار بیم
english_translation: And at the time when that Sage is asleep make for the rod abandon fear

persian_text: با قضا پنجه مزنای تند و تیز تا نگیرد هم قضا با تو ستیز
english_translation: Do not grapple with Destiny O fierce and furious one lest Destiny also pick a quarrel with thee

persian_text: گر بدیدی مطلعش را ز احتیال بند کردی راه هر ناخوش خیال
english_translation: If he saw its risingplace he would contrive to bar the way to every unlovely fancy

persian_text: گر ببندی در صطبلی گاو نر باز یابی در مقام گاو خر
english_translation: If you tether an ox in a stall and then find an ass in the place of the ox

persian_text: ظاهر شدن فضل و زیرکی لقمان پیش امتحان کنندگان
english_translation: How the excellence and sagacity of Luqmán became manifest to those who made trial

persian_text: نیست تخصیص خدا کس را به کار مانع طوع و مراد و اختیار
english_translation: Gods assignment of a particular lot to any one does not hinder consent and will and choice

persian_text: چون تو با من اینچنین بودی به ظن هم ز جان بیزار گشتم هم ز تن
english_translation: Inasmuch as thou hast such opinion of me I am grown weary both of soul and of body

persian_text: گفت شیطان که بما أغویتنی کرد فعل خود نهان دیو دنی
english_translation: Satan said Because Thou hast seduced me the vile Devil concealed his own act

persian_text: آن چه سرمهست آنک یزدان میکشد کز پس صد پرده بیند جان رشد
english_translation: What a collyrium is that which God applies  so that the spirit discerns the truth behind a hundred curtains

persian_text: اهل نار و اهل نور آمیخته در میانشان کوه قاف انگیخته
english_translation: He hath mixed the people of the Fire and the people of the Light between them He hath reared the mountain of Qáf

persian_text: کان تف خورشید شهوت بر زند آن خفاش مردریگت پر زند
english_translation: For the heat of the sun of lust strikes upon it that vile bat of thine flaps its wings

persian_text: نواختن مجنون آن سگ را کی مقیم کوی لیلی بود
english_translation: How Majnún petted the dog that lived in Laylas abode

persian_text: گفت اندر قصر کس را ره نبود کیست کاین گستاخی و جرات نمود
english_translation: He said  No one had entrance to the palace who is he that has shown such impudence and boldness

persian_text: چار عنصر چار استون قویست که بدیشان سقف دنیا مستویست
english_translation: The four elements are four strong pillars by which the roof of the present world is upright

persian_text: هر جمادی با نبی افسانهگو کعبه با حاجی گواه و نطقخو
english_translation: Every inanimate thing tells a tale to the Prophet the Kaba testifies to the pilgrim and is eloquent

persian_text: گوش کن هاروت را ماروت راای غلام و چاکران ما روت را
english_translation: Listen to Hárút and Márút O thou to whose face we are slaves and servants

persian_text: زیرکان با صنعتی قانع شده ابلهان از صنع در صانع شده
english_translation: The clever ones are content with an ingenious device the simple ones have gone from the artifice to rest in the Artificer

persian_text: آن دلی آور که قطب عالم اوست جان جان جان جان آدم اوست
english_translation: Bring the Heart that is the Qutb of the world and the soul of the soul of the soul of the soul of Adam

persian_text: آن دو دیدهروشنان بودند ازین وین دو را دیده ندیده غیر طین
english_translation: Those two were clairvoyant by this  while the eye of these two saw nothing but clay

persian_text: تا نگردد رازهای غیب فاش تا نگردد منهدم عیش و معاش
english_translation: Lest the mysteries of the Unseen should be divulged and the life and livelihood be destroyed

persian_text: هین بنه بر پایم آن زنجیر را که دریدم سلسلهی تدبیر را
english_translation: Hark put that fetter on my leg for I have torn the chain of consideration to pieces

persian_text: کشته بر جست و بگفت اسرار را وا نمود آن زمرهی خونخوار را
english_translation: The murdered man sprang up and told the secrets he revealed that bloodthirsty gang

persian_text: تا خر از هر که بود من واخرم ور نه توزیعی کنند ایشان زرم
english_translation: So that I might buy back the ass from whoever he is or else they might divide my money

persian_text: قوم گفتند ای امیر افزون مگو چیست حجت بر فزونجویی تو
english_translation: The party said O Amír do not say too much  what is thy argument for seeking more

persian_text: هیچ مگشا لب نشین و مینگر تا کند صدر جهان اینجا گذر
english_translation: Do not open thy lips at all sit down and look on till the Sadri Jahán passes here

persian_text: چون بزادم رستم از زندان تنگ در جهان خوش هوای خوب رنگ
english_translation: when I was born I escaped from the narrow prison into a world of pleasant air and beautiful colour

persian_text: هر یکی را او بگرزی میفکند سر نگوسار اندر اقدام سمند
english_translation: He felled each of them with a blow of his mace headlong at the feet of his steed

persian_text: شاخ تیزت بس جگرها را که خست نک عصاام شاخ شوخت را شکست
english_translation: Many are the hearts which thy sharp horn has wounded lo my rod has broken thy froward horn

persian_text: شد مناسب وصفها در خوب و زشت شد مناسب حرفها که حق نبشت
english_translation: The qualities are congruous in the good and evil  congruous are the letters that God hath written

persian_text: گر ترا صدقیست اندر دین خود ظلم بر صادق دلت چون میدهد
english_translation: If thou art steadfast in thy own religion how is thy heart consenting to maltreat him who is steadfast

persian_text: رنجدیده گنج نادیده ز یار کارها کرده ندیده مزد کار
english_translation: He has suffered the pain but never seen the gain from the Beloved he has done works but never seen the wages for his work

persian_text: راست گفتست آن شه شیرینزبان چشم گرد مو به موی عارفان
english_translation: That sweettongued king has said with truth that every hair of gnostics becomes an eye

persian_text: همچنان ای خواجهی تشنیع زن از بلا و فقر و از رنج و محن
english_translation: Even so O Khwája who art reviling on account of affliction and poverty and distress and tribulations

persian_text: از بلیس او پیرتر خود کی بود چونک عقلش نیست او لاشی بود
english_translation: How should he be older than Iblís When he lacks understanding he is goodfornaught

persian_text: ای بسا در گور خفته خاکوار به ز صد احیا به نفع و انتشار
english_translation: Oh many a one sleeping like dust in the grave is superior in usefulness and openhandedness to a hundred living

persian_text: هست احول را درین ویرانه دیر گوشه گوشه نقل نوای ثم خیر
english_translation: In this ruined monastery the man who sees double is removing from one nook to another O  The good is there

persian_text: چون هریسه گشته آنجا فرق نیست نیست فرقی کاندر آنجا غرق نیست
english_translation: They become like a harísa no difference exists there there is no difference that is not submerged there

persian_text: سنگ را صد سال گویی لعل شو کهنه را صد سال گویی باش نو
english_translation: A hundred years you may tell the pebble to become a ruby a hundred years you may tell the old to become new

persian_text: گفت شه هر منصبی و ملکتی که التماسش هست یابد این فتی
english_translation: The King replied This youth will obtain every high dignity and sovereignty that he seeks

persian_text: چون خدا خواهد کهمان یاری کند میل ما را جانب زاری کند
english_translation: When God wishes to help us He turns our inclination towards humble lament

persian_text: این همه گفت و به گوشش در نرفت بد گمانی مرد را سدی است زفت
english_translation: He said all this and it entered not his ear suspicion is a thick barrier to a man

persian_text: من ز مکر نفس دیدم چیزها کاو برد از سحر خود تمییزها
english_translation: I have seen things from the deceitfulness of the fleshly soul for by her magic she takes away the faculties of discernment

persian_text: بر لب جو من ترا نعرهزنان نشنوی در آب نالهی عاشقان
english_translation: I am on the riverbank crying aloud for you you in the water do not hear the wailing of lovers

persian_text: کین حیات او را چو مرگی مینمود کار پیشش بازگونه گشته بود
english_translation: For this life seemed to him like a death in his view the thing had become reversed

persian_text: در میان امت مرحوم باش سنت احمد مهل محکوم باشد
english_translation: Live amongst the community that is the object of mercy do not forsake the religion of Ahmad  be ruled

persian_text: شیخ کی بود پیر یعنی مو سپید معنی این مو بدانای کژ امید
english_translation: Who is a Shaykh An old man  that is  whitehaired Do thou apprehend the meaning of this  hair O you whose hopes are false

persian_text: چون خبیثان را چنین خلعت دهد طیبین را تا چه بخشد در رصد
english_translation: Since He confers such a robe of honour on the wicked what He bestows on the righteous in the place where He waits

persian_text: آنچنان که لمعهی درپاش اوست شمس دنیا در صفت خفاش اوست
english_translation: Since its pearlscattering radiance is such  the sun of the present world may be described as its bat

persian_text: تولیدن شیر از دیر آمدن خرگوش
english_translation: How the lion roared wrathfully because the hare was late in coming

persian_text: هی بیا در کشتی بابا نشین تا نگردی غرق طوفانای مهین
english_translation: Hey come and sit in thy fathers ark that thou mayst not be drowned in the Flood O despicable one

persian_text: همچو مه همچون عطارد تیزرو گوی صرصر علف بودش نه جو
english_translation: It moved as swiftly as the moon and Mercury you might say that its fodder was the sarsar wind not barley

persian_text: ما چو شطرنجیم اندر برد و مات برد و مات ما ز تستای خوش صفات
english_translation: We are as pieces of chess in victory and defeat our victory and defeat is from thee O thou whose qualities are comely

persian_text: روح را در غیب خود اشکنجههاست لیک تا نجهی شکنجه در خفاست
english_translation: Verily tortures are inflicted on the soul in the world invisible but until you escape the torture is concealed

persian_text: یک حکایت بشنو اینجاای پسر تا نگردی ممتحن اندر هنر
english_translation: Here listen to a story O son in order that you may not suffer affliction in talent

persian_text: زیافت تاویل رکیک مگس
english_translation: The baseness of the foul interpretation given by the fly

persian_text: در مروت ابر موسایی به تیه کآمد از وی خوان و نان بیشبیه
english_translation: In generosity you are Moses cloud in the desert whence came the dishes of food and bread incomparable

persian_text: ای که در روتان نشان مهتری فرتان خوشتر ز زر جعفری
english_translation: O ye in whose faces are the marks of eminence O ye whose splendour is more pleasing than the gold of Jafar

persian_text: آب شیرین تا نخوردی آب شور خوش بود خوش چون درون دیده نور
english_translation: Until you have drunk sweet water briny water is sweet sweet as the light in the eye

persian_text: یک غریبی شاعری از راه رسید روز عاشورا و آن افغان شنید
english_translation: A stranger a poet arrived from the road on the Day of Áshúrá and heard that lamentation

persian_text: چشمبسته میشود وقت قضا تا نبیند چشم کحل چشم را
english_translation: The eye is bandaged at the time if the Decree so that the eye does not see the eyes collyrium

persian_text: تو ز قرآنای پسر ظاهر مبین دیو آدم را نبیند جز که طین
english_translation: In the Qurán do not thou O son regard the exterior the Devil regards Adam as naught but clay

persian_text: اصبع لطفست و قهر و در میان کلک دل با قبض و بسطی زین بنان
english_translation: are the fingers of Grace and Wrath and between them the pen the heart is in a state of distress or ease by these fingers

persian_text: رفت پیش از نامه پیش مطبخی کای بخیل از مطبخ شاه سخی
english_translation: Before the letter he went to the kitchenstewerd and said O niggard of the kitchen of the generous king

persian_text: ظاهر نقرهگر اسپید است و نو دست و جامه میسیه گردد ازو
english_translation: If the surface of silver is white and new the hands and dress are blackened by it

persian_text: تا که این هر دو صفت ظاهر شود آن مبشر گردد این منذر شود
english_translation: To the end that both these attributes may be displayed and the former be hopeinspiring and the latter deterrent

persian_text: عفو کن زین بندگان تنپرست عفو از دریای عفو اولیترست
english_translation: Pardon these bodyworshipping slaves pardon from the ocean of pardon is more worthy

persian_text: چشم حس را هست مذهب اعتزال دیدهی عقل است سنی در وصال
english_translation: The doctrine held by the eye of sense is Mutazilism whereas the eye of Reason is Sunnite in respect of union

persian_text: ای برادر من بر آذر چابکم من نه آن جانم که گردم بیش و کم
english_translation: O brother I am eager for the fire I am not that spirit that I should become more and less

persian_text: هم سریر و قصر و هم تاج و ثیاب با بهشتی در سال و در جواب
english_translation: Both throne and palace and crown and robes are in question and reply with the inhabitant of Paradise

persian_text: بس مثال و شرح خواهد این کلام لیک ترسم تا نلغزد وهم عام
english_translation: This argument wants much illustration and exposition but I fear lest the opinion of the vulgar should stumble

persian_text: گفت گستاخی مکن بار دگر تا نسوزد جسم و جانت زین شرر
english_translation: He said Another time do not behave boldly lest thy body and soul be consumed by these sparks

persian_text: وصیت کردن مصطفی علیهالسلام صدیق را کی چون بلال را مشتری میشوی هر آینه ایشان از ستیز بر خواهند فزود بهای او را
english_translation: How Mustafá  on whom be peace enjoined the Siddíq saying Since thou art going to purchase Bilál they will certainly raise his price by wrangling  make me thy partner in this merit

persian_text: ای خداوند این خم و کوزهی مرا در پذیر از فضلالله اشتری
english_translation: O Lord accept this jar and jug of mine by the grace of God hath purchased

persian_text: گفت شه حکمت در اظهار جهان آنکه دانسته برون آید عیان
english_translation: The King said The wisdom in making the world manifest that the known should come forth plainly

persian_text: در یکی گفته که این جمله یکی است هر که او دو بیند احول مردکی است
english_translation: In one he said All this is one whoever sees two is a squinteyed manikin

persian_text: تو به جد کاری که بگرفتی به دست عیبش این دم بر تو پوشیده شدست
english_translation: Whenever thou hast earnestly taken a work in hand its faultiness has become veiled to thee at this moment

persian_text: علم را دو پر گمان را یک پرست ناقص آمد ظن به پرواز ابترست
english_translation: Knowledge has two wings Opinion one wing Opinion is defective and curtailed in flight

persian_text: میکند یک باد را زهر سموم مر صبا را میکند خرمقدوم
english_translation: One wind He makes the poison of the simoom He makes the advent of the eastwind to be delightful

persian_text: تا بداند در چه بود آن مبتلا از کجاها در رسید او تا کجا
english_translation: So that the afflicted one may know in what she was and from what places to what a place she has arrived

persian_text: غیرت حق پردهای انگیختست سفلی و علوی به هم آمیختست
english_translation: the jealousy of God has produced a veil and has mingled the low and the lofty together

persian_text: آن یکی میگیر و آن میهل ز دام وین دگر را صید میکن چون لام
english_translation: Go on catching one and releasing another from the trap and pursuing another like mean folk

persian_text: نان برون راند آدمی را از بهشت نان مرا اندر بهشتی در سرشت
english_translation: Bread drove an Adam forth from Paradise bread caused me to mix with those who belong to Paradise

persian_text: گفت دانستم ترا از وی بدان از تو جان گنده ست و از یارت دهان
english_translation: He said I know you from him by that  in you the spirit is foul and in your companion the mouth

persian_text: ای که جان را بهر تن میسوختی سوختی جان را و تن افروختی
english_translation: O thou who wert burning the soul for the bodys sake thou hast burned the soul and illumined the body

persian_text: زانک گردشهای آن خاشاک و کف باشد از غلیان بحر با شرف
english_translation: For the whirling of the sticks and straws and foam are caused by the boiling of the noble Sea

persian_text: گر روی رو در پی عنقای دل سوی قاف و مسجد اقصای دل
english_translation: If thou go go in pursuit of the Anqá of the heart towards the Qáf and Farther Mosque of the heart

persian_text: شسته در باطن میان گلستان ظاهر آحادی میان دوستان
english_translation: He is seated inwardly amidst the rosegarden  outwardly he is acting as a hádí amongst his friends

persian_text: ربع قرآن هر که را محفوظ بود جل فینا از صحابه میشنود
english_translation: Every one that knew a quarter of the Qurán by heart was hearing from the Companions  Great is he amongst us

persian_text: صوفیی گشته به پیش این لام الخیاطه واللواطه والسلام
english_translation: With these base scoundrels Súfism has become patching and sodomy and that is all

persian_text: جمله اجزای جهان زان حکم پیش جفت جفت و عاشقان جفت خویش
english_translation: Because of that foreordainment all the particles of the world re paired as mates and are in love with their own mate

persian_text: چون که عقل تو عقیلهی مردم است آن نه عقل است آنکه مار و کژدم است
english_translation: Since thy understanding is a shackle for mankind it is not understanding it is a snake and scorpion

persian_text: رو به خاک آریم کز وی رستهایم دل چرا در بیوفایان بستهایم
english_translation: We shall turn our faces to the earth whence we have sprung why have we fixed our hearts on creatures devoid of constancy

persian_text: در دو عالم تو مرایی بودهای در دو عالم تو چنین بیهودهای
english_translation: In both worlds thou hast been a hypocrite in both worlds thou art such a worthless creature

persian_text: بس سیاحت کرد آن جا سالها میفرستادش شهنشه مالها
english_translation: There for years he travelled much the king kept sending money to him

persian_text: وا خریدش آن دم از گردن زدن خاک نعل پاش بایستی شدن
english_translation: He redeemed him from beheading at that moment he ought to have become the dust of his shoe

persian_text: ذکر دانش خرگوش و بیان فضیلت و منافع دانستن
english_translation: An account of the knowledge of the hare and an explanation of the excellence and advantages of knowledge

persian_text: راند او کشتی ازین ساحل پریر گشته بود آن خواجه زین غمخانه سیر
english_translation: He pushed off his boat from this beach the day before yesterday the Khwája had become sated with this house of sorrow

persian_text: پس فرو رفت او به خود اندر نغول شد ملول از صورت خوابش فضول
english_translation: Then he sank deep down into himself An impertinent fellow was annoyed by his semblance of slumber

persian_text: مال مار آمد که در وی زهرهاست و آن قبول و سجدهی خلق اژدهاست
english_translation: Riches are a snake for therein are poisons and popular favour and worship is a dragon

persian_text: شد شریف از زخم آن ظالم خراب با فقیه او گفت ما جستیم از آب
english_translation: The Sharíf was devastated by the blows of that ruffian He said to the jurist W have jumped out of the water

persian_text: و آن دو عالم را غرضشان کور کرد علمشان را علت اندر گور کرد
english_translation: While those two who know are blinded by their selfinterest prejudice has put their knowledge into the grave

persian_text: چون ترازو دید خصم پر طمع سرکشی بگذارد و گردد تبع
english_translation: When the covetous adversary sees the scales  he abandons rebelliousness and becomes submissive

persian_text: لشکری از خاک ز آن سوی اجل تا ببیند هر کسی حسن عمل
english_translation: One army from the Earth beyond death that every one may behold the beauty of works

persian_text: تا بود که سالکی بر تو زند از خیالات نعاست بر کند
english_translation: That perchance a Traveller may attach himself to thee and tear thee from the phantasies of slumber

persian_text: جان به جان پیوست و قالبها چخید چون دو مرغ سربریده میطپید
english_translation: Anima cum anima conjuncta est corpora mutuo amplexu implicata tanquam duae aves abscissis capitibus tremebant Soul was joined to soul and bodies strove  trembling like two decapitated birds

persian_text: قوم گفتندش که کسب از ضعف خلق لقمهی تزویر دان بر قدر حلق
