english_translation: The party answered him saying Regard acquisition  arising from the infirmity of creatures as a mouthful of deceit proportionate to the size of the gullet

persian_text: اسپرت باشم گه تیر خدنگ مخلص تو باشم اندر وقت تنگ
english_translation: I will be thy shield amidst the arrows of khadang wood I will be thy refuge in the hour of distress

persian_text: ظاهر الفاظشان توحید و شرع باطن آن همچو در نان تخم صرع
english_translation: The outward form of their words is profession of the Divine Unity and the religion  the inward meaning thereof is like darnel seed in bread

persian_text: گفت غیر این نسب نامیت هست مر ترا آن نام خود اولیترست
english_translation: He said Thou hast a name other than this lineage truly that name is more proper for thee

persian_text: امر حق آمد به شهرستان و ده خانه و دیوار را سایه مده
english_translation: In town and village to house and wall came the command of God Give no shade

persian_text: گر در آمیزند عود و شکرش بر گزیند یکیک از یکدیگرش
english_translation: If his aloeswood and sugar get mixed he picks them out from each other piece by piece

persian_text: این ز حد و اندازهها باشد برون داعی فعل از خیال گونهگون
english_translation: These are boundless and immeasurable  the motive to action from various sorts of phantasy

persian_text: ناامیدیها به پیش او نهید تا ز درد بیدوا بیرون جهید
english_translation: Lay your despairs before Him that ye may escape from irremediable pain

persian_text: هر کجا او میدود این میدود از طمع هم درد صاحب میشود
english_translation: Wherever he runs this one runs from cupidity he becomes a partner in the owners pain

persian_text: اندرین باران و گل او کی رود بر سر و جان تو او تاوان شود
english_translation: How can he set out in this rain and mud He will become a tax upon thy head and soul

persian_text: بشنوید ای دوستان این داستان خود حقیقت نقد حال ماست آن
english_translation: O my friends hearken to this tale in truth it is the very marrow of our inward state

persian_text: داستان آن عجوزه کی روی زشت خویشتن را گلگونه میساخت
english_translation: Story of the old woman who used to rouge her ugly face

persian_text: مینبینی غیر این لیکای تو ننگ آن زمان لاف بود این وقت جنگ
english_translation: Thou art seeing naught but this but O thou disgrace that was the time of talk and this is the time of battle

persian_text: گفت المعنی هو الله شیخ دین بحر معنیهای ربالعالمین
english_translation: The Reality is Allah said the Shaykh of the Religion the sea of the spiritual realities of the Lord of created beings

persian_text: اندرین گفتن بدند و موج تیز بر سر کنعان زد وشد ریز ریز
english_translation: They were in this talk when a fierce billow dashed upon Canaans head and he was shivered to fragments

persian_text: کافران چون جنس سجین آمدند سجن دنیا را خوش آیین آمدند
english_translation: Inasmuch as the infidels were congeners of Sijjn  they were welldisposed to the prison of this world

persian_text: نفس چون مبدل شود این تیغ تن باشد اندر دست صنع ذوالمنن
english_translation: When the soul is transformed this sword namely the body remains in the hand of the action of the Beneficent

persian_text: بس کسانی کز جهان بگذشتهاند لا نیند و در صفات آغشتهاند
english_translation: Those persons then who have passed from the world are not naught  but they are steeped in the Attributes

persian_text: ای فلک بر گفت او گوهر ببار از جهان او جهانا شرم دار
english_translation: O Heaven shower pearls on his rede O World have shame of his world

persian_text: ای بسا نفس شهید معتمد مرده در دنیا چو زنده میرود
english_translation: Oh there is many a trusty martyred soul that has died in this world it is going about like the living

persian_text: در حجره گشادن مصطفی علیهالسلام
english_translation: How Mustafá opened the door of the room

persian_text: آن زکاتی دان که غمگین را دهی گوش را چون پیش دستانش نهی
english_translation: Know that when you set your ear to his tale that is the alms which you give to the sad

persian_text: او وثاقم داد و تو چرخ و زمین در وثاقت او و صد چون او سمین
english_translation: He gave me a house but Thou the sky and the earth in Thy house he and a hundred like him fat

persian_text: سبب آنک فرجی را نام فرجی نهادند از اول
english_translation: The reason why the name farají was first given to the garment known by that name

persian_text: میرهاند میبرد تا معدنش اندکاندک تا نبینی بردنش
english_translation: It sets it free it wafts it away to its source little by little so that you do not see its wafting

persian_text: بانگ زد بر وی جوان و گفت بس روز روشن از کجا آمد عسس
english_translation: The youth cried out at her and said Enough In bright daylight where did the nightpatrol come from

persian_text: زانک ظلمانیست سنگ و روزکور هست ظلمانی حقیقت ضد نور
english_translation: For the stone is dark and blind to the day  the dark is essentially opposed to light

persian_text: چاره آن باشد که خود را بنگرم ور نه او خندد مرا من کی خرم
english_translation: The plan is this that I look at myself  otherwise  he will laugh at me how shall I buy

persian_text: جمله گفتندش که جانبازی کنیم فهم گرد آریم و انبازی کنیم
english_translation: They all answered him saying We will hazard our lives and summon all our intelligence and put it into the common stock

persian_text: تو رسنبازی نمیدانی یقین شکر پاها گوی و میرو بر زمین
english_translation: you have no sure skill in ropedancing give thanks for your feet and walk on the ground

persian_text: صبر کرد و بود چندی در حرج کشف شد کالصبر مفتاح الفرج
english_translation: He showed patience and was in a quandary for some time it was disclosed for patience is the key to joy

persian_text: بندگانمان خواجهتاش ما شوند بیدلانمان دلخراش ما شوند
english_translation: our slaves should become our fellowservants our timorous ones should become those who wound our hearts

persian_text: لیک این نامه خیالست و نهان وآن شود در حشر اکبر بس عیان
english_translation: But this scroll is a fancy and hidden  though at the greater resurrection it will be very clearly seen

persian_text: گرچه گردد در قیامت آن فزون از خدا اینجا بخواهید آزمون
english_translation: Although that will be increased at the Resurrection beg of God trial here

persian_text: پس تو و عقلت چو اصطرلاب بود زین بدانی قرب خورشید وجود
english_translation: Hence you and your intellect are like the astrolabe by this means you may know the nearness of the Sun of existence

persian_text: این سخن پایان ندارد مکرهاش جمله میپیچید هم در ساق و پاش
english_translation: This discourse hath no end All his plots entangled his legs and feet

persian_text: در عجب درماند کین لغزش ز چیست من نپندارم که این حالت تهیست
english_translation: He remained in astonishment saying Wherefore is this slipping I do not think that this occurrence is empty

persian_text: ترک مکر خویشتن گیرای امیر پا بکش پیش عنایت خوش بمیر
english_translation: Abandon your own cunning O Amír draw back your foot before the favour and gladly die

persian_text: رو به زن کرد و بگفتش ای عجوز خود نباشد فصل دی همچون تموز
english_translation: He turned towards his wife and said to her Old woman verily the season of December is not like Tamúz

persian_text: خود فزون آن به که آن از فطرتست تا ز افزونی که جهد و فکرتست
english_translation: Truly the superiority that is from nature is even better than the superiority that is endeavour and reflection

persian_text: قصد ما سترست و پاکی و صلاح در دو عالم خود بدان باشد فلاح
english_translation: Our quest is modesty and purity and virtue truly welfare in both worlds depends on that

persian_text: قبهای بر ساختستی از حباب آخر آن خیمهست بس واهیطناب
english_translation: Thou hast raised up a tent of bubbles in the end that tent has exceedingly weak ropes

persian_text: تعیین کردن زن طریق طلب روزی کدخدای خود را و قبول کردن او
english_translation: How the wife specified to her husband the way to earn daily bread and how he accepted

persian_text: اعجمی چون گشتهای اندر قضا میگریزانی ز داور مال را
english_translation: How have you become in destiny a churl withholding your money from the Judge

persian_text: ور همی طوفان کند باران برو شهرها را میکند ویران برو
english_translation: And if the rain is making a flood upon it and devastating the cities upon it

persian_text: کور چون شد گرم از نور قدم از فرح گوید که من بینا شدم
english_translation: When the blind man is heated by the Light of Eternity from gladness he says I have become seeing

persian_text: همچنانک از چشمهی چشم تو نور او روان کردست بیبخل و فتور
english_translation: Even as He hath caused the light to flow from the fountain of your eye without stint or abatement

persian_text: قایل این بانگ ناید در نظر لیک دل بشناخت قایل را ز اثر
english_translation: He who utters this cry is not visible but the heart recognized the Speaker from the effect

persian_text: بیازو ندهد کسی را حق نوال شمهای گفتم من از صاحبوصال
english_translation: Without him God does not bestow bounty on any one I have told one sample of the possessor of union

persian_text: گر نمیبینی کنار بام راز روح میبیند که هستش اهتزاز
english_translation: If you do not see the mysterious roofedge the spirit is seeing for it is shuddering

persian_text: از نبی بر خوان که دیو و قوم او میبرند از حال انسی خفیه بو
english_translation: Recite from the Qurán that the Devil and his tribe secretly get scent of the state of humankind

persian_text: خضر وقتی غوث هر کشتی توی همچو روحالله مکن تنها روی
english_translation: Thou art the Khizr of the time and the Deliverer of every ship  do not like the Spirit of God practise solitude

persian_text: گفت ناهنگام حی عل فلاح خون ما را میکند خوار و مباح
english_translation: To say at the wrong time Come to welfare will make our blood of no account and licit

persian_text: بود بیحد آن چمین نسبت بدو آن نظر که بیند آن را راست کو
english_translation: That urine was boundless in relation to him where was the vision that should see it truly

persian_text: آتش افتادن در شهر به ایام عمر
english_translation: How a conflagration occurred in the city in the days of Umar may God be wellpleased with him

persian_text: گفت اندر یک جوالم گندم است در دگر ریگی نه قوت مردم است
english_translation: He replied In one sack I have wheat in the other is some sandnot food for men

persian_text: بهر عین غم نه از بهر فرج این تسافل پیش ایشان چون درج
english_translation: for the sake of the sorrow itself not for the sake of relief  in their eyes this lowliness is as a ladder

persian_text: وین دو بایسته در این خاکی سرا روز و شب در جنگ و اندر ماجرا
english_translation: And this necessary pair in this house of earth are in strife and altercation day and night

persian_text: این قدر تعظیم دینشان را خرید کز مری آن دست و پاهاشان برید
english_translation: This amount of respect purchased their religion so that it cut off the hands and feet of their contention

persian_text: پیش چوب و پیش سنگ نقش کندای بسا گولان که سرها مینهند
english_translation: Oh many are the fools that lay down their heads before wood and graven stone

persian_text: چشم اگر داری تو کورانه میا ور نداری چشم دست آور عصا
english_translation: If you have eyes do not walk blindly and if you have not eyes take a staff in your hand

persian_text: غافلی هم حکمتست و این عمی تا بماند لیک تا این حد چرا
english_translation: True heedlessness and this blindness is Divine Wisdom in order that he may endure but why to such an extent as this

persian_text: مدح او مهراست نه آن عکس را کفر شد آن چون غلط شد ماجرا
english_translation: His praise belongs to the moon not to that reflexion that becomes infidelity when the matter is misapprehended

persian_text: جملگان از بامها زیر آمدند سر برهنه جانب صحرا شدند
english_translation: All came down from the roofs and went bareheaded towards the open country

persian_text: چالیش عقل با نفس هم چون تنازع مجنون با ناقه هوا ناقتی خلفی و قدامی الهوی و انی و ایاها لمختلفان
english_translation: The battle of the reason against the flesh is like the contention of Majnún with his she camel Majnúns inclination is towards the noble woman My shecamels love is behind me while my love is in front of me and verily I and she are discordant

persian_text: گر رود صد سال آنک آگاه نیست بر عما آن از حساب راه نیست
english_translation: If one who is ignorant should journey a hundred years in blindness that is not reckoned as a journey

persian_text: زاهدی بیند بگوید ای کیا همتی میدار از بهر خدا
english_translation: he sees an ascetic he will say O venerable sir bestow on me a benediction for Gods sake

persian_text: هر که از دیدار برخوردار شد این جهان در چشم او مردار شد
english_translation: when any one enjoys vision  this world becomes carrion in his eyes

persian_text: گر بطراری کند پس دو گواه جرح شد در محکمهی عدل اله
english_translation: If he act as a cutpurse  then the two witnesses are invalidated in the court of Divine justice

persian_text: آن یکی گوید شتر یکچشم بود و آن دگر گوید ز گر بیپشم بود
english_translation: One says The camel had one eye and another says It from mange had no hair

persian_text: انهم تحت قبابی کامنون جز که یزدانشان نداند ز آزمون
english_translation: Verily they are hidden beneath My tents None knows them by experience except God

persian_text: در نماز جمعه بنگر خوش به هوش جمله جمعند و یکاندیشه و خموش
english_translation: At the Friday prayerservice look well and attentively all are concentrated and possessed by a single thought and silent

persian_text: که همیدانم یجوز و لایجوز خود ندانی تو یجوزی یا عجوز
english_translation: Saying I know permissible and unpermissible Thou knowest not whether thou thyself art permissible or an old woman

persian_text: لیک ز آن درمان نبینی رنگ و بو بهر درد خویش بیفرمان او
english_translation: But of that remedy for your pain you will not see the colour or scent without His command

persian_text: آنکه عالم مست گفتش آمدی کلمینی یا حمیراء میزدی
english_translation: He  by whose words the world was intoxicated used to cry Speak to me O Humayrá

persian_text: لیک با خورشید و کعبه پیش رو این قیاس و این تحری را مجو
english_translation: But with the sun and with the Kaba before your face do not seek to reason and investigate in this manner

persian_text: تیغ حلم از تیغ آهن تیزتر بل ز صد لشکر ظفر انگیزتر
english_translation: The sword of clemency is sharper than the sword of iron nay it is more productive of victory than a hundred armies

persian_text: گفت حسرت میخورم که صد هزار دیده بودی تا همیکردم نثار
english_translation: I am grieving she replied  would that I had a hundred thousand eyes to lavish

persian_text: چونک آن مخفی نماند از محرمان ذات و وصفی چیست کان ماند نهان
english_translation: Since that does not remain hidden from familiars what is the essence and attribute that should remain concealed

persian_text: جز از آن خلاق گوش و چشم و سر نشنوم از جان خود هم خیر و شر
english_translation: Save from the Creator of ear and eye and head I will hear neither good nor evilnot even from my own soul

persian_text: نعمت حق را به جان و عقل ده نه به طبع پر زحیر پر گره
english_translation: Bestow the bounty of God on the spirit and reason not on the nature full of disease and complications

persian_text: میگریزی همچو یوسف ز اندهی تا ز نرتع نلعب افتی در چهی
english_translation: Like Joseph thou art fleeing from one sorrow to fall into a well through let us frolic and play

persian_text: شه برای من ز زندان یاد کرد صد هزاران بسته را آزاد کرد
english_translation: For my sake the King bethought him of the prison  and set free hundreds of thousands of those in bondage

persian_text: وقت خواب و مرگ از وی میرود وقت بیداری قرینش میشود
english_translation: At the time of sleep and death it goes from it  at the time of waking it rejoins it

persian_text: صد هزاران فایده ست و هر یکی صد هزاران پیش آن یک اندکی
english_translation: There are myriads of benefits and every myriad is a few beside that one

persian_text: خویشتن را من نمیبینم هنر تا شوم مقبول این مسعود در
english_translation: I see not any merit in myself that I should be accepted of this auspicious door

persian_text: گوهر معقول را محسوس کرد پیر بینا بهر کم عقلی مرد
english_translation: On account of the mans little understanding the clairvoyant Pír made the intelligible pearls objects of senseperception

persian_text: میبسوزد هر خزان مر باغ را باز رویاند گل صباغ را
english_translation: Every autumn He burns the garden He makes to grow again the rose that dyes

persian_text: در حدیث آمدی که مومن در دعا چون امان خواهد ز دوزخ از خدا
english_translation: It is related in the Hadíth that when the true believer prays to God for protection from Hell

persian_text: آنچنان که نیش کژدم بر کنی تا که یابد او ز کشتن ایمنی
english_translation: Just as you would extract the scorpions sting in order that it might be saved from being killed

persian_text: هر کسی را خود دو پا و یکسرست با هزاران پا و سر تن نادرست
english_translation: Yet every one has two feet and one head the body with thousands of feet and heads is a rarity

persian_text: آن عظیم العرش عرش او محیط تخت دادش بر همه جانها بسیط
english_translation: Allencompassing is the Throne of Him who is throned in grandeur over all souls is spread the Throne of His justice

persian_text: اول او بد برگافشان بره را گشت معجز آن گروه غره را
english_translation: At first it was shaking down leaves for the lambs it reduced to impotence that deluded people

persian_text: مارگیری اژدها آورده است بوالعجب نادر شکاری کرده است
english_translation: A snakecatcher has brought a dragon he has captured a marvellous rare beast

persian_text: انس تو با دایه و لالا چه شدگر کسی شاید بغیر حق عضد
english_translation: If any one but God is worthy to be relied upon what has become of thy friendship with thy nurse and tutor

persian_text: گر نماید خواجه را این دم غلط ز اول والنجم بر خوان چند خط
english_translation: If this saying appear to the Khwája to be erroneous recite a few lines at the beginning of WalNajm

persian_text: هین به ملک نوبتی شادی مکنای تو بستهی نوبت آزادی مکن
english_translation: Take heed Do not exult in a kingdom bestowed in turns  O thou who art the bondsman of Vicissitude do not act as though thou wert free

persian_text: این همه بشنید آن خاک نژند زان گمان بد بدش در گوش بند
english_translation: The wretched Earth heard all this  in her ear was a plug arising from that evil suspicion

persian_text: ساعتی با آن گروه مجتبی چون مراقب گشتم و از خود جدا
english_translation: When for a while I had taken part with that elect company in contemplation and had been separated from myself

persian_text: هر که او بر خو و بر طبع تو زیست پیش طبع تو ولی است و نبیست
english_translation: Whosoever lives in accordance with thy disposition and nature seems to thy nature to be a saint and a prophet

persian_text: وانما سری ز اسرارت به ما تا ببریم از میان زنارها
english_translation: Reveal to us one of thy mysteries that we may cut from our waists the cords

persian_text: باز هستی جهان حس و رنگ تنگتر آمد که زندانی است تنگ
english_translation: Again the existence of the world of sense and colour is narrower  for tis a narrow prison

persian_text: ای ضیاء الحق حسامالدین بگیر یک دو کاغذ بر فزا در وصف پیر
english_translation: O Splendour of the Truth Husámuddín take one or two sheets of paper and add in description of the Pír

persian_text: عکس عبدالله همه نوری بود عکس بیگانه همه کوری بود
english_translation: The reflexion of the servant of God is wholly luminous the reflexion of the stranger is wholly blindness

persian_text: دل محیطست اندرین خطهی وجود زر همیافشاند از احسان و جود
english_translation: The heart is encompassing this realm of existence and scattering gold in beneficence and bounty

persian_text: چون همه نارست جانش نیست نور که افکند در نار سوزان جز قشور
english_translation: Inasmuch as his spirit is wholly fire not light who would throw anything but husks into a blazing fire

persian_text: جمله اجزای تنش خصم ویند کز بهاری لافد ایشان در دیند

persian_text: این رجا و خوف در پرده بود تا پس این پرده پرورده شود

persian_text: ای برادر تو همان اندیشهای ما بقی تو استخوان و ریشهای

persian_text: آنک خود را فتحها پنداشتید تخم منحوسی خود میکاشتید

persian_text: در حق او مدح و در حق تو ذم در حق او شهد و در حق تو سم

persian_text: چون در آید در تگ دریا بود دیدهی فرعون کی بینا بود

persian_text: با زبان معنوی گل با جعل این همیگوید کهای گنده بغل

persian_text: مر مروت را نهاده زیر پا گشته زندان دوزخی ز آن نان ربا

persian_text: نیست اندر بحر شرک و پیچ پیچ لیک با احول چه گویم هیچ هیچ

persian_text: آن هنر فی جیدنا حبل مسد روز مردن نیست زان فنها مدد

persian_text: آن صفای آینه وصف دل است کاو نقوش بیعدد را قابل است

persian_text: در مقامی زهر و در جایی دوا در مقامی کفر و در جایی روا

english_translation: I advanced farther running what thing those candles are of the signs of the Divine Majesty

persian_text: موج بر وی میزند بیاحتراز خفته پویان در بیابان دراز
english_translation: The waves are beating upon him without restraint he asleep is running in the long wilderness

persian_text: اندکی سرگین سگ در آستین خلق را بشکافت و آمد با حنین
english_translation: a small quantity of dogs dung in his sleeve he cleft the crowd and approached with cries of grief

persian_text: عیب شد نسبت به مخلوق جهول نی به نسبت با خداوند قبول
english_translation: Fault arises in relation to the ignorant creature not in relation to the Lord of favour

persian_text: ما پی گل سوی بستانها شده گل نموده آن و آن خاری بده
english_translation: We went into the gardens to gather roses they seemed to be roses but they were thorns

persian_text: گردگان ما درین مطحن شکست هر چه گوییم از غم خود اندکست
english_translation: Our walnuts are crushed in this mill whatever we may tell of our anguish tis little

persian_text: چون که مخزنهای افلاک و عقول چون خسی آمد بر چشم رسول
english_translation: Inasmuch as to the eye of the Prophet the treasuries of the celestial spheres and intelligences seemed as a straw

persian_text: ریخت چندان اشک و کرد او بس فغان که ازو گریان شدند آن دیگران
english_translation: She shed so many tears and made much lamentation that those others began to weep because of her

persian_text: هین چه لاف است اینکه از تو بهتران در نیاوردند اندر خاطر آن
english_translation: Eh what brag is this which thy betters never brought into their minds

persian_text: پادشاهی بندهای را از کرم بر گزیده بود بر جمله حشم
english_translation: A King had of his grace preferred a certain slave above all his retinue

persian_text: این کسی دیدست کز یک راویه سرد گردد سوز چندان هاویه
english_translation: Has any one seen this that the burning glow of so many Hells should be cooled by a single waterskin

persian_text: گول را و غول را کو را فریفت از خلاص و فوز میباید شکیفت
english_translation: the fool and the ghoul who deceived him must ever endure to be deprived of salvation and felicity

persian_text: پیش حالیبین که در جهلست و شک صبح صادق صبح کاذب هر دو یک
english_translation: To the seer of the present who is in ignorance and doubt both the true dawn and the false dawn are one

persian_text: خوش مربع چون شهان بر تخت خویش او فراز اوج و کشتیاش به پیش
english_translation: at ease crosslegged as kings upon their thrones he above the zenith and the ship before him

persian_text: پیش آب زندگانی کس نمرد پیش آبت آب حیوانست درد
english_translation: None died in the presence of the Water of Life compared with thy water the Water of Life is dregs

persian_text: پس حلیمه گفت ای جانم فدا مر تراای شیخ خوب خوشندا
english_translation: Then Halíma said Oh my soul be a ransom for thee O goodly and fairspoken Shaykh

persian_text: پس همه دانستهاند آن را یقین که فرستد باد ربالعالمین
english_translation: Therefore all have known for certain that the wind is sent by the Lord of created beings

persian_text: جنبش او وا شکافد سیب را بر نتابد سیب آن آسیب را
english_translation: Its movement splits the apple asunder the apple cannot endure that shock

persian_text: بعد از آن هر صورتی را بشکنی همچو حیدر باب خیبر بر کنی
english_translation: After that thou wilt break every form like Haydar  thou wilt uproot the gate of Khaybar

persian_text: زآنک این فانی دلیل باقیست لابهی مستان دلیل ساقیست
english_translation: For this perishable is a proof of the everlasting  the maundering of the intoxicated is a proof of the Cupbearer

persian_text: در حقیقت ره زن جان خودند راه عقل و جان خود را خود زدند
english_translation: In truth they act as brigands against themselves they themselves waylay their own mind and spirit

persian_text: پنج کرت اینچنین آواز سخت میرسید و دل همیشد لختلخت
english_translation: Five times came such a terrible voice and his heart was being rent piecemeal

persian_text: قلعهای سلطان عمارت میکند لیک دعوی امارت میکند
english_translation: he keeps the Kings fortress in good repair but he is claiming to be in command

persian_text: نوبت صدرنگیست و صددلی عالم یک رنگ کی گردد جلی
english_translation: Tis the turn of manycolouredness and manymindedness how should the onecoloured world become unveiled

persian_text: مینماید او که چشمی میزند ابلهان سازیدهاند او را سند
english_translation: It appears to wink the foolish have made it their support

persian_text: رفتنم سوی نماز و آن خلا بهر تعلیمست ره مر خلق را
english_translation: My going to prayer and to that solitude is for the purpose of teaching the people the Way

persian_text: رخت ما هم رخت ما را راه زن جسم ما مر جان ما را جامه کن
english_translation: Our goods waylay our goods our bodies tear the garment from our souls

persian_text: تو چو عقلی ما مثال این زبان این زبان از عقل دارد این بیان
english_translation: Thou art as the reason we are like this tongue this tongue hath its expression from the reason

persian_text: عاشقان از درد زان نالیدهاند که نظر ناجایگه مالیدهاند
english_translation: The cause why lovers have moaned in grief is that they have rubbed their eyes malapropos

persian_text: از خر عیسی دریغش نیست قند لیک خر آمد به خلقت که پسند
english_translation: Candy is not withheld from the ass of Jesus by him  but the ass is naturally pleased with straw

persian_text: رفت پیغامبر به رغبت بهر او اندر آخر وآمد اندر جست و جو
english_translation: The Prophet went eagerly into the stable for him and began to search

persian_text: سر طلب کردن موسی خضر را علیهماالسلام با کمال نبوت و قربت
english_translation: The mystery of Moses seeking Khizr notwithstanding his perfection as a prophet and as one nigh unto God

persian_text: خواهرانت را ز بخششهای راد هیچ میدانی که آن سلطان چه داد
english_translation: Dost thou know at all what noble gifts that Sultan gave to thy sisters

persian_text: که برو آنجا که اول منزلست حق آن نعمت گروگان دلست
english_translation: Saying Begone to the place that is thy first lodging obligation for that kindness is the hearts pledge

persian_text: بعد از آن عالم بگیرد اسم تو دو جهان محکوم تو چون جسم تو
english_translation: After that thy name will conquer the world the two worlds ruled by thee like thy body

persian_text: نه از برای منتی بل مینمود بر درستی محبت صد شهود
english_translation: Not for the sake of reproach nay he was displaying a hundred testimonies of the trueness of his love

persian_text: گر نبودی بهر عشق پاک را کی وجودی دادمی افلاک را
english_translation: Had it not been for pure Loves sake how should I have bestowed an existence on the heavens

persian_text: او به قول و فعل یار ما بود چون به حکم حال آیی لا بود
english_translation: It is our friend in word and deed when you come to the case of inward feeling  it is naught

persian_text: پس نکو گفت آن حکیم کامیار که تو طفلی خانه پر نقش و نگار
english_translation: That fortunate Sage then has said well Thou art a child the house is full of pictures and paintings

persian_text: حکم حق گسترد بهر ما بساط که بگویید از طریق انبساط
english_translation: The decree of God spread for us the carpet   Speak ye in the way of boldness

persian_text: دید کو سابق زراعت کرد ماش او همیداند چه خواهد بود چاش
english_translation: He perceived that in the past he had sown pulse he knows what the produce will be

persian_text: تا که آن بو جاذب جانت شود تا که آن بو نور چشمانت شود
english_translation: In order that that scent may draw your soul  in order that that scent may become the light of your eyes

persian_text: هم خلق گردم من ار تازه و نوم چونک یار اینچنین خواری شوم
english_translation: I too though I am fresh and new would become shabby through being associated with such a contemptible person

persian_text: چون خدا از خود سال و کد کند پس دعای خویش را چون رد کند
english_translation: Since God asks and begs of Himself how then should He refuse to grant His own prayer

persian_text: گفتن خواجه در خواب به آن پایمرد وجوه وام آن دوست را
english_translation: How the Khwája disclosed to the bailiff in his dream the means of paying the debts incurred by the friend

persian_text: بر درختان شکر گویان برگ و شاخ که زهی ملک و زهی عرصهی فراخ
english_translation: The leaves and boughs on the trees are giving thanks  crying Oh what a fine kingdom Oh what a broad expanse

persian_text: چونک مقضی بد دوام آن روش میدهدشان از دلایل پرورش
english_translation: inasmuch as their continuance in that course was destined God feeds them with proofs

persian_text: محرم آن آه کمیابست بس شبرو و پنهانروی کن چون عسس
english_translation: Very seldom is there found a fit confidant for that sigh go by night and let thy movement be hidden like the nightpatrol

persian_text: عشق در هنگام استیلا و خشم زشت گرداند لطیفان را به چشم
english_translation: Love in the hour of domination and anger makes the pleasing ones to become hideous to the eye

persian_text: اینکه در وقتست باشد تا اجل وان دگر یار ابد قرن ازل
english_translation: This which is in Time endures till death while the other is the associate of everlastingness and the peer of eternity

persian_text: صد ره و مخلص بود از چپ و راست از قضا بسته شود کو اژدهاست
english_translation: There are a hundred ways and places of refuge on left and right they are barred by Destiny for it is a dragon

persian_text: سرکه مفروش و هزاران جان ببین از قناعت غرق بحر انگبین
english_translation: Do not look sour and see thousands of souls plunged through contentment in an ocean of honey

persian_text: گر تو علامهی زمانی در جهان نک فنای این جهانبین وین زمان
english_translation: If in the world thou art the most learned scholar of the time behold the passing away of this world and this time

persian_text: کم خوری خوی بد و خشکی و دق پر خوری شد تخمه را تن مستحق
english_translation: If you eat little illtemper and anaemia and consumption if you eat your fill your body will incur indigestion

persian_text: باقی قصهی هاروت و ماروت و نکال و عقوبت ایشان هم در دنیا به چاه بابل
english_translation: The rest of the story of Hárút and Márút and how an exemplary punishment was inflicted on them even in this world in the pit of Babylon

persian_text: خبر کردن خروس از مرگ خواجه
english_translation: How the cock foretold the death of the Khwája

persian_text: نه ترا از روی ظاهر طاعتی نه ترا در سر و باطن نیتی
english_translation: Neither outwardly hast thou any act of piety  nor inwardly and in thy heart an intention

persian_text: گفت با جسم آیتی تا جان شد او گفت با خورشید تا رخشان شد او
english_translation: He spake to the body a sign  so that it became spirit He spake to the sun so that it became radiant

persian_text: چونک هر دم راه خود را میزنم با دگر کس سازگاری چون کنم
english_translation: Since I am incessantly waylaying myself how should I act in harmony with another

persian_text: با دل خود شه نفرمود این قدر شیر را مفریب زین راس البقر
english_translation: The King did not say in his heart Do not deceive the lion so greatly by means of the head of an ox

persian_text: هر زمان که قصد خواندن باشدت یا ز مصحفها قرائت بایدت
english_translation: Whensoever thou intendest to read or wantest the lection from copies

persian_text: هم ملک هم عقل حق را واجدی هر دو آدم را معین و ساجدی
english_translation: The Angel as well as the Intelligence is a finder of God each of the twain is a helper and worshipper of Adam

persian_text: جنگ پیغمبر مدار صلح شد صلح این آخر زمان ز آن جنگ بد
english_translation: The wars of the Prophet became the pivot of peace the peace of this latter age was from those wars

persian_text: او ز حیوانها فزونتر جان کند در جهان باریک کاریها کند
english_translation: He suffers more anxiety than the beasts he practises subtle arts in the world

persian_text: آتش خشم از شما هم حلم شد ظلمت جهل از شما هم علم شد
english_translation: at once the fire of anger in you hath turned to forbearance and the darkness of ignorance in you hath turned to knowledge

persian_text: اول فکر آخر آمد در عمل بنیت عالم چنان دان در ازل
english_translation: The beginning which is thought comes to an end in action know that in such wise was the construction of the world in eternity

persian_text: پاکی محدود تو خواهد مدد ور نه اندر خرج کم گردد عدد
english_translation: Your finite purity wants reinforcement otherwise number is diminished in expenditure

persian_text: ور بگیری کیت جستجو کند نقش با نقاش چون نیرو کند
english_translation: And if thou take it away who will make inquiry of thee How should the picture strive with the painter

persian_text: دیده هر ساعت دلش در اجتهاد روز و شب چفسیده او بر اجتهاد
english_translation: Every hour saw his heart in the struggle by day and night he was firmly attached to the struggle

persian_text: آمدی در صورت باران و تاب میروی اندر صفات مستطاب
english_translation: Thou camest in the form of rain and heat thou wilt go into the goodly attributes

persian_text: میکشانش در جهاد و در قتال مردوار الله یجزیک الوصال
english_translation: Lead it manfully to the warfare and battle God will reward thee with access

persian_text: هالک آید پیش وجهش هست و نیست هستی اندر نیستی خود طرفهایست
english_translation: Before His Face the existent and the nonexistent perish existence in nonexistence is in sooth a marvellous thing

persian_text: گفت روبه آن توکل نادرست کم کسی اندر توکل ماهرست
english_translation: The fox replied Such trust in God is exceptional few are proficient in trust in God

persian_text: اندر این آتش بدیدم عالمی ذره ذره اندر او عیسی دمی
english_translation: In this fire I have seen a world wherein every atom possesses the breath of Jesus

persian_text: هر که بر گوید نشان از اشترم مژدگانی میدهم چندین درم
english_translation: Whoever will tell a clue to my camel I will give so many dirhems as a reward

persian_text: ذوق باید تا دهد طاعات بر مغز باید تا دهد دانه شجر
english_translation: Spiritual savour is required in order that devotions may yield fruit a kernel is required in order that the berry may produce a tree

persian_text: هیچکس در ملک او بیامر او در نیفزاید سر یک تای مو
english_translation: Without His command no one in His kingdom shall add the tip of a single hair

persian_text: کودکان گرچه به یک مکتب درند در سبق هر یک ز یک بالاترند
english_translation: Children though they are at one school surpass each other in lessons

persian_text: چون ز عادت گشت محکم خوی بد خشم آید بر کسی کت واکشد
english_translation: When an evil disposition becomes confirmed by habit you are enraged with any one who restrains you

persian_text: جزوها را رویها سوی کل است بلبلان را عشق با روی گل است
english_translation: The faces of particulars are set towards the universal nightingales are in love with the face of the rose

persian_text: جامه پوشان را نظر بر گازر است جان عریان را تجلی زیور است
english_translation: Those who wear clothes look to the launderer the soul of the naked hath illumination as its adornment

persian_text: اینچنین ساحر درون تست و سر ان فی الوسواس سحرا مستتر
english_translation: Such a magician is within you and latent truly there is a concealed magic in temptation

persian_text: ای دهندهی عقلها فریاد رس تا نخواهی تو نخواهد هیچکس
english_translation: O Giver of understandings come to my help none wills unless Thou will

persian_text: از منافق عذر رد آمد نه خوب زانک در لب بود آن نه در قلوب
english_translation: The excuse made by the hypocrite was bad not good because it was on their lips not in their hearts

persian_text: آب ما محبوس گل ماندست هین بحر رحمت جذب کن ما را ز طین
english_translation: our water has remained imprisoned in earth Hark O Sea of Mercy draw us out of the clay

persian_text: آن بزرگین گفت ای اخوان خیر ما نه نر بودیم اندر نصح غیر
english_translation: The eldest said O men of probity were not we masculine in giving counsel to others

persian_text: چون خوری یکبار از ماکول نور خاک ریزی بر سر نان و تنور
english_translation: When you eat once of the light you will pour earth over the bread and oven

persian_text: من ترا بر چرخ گشته نردبان تو شده در حرب من تیر و کمان
english_translation: I have become for thee a ladder to Heaven and thou hast become a bow and arrow in combat with me

persian_text: عنف کردن معاویه با ابلیس
english_translation: How Muáwiya dealt sternly with Iblís

persian_text: لیک اغلب هوشها در افتکار همچو خفاشند ظلمت دوستدار
english_translation: But most minds in thinking are lovers of darkness like the bat

persian_text: پس همیگویند هر نقش و نگار مژده مژده نک همیآید بهار
english_translation: Therefore every fair form and shape is crying Good news good news Lo here comes the Spring

persian_text: هین سگ نفس ترا زنده مخواه کاو عدوی جان تست از دیرگاه
english_translation: Take heed Do not wish your currish soul alive for it is the enemy of your spirit since long ago

persian_text: ای خلیل اینجا شرار و دود نیست جز که سحر و خدعهی نمرود نیست
english_translation: O Khalíl  here are no sparks and smoke tis naught but the sorcery and deceit of Nimrod

persian_text: نبض او بگرفت و واقف شد ز حال که امید صحت او بد محال
english_translation: He felt his pulse and ascertained his state  that it was absurd to hope for his recovery

persian_text: هر زمان میگفت ای عمران مرا سخت از جا برده است این نعرهها
english_translation: Every moment he would say O Imrán these clamours have upset me mightily

persian_text: خویش را دیدیم و رسوایی خویش امتحان ما مکنای شاه بیش
english_translation: We have seen ourselves and our shame Put us not to further trial O King

persian_text: صد هزاران نام خوش را کرد ننگ صد هزاران زیرکان را کرد دنگ
english_translation: It has disgraced a hundred thousand good names it has stupefied a hundred thousand clever men

persian_text: گر زبانت مدح آن شه میتند هفت اندامت شکایت میکند
english_translation: If thy tongue is weaving praise of that king thy seven members are complaining

persian_text: ظاهرست آثار و میوهی رحمتش لیک کی داند جز او ماهیتش
english_translation: The effects and fruit of His mercy are manifest but how should any one except Him know its quiddity

persian_text: پس بکن دفعش چو نمرودی به جنگ سوی او کش در هوا تیری خدنگ
english_translation: Like a Nimrod repel Him by war  Launch an arrow of poplarwood into the air against Him

persian_text: باغ سبز عشق کاو بیمنتهاست جز غم و شادی در او بس میوههاست
english_translation: In the verdant garden of Love which is without end there are many fruits besides sorrow and joy

persian_text: سنقر آنجا ماند تا نزدیک چاشت میر سنقر را زمانی چشمداشت
english_translation: Sunqur remained there till near the forenoon the Amír awaited him for some time

persian_text: قطرهای بر ریز بر ما زان سبو شمهای زان گلستان با ما بگو
english_translation: Spill upon us one drop from that pitcher give us one word that smells of that garden

persian_text: پیش او آیید اگر خاین نیید نیشکر گردید ازو گرچه نیید
english_translation: Come to him if ye are not disloyal be made sugarcane by him though ye are reeds

persian_text: کارگه چون جای باش عامل است آنکه بیرون است از وی غافل است
english_translation: Since the workshop is the dwellingplace of the Worker he that is outside is unaware of Him

persian_text: از حشم هر که به ما کردی گله از بلا و فقر و خوف و زلزله
english_translation: Whenever one of the retainers complained to us of affliction and poverty and fear and agitation

persian_text: رنج و غم را حق پی آن آفرید تا بدین ضد خوش دلی آید پدید
english_translation: God created pain and sorrow for the purpose that happiness might be made manifest by means of this opposite

persian_text: پر خود میکند طاوسی به دشت یک حکیمی رفته بود آنجا بگشت
english_translation: A peacock was tearing out his feathers in the open country where a sage had gone for a walk

persian_text: پیش از آنک اشکسته گردد کاروان آن زمان چوبک بزنای پاسبان
english_translation: O watchman use your rattle before the caravan is ruined

persian_text: جادوی کردش عجوزهی کابلی کی برد زان رشک سحر بابلی
english_translation: An old woman of Kábulbewitched him with a sorcery of which the magic of Babylon would be envious

persian_text: شاه مردان و امیرالمومنین راهبان و رازدان و دوستبین
english_translation: A king of men and commander of the Faithful a keeper of the Way and a knower of secrets and a discerner of friends

persian_text: گوشت کاندر شست تو ماهی رباست آنچنان لقمه نه بخشش نه سخاست
english_translation: The meat that catches the fish on the hooksuch a morsel is neither bounty nor generosity

persian_text: پس خداعی را خداعی شد جزا کاسه زن کوزه بخور اینک سزا
english_translation: So a deceit is paid with a deceit strike with a cup get struck with a jugserve you right

persian_text: گر قضا صد بار قصد جان کند هم قضا جانت دهد درمان کند
english_translation: If the Divine destiny a hundred times attempts thy life yet the Divine destiny gives thee life and heals thee

persian_text: حامله گریان ز زه کاین المناص و آن جنین خندان که پیش آمد خلاص
english_translation: The pregnant woman weeps at childbirth saying Where is the refugebut the embryo laughs saying Deliverance has appeared

persian_text: مردم هنگامه افزونتر شود کدیه و توزیع نیکوتر رود
english_translation: The greater the crowd the better goes the begging and contributing

persian_text: عاشق رنج است نادان تا ابد خیز لا أقسم بخوان تا فی کبد
english_translation: The ignorant man is in love with pain unto everlasting Arise and read I swear as far as in trouble

persian_text: کهگر ایشان با شما همره شوند غازیان بیمغز همچون که شوند
english_translation: For if they go along with you the warriors will become pith less like straw

persian_text: زانک خاکی را نباشد تاب آنکه زند بر وی شعاعش جاودان
english_translation: Because a creature of earth cannot endure that its beams should strike upon it everlastingly

persian_text: بسته درهای موالید از سبب چون پشیمان شد ولی ز آن دست رب
english_translation: When the saint repents he closes the doors of the results from the cause by that hand of the Lord

persian_text: ترک این فکر و پریشانی بگو حال و یار و کار نیکوتر بجو
english_translation: Take leave of this thought and repentance seek a better state and friend and work

persian_text: حاصل آنک هر که او طالب بود جان مطلوبش درو راغب بود
english_translation: If I explain this twill be endless the Mathnawí will amount to eighty volumes

persian_text: در خواب گفتن هاتف مر عمر را که چندین زر از بیتالمال به آن مرد ده که در گورستان خفته است
english_translation: How the heavenly voice spoke to Umar while he was asleep saying Give a certain sum of gold from the public treasury to the man who is sleeping in the graveyard

persian_text: در زمانه صاحب دامی بود همچو ما احمق که صید خود کند
english_translation: Is any owner of a trap in the world such a dolt that like us he tries to catch himself

persian_text: گوهر ار بردست او بردست و بس زو ملازمتر به خاتون نیست کس
english_translation: If has taken the pearl only he can have taken it none is more closely attached to the Lady than he

persian_text: هفت سال ایوب با صبر و رضا در بلا خوش بود با ضیف خدا
english_translation: Job the patient and wellpleased  showed sweetness to Gods guest during seven years in tribulation

persian_text: گر رهایی بایدت زین چاه تنگای برادر رو بر آذر بیدرنگ
english_translation: If thou hast need of deliverance from this narrow dungeon  O brother go without delay on the fire

persian_text: کی توان با شیعه گفتن از عمر کی توان بربط زدن در پیش کر
english_translation: How can one speak of Umar to Shíites How can one play a lute before the deaf

persian_text: هست پنهان معنی هر داروی همچو سحر و صنعت هر جادوی
english_translation: The virtue of every drug is hidden like magic and the art of any sorcerer

persian_text: یادش آمد مردی آن پهلوان که بکشت او شیر و اندامش چنان
english_translation: Venit in mentem virilatas ducis fortissimi qui leonem interfecit dum membrum ejus tale est She remembered the virility of the Captain who killed the lion with his organ like that

persian_text: کیست مولا آنک آزادت کند بند رقیت ز پایت بر کند
english_translation: Who is the protector He that sets you free and removes the fetters of servitude from your feet

persian_text: تو اگر صد قفل بنهی بر دری بر کند آن جمله را خیرهسری
english_translation: If you put a hundred locks on a door some reckless fellow may remove them all

persian_text: چند روزی که ز پیشم رانده است چشم من در روی خوبش مانده است
english_translation: During the short while since He drove me from His presence mine eye hath remained upon His beauteous face

persian_text: آن یکی میخورد نان فخفره گفت سایل چون بدین استت شره
english_translation: A certain person was eating bread made of bran some one asked him How are you so fond of this

persian_text: چون ز سینه آب دانش جوش کرد نه شود گنده نه دیرینه نه زرد
english_translation: When the water of knowledge gushes from the breast it does not become fetid or old or yellow

persian_text: قلب میگوید ز نخوت هر دممای زر خالص من از تو کی کمم
english_translation: The adulterated coin is ever saying to me arrogantly O pure gold how am I inferior to thee

persian_text: او همیبیند که آن از بهر اوست چشم دشمن بسته زان و چشم دوست
english_translation: He sees that that is for his sake the eye of foe and friend is barred from it

persian_text: من طفیل تو بنوشم آب هم که طفیلی در تبع به جهد ز غم
english_translation: I too will drink the water as thy parasite for a parasite in following  is relieved from anguish

persian_text: خاک از دردی نشیند زیر آب خاک بین کز عرش بگذشت از شتاب
english_translation: Earth from its grossness settles beneath water see how earth has sped beyond the empyrean

persian_text: پیش چشم خویش او میدید مرگ رفت و میلرزید او مانند برگ
english_translation: He saw death before his eyes he went  trembling like a leaf

persian_text: یا رسولالله جوان ار شیرزاد غیر مرد پیر سر لشکر مباد
english_translation: O Messenger of God if the youth is lionborn  may none but an old man be head of the army

persian_text: از پدر یابید آن ملکای عجب تا غرورش داد ظلمات نسب
english_translation: Oh tis wonderful Did he obtain that empire from his father so that he was befooled by the darkness of noble lineage

persian_text: کای خداتان وا خریده از غرور نک ریاض فضل و نک رب غفور
english_translation: Saying O ye whom God hath redeemed from delusion behold the gardens of grace and behold the forgiving Lord

persian_text: همچو آن چوپان که میگفت ای خدا پیش چوپان و محب خود بیا
english_translation: like the shepherd who used to say O God come to Thy shepherd and lover

persian_text: تو از آن بگذشتهای کز مرگ تن ترسی وز تفریق اجزای بدن
english_translation: Thou art past being afraid of bodily death and dismemberment of the limbs

persian_text: خواب زن کمتر ز خواب مرد دان از پی نقصان عقل و ضعف جان
english_translation: Know that a womans dreams are inferior to those of a man because of her deficiency of intelligence and weakness of soul

persian_text: هر چه جسم آمد ولادت وصف اوست هر چه مولود است او زین سوی جوست
english_translation: Birth is the attribute of everything that is body whatever is born is on this side of the river

persian_text: تو قیاس گریه بر گریه مساز هست زین گریه بدان راه دراز
english_translation: Do not judge weeping by the analogy of weeping tis a long way from this weeping to that

persian_text: نه که ما را دست فضلش کاشته ست از عدم ما را نه او برداشته ست
english_translation: Was it not the hand of His bounty that sowed me Was it not He that raised me up from nonexistence

persian_text: از سبب اندیشه کرد آن ذو لباب دید علت خوردن بسیار از آب
english_translation: that possessor of the marrow meditated concerning the cause he perceived that the cause was much waterdrinking

persian_text: آب در جو ز آن نمیگیرد قرار ز آنکه آن جو نیست تشنه و آب خوار
english_translation: The water does not settle in the riverbed because the riverbed is not thirsty and waterdrinking

persian_text: داستان آن درویش کی آن گیلانی را دعا کرد کی خدا ترا به سلامت به خان و مان باز رساناد
english_translation: Story of the dervish who blessed a man of Gílán saying May God bring thee back in safety to thy home and household

persian_text: در طلب زن دایما تو هر دو دست که طلب در راه نیکو رهبرست
english_translation: Always apply yourself with both hands to seeking for search is an excellent guide on the way

persian_text: مرغ پس در خود فرو رفت آن زمان توسنش سر بستد از جذب عنان
english_translation: Thereupon the bird pondered deeply its restive steed took its head from the pull of the rein

persian_text: همنشینا هین در آ اندر چمن گوید ای جان من نیارم آمدن
english_translation: Hark neighbour come into the garden He says O soul I cannot come

persian_text: ای بسا کفار را سودای دین بندشان ناموس و کبر آن و این
english_translation: Oh many are the infidels that have a passionate longing for the Religion  their chain is reputation and pride in this and that

persian_text: هیچ کوزهگر کند کوزه شتاب بهر عین کوزه نه بر بوی آب
english_translation: Does any potter make a pot in haste for the sake of the pot itself and not in hope of the water

persian_text: برق میانداخت میسوزید سنگ ابر میغرید رخ میریخت رنگ
english_translation: It was shooting lightning the rocks were burning the cloud was roaring cheeks were shedding colour

persian_text: سوی دام آن تملق شادمان همچو مرغی سوی دانهی امتحان
english_translation: joyously towards the snare of that flattery as a bird towards the bait of tribulation

persian_text: سبزه رویاند ز خاکت آن دلیل نیست کم از سم اسپ جبرئیل
english_translation: That Guide will cause verdure to grow from thy earth he is not inferior to the hoof of the horse of Gabriel

persian_text: آب خوش کاو روح را همشیره شد در غدیری زرد و تلخ و تیره شد
english_translation: The sweet water that was a sister to the spirit in a pool became yellow and bitter and turbid

persian_text: گرد خمخانه بر آمد آن مرید بهر شیخ از هر خمی او میچشید
english_translation: The disciple went round the winecellar tasting of every jar on the Shaykhs behalf

persian_text: هر که آخربینتر او مسعودتر هر که آخربینتر او مطرودتر
english_translation: The more any one regards the end the more blessed he is the more any one regards the stable the more banned he is

persian_text: آفتاب اندر فلک کژ میجهد در سیهروزی خسوفش میدهد
english_translation: the sun in heaven jumps crookedly He causes it to be eclipsed in black disgrace

persian_text: از بر حق میرسد تفضیلها باز هم از حق رسد تبدیلها
english_translation: From God come preferments  from God also come changes

persian_text: حور را این پرده زالی میکند بدر را کم از هلالی میکند
english_translation: This veil makes the houri a hag it makes the fullmoon less than a new moon

persian_text: دادن شاه گوهر را میان دیوان و مجمع به دست وزیر
english_translation: How the King  in the midst of the Diwán and assembly place put a pearl in the hand of the Vizier

persian_text: پیش او بنوشت شه کای مقبلم وقت آمد زود فارغ کن دلم
english_translation: The king wrote to him saying O my fortunate one the time is come quickly set my mind at ease

persian_text: جز یکی لقیه که اول از قضا بر وی افتاد و شد او را دلربا
english_translation: Except one meeting which happened to him by destiny at the first and enravished his heart

persian_text: چون نهالی کاشتی آبش بده چون گشادش دادهای بگشا گره
english_translation: Since thou hast planted the sapling give it water Since thou hast given it freedom  untie the knots

persian_text: چون ز راز و ناز او گوید زبان یا جمیل الستر خواند آسمان
english_translation: When the tongue tells of His mystery and coquetry Heaven chants  O Thou that art goodly in covering

persian_text: جزو عقل این از آن عقل کلست جنبش این سایه زان شاخ گلست
english_translation: The particular intelligence of this is derived from that Universal Intelligence the motion of this shadow is derived from that Rosebough

persian_text: وز حذر از ننگ و از نامی کنند چارهای سازند و پیغامی کنند
english_translation: And if they be afraid of disgrace and reputation they devise some means and send a message

persian_text: ژیغژیغ تلخ آن درهای مرگ نشنود گوش حریص از حرص برگ
english_translation: from greed for provision the ear of the covetous does not hear the harsh creaking of those doors of death

persian_text: پنج از آن چون حس به سوی رنگ و بو پنج از آن چون حس باطن رازجو
english_translation: Five of those  like the senses facing towards colour and perfume  five of them like the interior senses seeking the mystery

persian_text: آب حیوان بود و دریای کرم زنده گشته هم عرب زو هم عجم
english_translation: He was the Water of Life and the Ocean of Bounty by him both Arabs and foreigners were revived

persian_text: آنک چشمش بست گرچه گربزست ز احولی اندر دو چشمش خربزست
english_translation: Although he whose eye it has bandaged is clever because of his seeing double in his eyes the ass is a goat

persian_text: بعد ازین کد و مذلت جان من بیست عباساند در انبان من
english_translation: Henceforth beggary and abasement are my soul in my wallet are twenty Abbás

persian_text: چون شدی اول سیه اندر لقا دور بودی از نفاق و از شقا
english_translation: Since it would have become black at first in confrontation it would have been far from duplicity and damnation

persian_text: شیخ میشد با مریدی بیدرنگ سوی شهری نان بدانجا بود تنگ
english_translation: The Shaykh accompanied by a disciple was going without delay towards a certain town where bread was scarce

persian_text: پیش بزازان قز و ادکن بود بهر گز باشد اگر آهن بود
english_translation: The drapers have silk and duncoloured cloth if iron be  it is for a yardmeasure

persian_text: ای امام چشمروشن در صلا چشم روشن باید ایدر پیشوا
english_translation: O clearsighted Imám  the leader in the ritual prayer must always be clearsighted

persian_text: باز میمالید پر بر دست شاه بیزبان میگفت من کردم گناه
english_translation: the falcon was rubbing its wings against the Kings hand without tongue it was saying I have sinned

persian_text: این سخن پایان ندارد ای پدر این سخن را ترک کن پایان نگر
english_translation: This discourse hath no end O father leave this discourse and consider the end

persian_text: اوت گوید ریش و سبلت بر مخند آینه و میزان و آن گه ریو و پند
english_translation: They will say to thee Do not laugh at thy beard and moustache mirror and balance and then deceit and advice

persian_text: مر علی را در مثالی شیر خواند شیر مثل او نباشد گرچه راند
english_translation: He once compared Alí to a lion the lion is not like him though he used

persian_text: بر امید وعدهی هاتف که گنج یابد اندر مصر بهر دفع رنج
english_translation: in hope of the promise given by the heavenly Voice that he would find in Cairo the treasure to remove his trouble

persian_text: لا شدی پهلوی الا خانهگیر این عجب که هم اسیری هم امیر
english_translation: Thou hast become not take up thy abode beside except This is wonderful that thou art both a prisoner and a prince

persian_text: آن یکی اندر بلا ایوبوار وان دگر در صابری یعقوبوار
english_translation: One is like Job in tribulation another like Jacob in patience

persian_text: ما همه کوران اصلی بودهایم از تو ما صد گون عجایب دیدهایم
english_translation: We all have been blind from birth we have seen from thee marvels of a hundred kinds

persian_text: این مگیر از فرع این از اصل گیر بر کمان کم زن که از بازوست تیر
english_translation: Do not regard this from the branch  regard it from the root  do not strike at the bow for the arrow is from the arm

persian_text: تا مثنا بشنوم من نام تو عاشقم برنام جان آرام تو
english_translation: So that I may hear Thy name redoubled I am in love with Thy soulsoothing name

persian_text: مثنوی را مسرح مشروح ده صورت امثال او را روح ده
english_translation: Give the Mathnawí a free and open course endow the outward form of its parables with the spirit

persian_text: تا کشد بیاختیاری صید را تا برد بگرفته گوش او زید را
english_translation: So that he drags the prey devoid of will so that having seized Zayd by the ear he leads him away

persian_text: تیر قهر خویش بر پرش زنم پر و بال مردریگش بر کنم
english_translation: I will shoot the arrow of my vengeance at his wings I will tear out his goodfornothing wings and feathers

persian_text: گفت پیغامبر که معراج مرا نیست بر معراج یونس اجتبا
english_translation: The Prophet said No preference is to my ascension as being superior to the ascension of Yúnus

persian_text: روز شد گفتش کهای عمران برو واقف آن غلغل و آن بانگ شو
english_translation: Day broke he said to him  O Imrán go inform thyself concerning that uproar and noise

persian_text: یا چو بازانند و دیده دوخته در حجاب از عشق صیدی سوخته
english_translation: Or they resemble falcons with eyes sealed  in the veil consumed with passion for a prey

persian_text: حیله و افسونگری کار منست کار من دستان و از ره بردنست
english_translation: Cunning and enchantment is my business it is my business to beguile and lead astray

persian_text: معجزه بیند فروزد آن زمان بعد از آن گوید خیالی بود آن
english_translation: It sees a miracle  and at the moment it glows  afterwards it says Twas a phantasy

persian_text: از گزافه کی شدند این قوم لنگ فخر را دادند و بخریدند ننگ
english_translation: How did this party become lame and give up glory and purchase ignominy in vain

persian_text: گر به مظروفش نظر داری شهی ور به ظرفش بنگری تو گمرهی
english_translation: If you keep your eye fixed on its contents you are a king but if you regard its vessel you are misguided

persian_text: جمله اهل محکمه گفتند ما هم بر ادبار و بر افلاسش گوا
english_translation: All the people belonging to the court of justice said We bear witness both to his degeneracy and his insolvency

persian_text: دیدهی این هفت رنگ جسمها در نیابد زین نقاب آن روح را
english_translation: The eye that sees these seven bodily colours cannot perceive the spirit because of this veil

persian_text: من شکستم عهد و دانستم بدست تا رسید آن شومی جرات بدست
english_translation: I broke my covenant and knew twas evil  so that that illomened audacity reached my hand

persian_text: گرچه خواجه بس سخاوت کرده بود هیچ آن کفو عطای تو نبود
english_translation: Though the Khwája had shown great generosity that was never a match for Thy bounty

persian_text: پس نکو گفت آن رسول خوشجواز ذرهای عقلت به از صوم و نماز
english_translation: Excellently well said the complaisant Prophet A mote of intelligence is better for thee than fasting and performing the ritual prayer

persian_text: قصهی درویشی کی از آن خانه هرچه میخواست میگفت نیست
english_translation: Story of the dervish to whom whenever he begged anything from a certain house he used to say It is not

persian_text: قوس ابرو تیر غمزه دام کید بهر چه دادت خدا از بهر صید
english_translation: Wherefore has God given thee the bow of thine eyebrow the arrow of thy amorous glance and the snare of thy craftiness For hunting

persian_text: عرش معدن گاه داد و معدلت چار جو در زیر او پر مغفرت
english_translation: The Throne is the mine of justice and equity beneath it are four rivers filled with forgiveness

persian_text: نک در افتادیم در خندق همه کشته و خستهی بلا بی ملحمه
english_translation: Lo we all are fallen into the moat killed and wounded by affliction without combat

persian_text: گفت این مرد این طرف چونست عور در بیابان از ره و از شهر دور
english_translation: said  How is this man destitute here in the wilderness far from road and town

persian_text: ما خلقت الجن و الانس این بخوان جز عبادت نیست مقصود از جهان
english_translation: I did not create the Jinn and mankind  Recite this  The object of the world is naught but Divine worship

persian_text: حس تو از حس خر کمتر بده ست که دل تو زین وحلها بر نجست
english_translation: Your sense has been less than the sense of the ass for your heart has not recoiled from these clods of mud

persian_text: این همه کردیم و دولت تیره شد دشمن شه هست گشت و چیره شد
english_translation: We have done all this and Fortune is darkened the Kings enemy has come into being and has prevailed

persian_text: ز انکه غالب عقل بود و خر ضعیف از سوار زفت گردد خر نحیف
english_translation: Because intellect was ruling and the ass weak the ass is made lean by a strong rider

persian_text: موسی و فرعون در هستی تست باید این دو خصم را در خویش جست
english_translation: Moses and Pharaoh are in thy being thou must seek these two adversaries in thyself

persian_text: ای سلیمان مسجد اقصی بساز لشکر بلقیس آمد در نماز
english_translation: O Solomon build the Farther Mosque the army of Bilqís has come into the prayer

persian_text: صد هزاران نام و او یک آدمی صاحب هر وصفش از وصفی عمی
english_translation: hundreds of thousands of names he is one man the owner of every quality belonging to him is blind to giving any description

persian_text: ز آن مناسب آمدن افعال دست فهم آید مر ترا که عقل هست
english_translation: From manual actions being conformable you may perceive that there is intellect

persian_text: چینیان گفتند ما نقاشتر رومیان گفتند ما را کر و فر
english_translation: The Chinese said We are the better artists the Greeks said The power and excellence belongs to us

persian_text: اهل و فرزندان سفر را ساختند رخت را بر گاو عزم انداختند
english_translation: His kinsfolk and children made ready for the journey and threw the baggage upon the ox of departure

persian_text: عاشقان لعبتان پر قذر کرده قصد خون و جان همدگر
english_translation: The lovers of filthy dolls have sought each others blood and life

persian_text: ور نه بهتر را سجود دونتری امر کردن هیچ نبود در خوری
english_translation: Else  it would not be at all a suitable thing to command the superior to worship an inferior

persian_text: همچنان که شه سلیمان در نبرد جذب خیل و لشکر بلقیس کرد
english_translation: Even as in warfare King Solomon drew the cavalry and footsoldiers of Bilqís

persian_text: جنبش این جزو بادای ساده مرد بیتو و بیبادبیزن سر نکرد
english_translation: Without you and without the fan the movement of this portion of wind does not arise O simpleton

persian_text: آن غلامان و کنیزان بناز پیش چشمش همچو پوسیده پیاز
english_translation: Those charming pages and handmaidens to her eye as a rotten onion

persian_text: ور نداری کار نیکوتر به دست پس پشیمانیت بر فوت چه است
english_translation: And if thou hast no better work in hand then for the omission of what is thy repentance

persian_text: نور او را زان زیانی نابده وان حدث از خشکیی هیزم شده
english_translation: Its light suffers no loss thereby and the ordure is made dry and fuel

persian_text: چون که شب آن رنگها مستور بود پس بدیدی دید رنگ از نور بود
english_translation: Inasmuch as at night those colours were hidden thou sawest that thy vision of the colour was from the light

persian_text: کودکان خرد در کتابها و آن امامان جمله در محرابها
english_translation: The little children at school and all the Imáms in the mosques

persian_text: صد چنین سگ اندرین تن خفتهاند چون شکاری نیستشان بنهفتهاند
english_translation: In this body a hundred such dogs are sleeping when they have no prey  they are hidden

persian_text: ذرهای بالا و آن دیگر نگون جنگ فعلیشان ببین اندر رکون
english_translation: One mote up and another down in their inclination behold actual strife

persian_text: نور بی این چشم میبیند به خواب چشم بیاین نور چه بود جز خراب
english_translation: The light sees in dream without this eye without this light what would the eye be but ruined

persian_text: لوتش آورد و حکایتهاش گفت کز امید اندر دلش صد گل شکفت
english_translation: He fetched viands for him and told him stories so that from the hope a hundred roses blossomed in his heart

persian_text: حوت اگرچه کشتی غی بشکند دوست را چون ثور کشتی میکند
english_translation: Though the Fish wreck the ship of error for the friend it is sowing like the bull

persian_text: پس بد مطلق نباشد در جهان بد به نسبت باشد این را هم بدان
english_translation: Hence there is no absolute evil in the world evil is relative Know this also

persian_text: خشک گردد موش زان گربهی عیارگر بود اعداد موشان صد هزار
english_translation: The mouse is paralysed by the wily cat if the numbers of the mice amount to a hundred thousand

persian_text: چون شود از رنج و علت دل سلیم طعم کذب و راست را باشد علیم
english_translation: When the heart becomes whole of pain and disease it will recognize the flavour of falsehood and truth

persian_text: گفت اگر رویش به شهر و دم به ده روی او از دم او میدان که به
english_translation: He replied If its face is to the town and its tail to the country know that its face is better than its tail

persian_text: زد ستارهی آن پیمبر بر سما ما ستارهبار گشتیم از بکا
english_translation: The star of that prophet shot up in the sky we from weeping began to shed stars

persian_text: این به اصل خویش ماند پرخلل وان به اصل خود که علمست و عمل
english_translation: This resembles its foundation full of defect and that its foundation which is knowledge and action

persian_text: نگذری زین بعد سیصد ساله تو تا که داری عشق آن گوساله تو
english_translation: Thou wilt never traverse this three hundred years distance so long as thou hast love for the calf

persian_text: چون عمر شیدای آن معشوق شد حق و باطل را چو دل فاروق شد
english_translation: When Umar became distraught with that Beloved he became a Fárúq  like the heart between truth and falsehood

persian_text: چون بدید آن چشمهای پرخمار که کند عقل و خرد را بیقرار
english_translation: When he saw those languorous eyes which make the reason and understanding unquiet

persian_text: جیفة اللیلست و بطال النهار هر که او شد غرهی این طبلخوار
english_translation: Any one who is duped by this parasite is a carcase by night and a goodfornothing by day

persian_text: کردهای مبدل تو میرا از حدث جان ما را هم بدل کن از خبث
english_translation: Thou hast changed the wine from filth change our souls also from defilement

persian_text: جو یکی کوچک که دایم میرود نه نجس گردد نه گنده میشود
english_translation: A little rivulet which is moving continually does not become defiled or grow fetid

persian_text: سوی لطف بیوفایان هین مرو کان پل ویران بود نیکو شنو
english_translation: Beware do not go towards the courtesy of the faithless for it is a ruined bridge heed well

persian_text: سر گبر کور نامذکور به دود دوزخ از ارم مهجور به
english_translation: Tis better that the secret of the blind infidel should be untold tis better that the smoke of Hell should be banished from Iram

persian_text: خشم تو تخم سعیر دوزخست هین بکش این دوزخت را کین فخست
english_translation: Your anger is the seed of Hellfire take heed extinguish this Hell of yours for this is a trap

persian_text: خویش را موزون و چست و سخته کن ز آبدیده نان خود را پخته کن
english_translation: Make yourself harmonious and congruous and balanced let your bread be baked well with tears

persian_text: عشق را پانصد پرست و هر پری از فراز عرش تا تحتالثری
english_translation: Love hath five hundred wings and every wing from above the empyrean to beneath the earth

persian_text: کی شود کشف از تفکر این انا آن انا مکشوف شد بعد از فنا
english_translation: How should this I be revealed by thinking That I is revealed after passing away from self

persian_text: معجزه بحرست و ناقص مرغ خاک مرغ آبی در وی آمن از هلاک
english_translation: The miracle is the sea and the deficient man is the landbird  the waterbird is safe from destruction there

persian_text: جسم جسمانه تواند دیدنت در خیال آرد غم و خندیدنت
english_translation: The body can see Thee in bodily fashion it fancies Thy sadness or laughter

persian_text: چینه و نقلش همه بر بام تست پر زنان بر اوج مست دام تست
english_translation: All its grain and food is on thy roof flying in the zenith it is intoxicated with thy snare

persian_text: لعل و یاقوتست بهر وام او در خنوری و نبشته نام او
english_translation: Rubies and corundums for his debt are in a certain vessel on which his name is written

persian_text: تنگ بالا بهر جسمآرای را زیر واسع تا نگیرد پای را
english_translation: Tight above to show off my body  wide below so as not to hamper my legs

persian_text: از میان مشت او هر پارهسنگ در شهادت گفتن آمد بیدرنگ
english_translation: Without delay from the middle of his fist every pebble began to pronounce the profession of faith

persian_text: هر دم آسیبست بر ادراک تو نبت نو نو رسته بین از خاک تو
english_translation: At every moment the touch is upon thy apprehension behold the plants ever springing up anew from thy earth

persian_text: لا تخف دان چونک خوفت داد حق نان فرستد چون فرستادت طبق
english_translation: Know that it is Be not afraid when God hath given you the fear  He will send the bread since He hath sent the tray to you

persian_text: کی کنم من از معیت فهم راز جز که از بعد سفرهای دراز
english_translation: How should I apprehend the mystery of His being with me except after long journeys

persian_text: رو سپس کردم بدان محض کرم سوی فعل خویشتن میننگرم
english_translation: I turn my face back to that pure grace I am not looking towards my own actions

persian_text: از دگر انجم بجز نقشی نیافت این جنین تا آفتابش بر نتافت
english_translation: From the other stars this embryo received only an impression until the sun shone upon it

persian_text: کاو دغا و مفلس است و بد سخن هیچ با او شرکت و سودا مکن
english_translation: Saying He is a swindler and insolvent and liar do not make any partnership or do any trade with him

persian_text: تا ز روز و شب گذر کردم چنانکه از اسپر بگذرد نوک سنان
english_translation: So that I passed through day and night as the point of the spear passes through the shield

persian_text: آه و زاری پیش تو بس قدر داشت من نتانستم حقوق آن گذاشت
english_translation: Moaning and wailing had great value with Thee I could not leave their rights unheeded

persian_text: کز زنای چشم حظی میبری نه کباب از پهلوی خود میخوری
english_translation: If you are deriving some pleasure from the eyes cupidity are not you eating roastmeat from your own side

persian_text: آینهی دل صاف باید تا در او واشناسی صورت زشت از نکو
english_translation: The mirror of the heart must be clear in order that you may know therein the ugly form from the beautiful

persian_text: تا یقین گرددمرا ایقان تو و اعتقاد خوب با برهان تو
english_translation: So that thy sure faith and thy goodly proven conviction may become evident to me

persian_text: زاهدی بد در میان بادیه در عبادت غرق چون عبادیه
english_translation: Amidst the desert lived an ascetic absorbed in devotion like the people of Abbádán

persian_text: چیست امعان چشمه را کردن روان چون ز تن جان رست گویندش روان
english_translation: What is imán causing the spring to flow when the spirit has escaped from the body they call it rawán

persian_text: در جزای آن عطای نور پاک تو زدی در دیدهی من خار و خاک
english_translation: And in requital for that gift of pure light thou hast thrown thorns and earth in mine eye

persian_text: ملکت حسنش سوی زندان کشید ملکت علمش سوی کیوان کشید
english_translation: The kingdom of beauty led him to prison the kingdom of knowledge led him to Saturn

persian_text: انصتوا یعنی که آبت را بلاغ هین تلف کم کن که لبخشکست باغ
english_translation: Be ye silent that is do not waste your water in idle talk for the orchard is drylipped

persian_text: علم الاسما بد آدم را امام لیک نه اندر لباس عین و لام
english_translation: He taught the names was an Imám for Adam but not in the garb of ayn and lám

persian_text: لیک بی هیچ آلتی صنع صمد اختیارش را کمند او کند
english_translation: But the action of the Lord  without any instrument makes his freewill a noose for him

persian_text: در ببست از حسن او پیش بصر آن سخن بد در میان چون بانگ در
english_translation: He shut the door on its beauty to the eye  those words intervened like the sound of the door

persian_text: میدهد رنگ احسن التقویم را تا به اسفل میبرد این نیم را
english_translation: Is giving colour to those who are most excellent in their constitution it is bearing this half down to the lowest depth

persian_text: تا نگرید کودک حلوا فروش بحر رحمت در نمیآید به جوش
english_translation: Until the halwáselling boy weeps the sea of mercy is not aroused

persian_text: گفت ستارم نگویم رازهاش جز یکی رمز از برای ابتلاش
english_translation: He said I am the Coverer  I will not tell his secrets but one indication for the sake of trying him

persian_text: راستگو تا وارهی از چنگ من مکر ننشاند غبار جنگ من
english_translation: Tell the truth so that you may be delivered from my hand cunning will not lay the dust of my war

persian_text: گمان بردن کاروانیان که بهمیهای صوفی رنجور است
english_translation: How the people of the caravan supposed the Sufis beast was ill

persian_text: عشق بینایان بود بر کان زر لاجرم هر روز باشد بیشتر
english_translation: the love of them that have insight is on the goldmine necessarily it is greater every day

persian_text: آنک بستد پیرهن را میشتافت بوی پیراهان یوسف مینیافت
english_translation: He that took the shirt was hastening and was not perceiving the scent of Josephs shirt

persian_text: نیست دستوری بدینجا قرع باب جز امید الله اعلم بالصواب
english_translation: Here it is not permitted to knock at the door  naught but hope  God best knoweth the right course

persian_text: همچو فرعونی تو کور و کوردل با عدو خوش بیگناهان را مذل
english_translation: You are like Pharaoh blind and blindhearted complaisant to your enemy and treating the guiltless with ignominy

persian_text: بیان این خبر کی کلموا الناس علی قدر عقولهم
english_translation: Explanation of the Tradition Speak ye unto men according to the measure of their understandings

persian_text: سرنگون خود را از اشتر در فکند گفت سوزیدم ز غم تا چندچند
english_translation: He threw himself headlong from the camel He said I am consumed with grief how long how long

persian_text: من ترا اندر دو عالم حافظم طاعنان را از حدیثت رافضم
english_translation: I am exalting thee in both worlds I am driving away the scoffers from thy Tidings

persian_text: از حدیث اولیا نرم و درشت تن مپوشان ز آنکه دینت راست پشت
english_translation: Against the sayings of the saints whether soft or rough do not thou cover thy body for they are the support of thy religion

persian_text: جان جان تن حیات دل بود پس ز دادش داد تو فاضل بود
english_translation: The life of the heart is the soul of the soul of the body therefore thy gift is superior to his

persian_text: بیان آنک در توبه بازست
english_translation: Explaining that the door of repentance is open

persian_text: رقعهگر بر پر مرغی دوختی پر مرغ از تف رقعه سوختی
english_translation: If he sewed the letter on the wing of a bird the birds wing would be burnt by the ardour of the letter

persian_text: گر ز تو راضیست دل من راضیم ور ز تو معرض بود اعراضیم
english_translation: If the Heart be pleased with thee I am pleased and if it be averse to thee I am averse

persian_text: چون موکل نیست بر تو هیچکس از چه بسته گشت بر تو پیش و پس
english_translation: Since no one is a custodian over thee wherefore have the future and the past become sealed to thee

persian_text: گوید ای بنده تو رو بر صدر شین من بگیرم کفش چون بندهی کهین
english_translation: O slave says he go you and sit in the place of honour I will take shoes like the meanest slave

persian_text: گفت روزی کشتیی بر موج تیز من شکستم ز امر تا شد ریز ریز
english_translation: One day said he by command I wrecked a ship on the fierce waves so that it went to pieces

persian_text: ای بسا سرمست نار و نارجو خویشتن را نور مطلق داند او
english_translation: Oh many a one intoxicated with fire and seeking fire deems himself absolute light

persian_text: دانش آن بود موقوف سفر ناید آن دانش به تیزی فکر
english_translation: the knowledge thereof depended on journeying that knowledge is not to be gained by keenness of thought

persian_text: انبیا چون جنس روحند و ملک مر ملک را جذب کردند از فلک
english_translation: Since the prophets are homogeneous with spirit and angel they drew angels from heaven

persian_text: که لو انزلنا کتابا للجبل لانصدع ثم انقطع ثم ارتحل
english_translation: For if We had sent down a Book to the mountain it would have been riven asunder then cut to pieces and then it would have departed

persian_text: دارم ایمان که آن ز جمله برترست بس لطیف و با فروغ و با فرست
english_translation: I hold the faith that that is higher than all  tis very beautiful resplendent and glorious

persian_text: عقل توگر اژدهایی گشت مست یار بد او را زمرد دان که هست
english_translation: if thy reason has become a furious dragon know that the evil companion is an emerald to it

persian_text: حمله بردند اسپه جسمانیان جانب قلعه و دز روحانیان
english_translation: The army of the corporeal ones attacked in the direction of the fortress and stronghold of the spiritual ones

persian_text: چون شما سوی جمادی میروید محرم جان جمادان چون شوید
english_translation: Forasmuch as ye are going towards inanimateness  how shall ye become familiar with the spiritual life of inanimate beings

persian_text: آن به خاک اندر شد و کل خاک شد وین نمک اندر شد و کل پاک شد
english_translation: That one went into the earth and became earth entirely this went into the salt and became entirely pure

persian_text: در سمر میخواند دزدینامهای گرد او جمع آمده هنگامهای
english_translation: And during the nighttalk he would read aloud a book on tailors when a throng had gathered round him

persian_text: گشت پران جانب خانه شتافت خواجه را در خانه در خلوت بیافت
english_translation: She flew off and hastened towards the house she found the master at home and alone

persian_text: کم کسی داند مگر ربانیی کش بود در دل محک جانیی
english_translation: No one knows except the divine man in whose heart is a spiritual touchstone

persian_text: شب همه شب میسگالیدند مکر روی در رو کرده چندین عمرو و بکر
english_translation: During the night the whole night they were devising a plot many a Amr and Bakr had put their faces together

persian_text: پس تبسم کرد و گفت اکنون بیا لیک مخفی دار این راای کیا
english_translation: Then he smiled and said Now come in but keep this secret O noble sir

persian_text: حق آنکه چرخهی چرخ ترا کرد گردان بر فراز این سرا
english_translation: By the truth of Him who hath set the wheel of thy sphere turning above this abode

persian_text: یا مسیحی که به تعلیم ودود در ولادت ناطق آمد در وجود
english_translation: Or the Messiah who through being taught by the Loving  at his birth came speaking into the world

persian_text: گفت چون طفلی به پیش والده وقت قهرش دست هم در وی زده
english_translation: He said Thou art like a child in the presence of its mother when she chastises it it still lays hold of her

persian_text: خواجه چون دیدش فتاده همچنین بر جهید و زد کله را بر زمین
english_translation: The merchant seeing her thus fallen sprang up and dashed his cap on the ground

persian_text: ما بیاموزیم این سحرای فلان از برای ابتلا و امتحان
english_translation: We teach this magic O such and such for the purpose of trial and probation

persian_text: زین تگ جو ماه گوید من مهم من نه عکسم همحدیث و همرهم
english_translation: From the bottom of this River the Moon cries I am the Moon I am not a reflexion I am conversing and travelling with

persian_text: ای نخود میجوش اندر ابتلا تا نه هستی و نه خود ماند ترا
english_translation: Continue O chickpea to boil in tribulation that neither existence nor self may remain to thee

persian_text: لیک نی چندانک ناسوری شود زهر جان و عقل رنجوری شود
english_translation: But not so great that it becomes an incurable sore and a poison to the spirit and intellect of one who is sick

persian_text: قوت ایمانی در این زندان کم است وان که هست از قصد این سگ در خم است
english_translation: In this prison the food of faith is scarce and that which exists is in the noose through the attack of this cur

persian_text: بازگشتن آن شخص شادمان و مراد یافته و خدای را شکر گویان و سجدهکنان
english_translation: How that person returned rejoicing and successful and giving thanks to God and prostrating himself

persian_text: جملهی مرغان آب آن روز نحر همچو کشتیها روان بر روی بحر
english_translation: On that Day of Slaughter all the waterbirds sailing along like ships on the surface of the Sea

persian_text: این طمع را ماند و رحمت بود کو طمع آن جا که آن نعمت بود
english_translation: This resembles cupidity and it is a mercy where that blessing is where is cupidity

persian_text: آن شده آواز صافی و حزینای خدا وی مستغاث وای معین
english_translation: the voice become pure and sad O God and O Thou whose help is besought and O Helper

persian_text: خلوت و چله برو لازم نماند هیچ غیمی مر ورا غایم نماند
english_translation: The religious seclusion and the forty days fast are no longer incumbent on him no cloud is overclouding him any more

persian_text: طبع گاوی از سرت بیرون کند خوی حیوانی ز حیوان بر کند
english_translation: He will expel the bovine nature from your head he will uproot animality from the animal

persian_text: گفت شاهی شیخ را اندر سخن چیزی از بخشش ز من درخواست کن
