english_translation: A certain king said to the Shaykh in conversation Ask me to bestow some bounty upon thee

persian_text: چون برید از جنس با شه گشت یار بر گشاید چشم او را بازدار
english_translation: When it has been severed from its kind it associates with the king the falconer unveils its eye

persian_text: این صدا جان مرا تغییر کرد از غم و اندوه تلخم پیر کرد
english_translation: This sound hath marred my soul and aged me with bitter pain and grief

persian_text: این جهان و ساکنانش منتشر وان جهان و سالکانش مستمر
english_translation: This world and its inhabitants are dispersed that world and its travellers are continuing

persian_text: صرصری میبرد بر سر تخت شاه هر صباح و هر مسا یک ماهه راه
english_translation: A roaring wind carried on its head the throne of the king a months journey every morn and eve

persian_text: خواست مردن قالبش بیکار شد عمر مانده بود شه بیدار شد
english_translation: He was about to die his body became inert his life had been left  the king awoke

persian_text: دیو که بود کاو ز آدم بگذرد بر چنین نطعی از او بازی برد
english_translation: Who is the Devil that he should surpass Adam and win the game from him on such a board

persian_text: تو جهود از امر موسی سر کشیگر بخواند در خوشی یا ناخوشی
english_translation: Will you Jew rebel against the command of Moses if he summon you in a fair cause or a foul

persian_text: سوی ساحل میفشاند بیخطر جوش موجش هر زمانی صد گهر
english_translation: At every moment the surge of its waves is scattering shorewards a hundred pearls without danger

persian_text: هم بر آن در باشدش باش و قرار کفر دارد کرد غیری اختیار
english_translation: Still will he stay and abide at that door he will deem it ingratitude to prefer another

persian_text: چند حرف طمطراق و کار و بار کار و حال خود ببین و شرم دار
english_translation: How long pompous and artificial phrases Look at thine own acts and feelings and be ashamed

persian_text: پس ز من زایید در معنی پدر پس ز میوه زاد در معنی شجر
english_translation: Therefore in reality the Father was born of me therefore in reality the tree was born of the fruit

persian_text: افتادن شغال در خم رنگ و رنگین شدن و دعوی طاوسی کردن میان شغالان
english_translation: How the jackal fell into the dyeingvat and was dyed with many colours and pretended amongst the jackals that he was a peacock

persian_text: که نمیماند به ما گرچه ز ماست ما همه مسیم و احمد کیمیاست
english_translation: For he doth not resemble us though he is of us we all are the copper while Ahmad is the Elixir

persian_text: باز شب اندر تب افتد از فزع تا شود لاغر ز خوف منتجع
english_translation: Then again at night she by panic falls into a fever  so that from fear of seeking for fodder she becomes lean

persian_text: گه به درویشی کنم تهدیدشان گه به زلف و خال بندم دیدشان
english_translation: sometimes I may threaten them with poverty sometimes bind their eyes with tress and mole

persian_text: نوح اندر بادیه کشتی بساخت صد مثلگو از پی تسخیر بتاخت
english_translation: Noah built an Ark in the desert  a hundred speakers of parables ran up to ridicule

persian_text: ما نمیخواهیم این ایوان و باغ نه زنان خوب و نه امن و فراغ
english_translation: We do not desire these palaces and orchards nor fair women nor that safety and ease

persian_text: این کرم چون دفع آن انکار تست که میان خاک میکردی نخست
english_translation: Inasmuch as this bounty is the rebutting the denial which thou didst make in the beginning amidst the clay

persian_text: خر کمیز خر ببوید بر طریق مشک چون عرضه کنم با این فریق
english_translation: The donkey smells donkeys urine on the road how should I offer musk to this class

persian_text: بر صطرلابش نقوش عنکبوت بهر اوصاف ازل دارد ثبوت
english_translation: The figures on the spider on his astrolabe are there for the sake of the Eternal Attributes

persian_text: همچنان چون شاه فرمود اصبروا رغبتی باید کزان تابی تو رو
english_translation: Similarly since the King has given the command Refrain yourselves there must be some desire from which thou shouldst avert thy face

persian_text: خوشدلم در باطن از حکم زبر گرچه شد رویم ترش کالحق مر
english_translation: I am inwardly pleased with the decision in the Scrolls though my face has become sourfor Truth is bitter

persian_text: ور سوی شهرست دم رویش به ده خاک آن دم باش و از رویش بجه
english_translation: But if its tail is towards the town and its face to the country be the dust on that tail and spring away from its face

persian_text: پس خروسش گفت تن زن غم مخور که خدا بدهد عوض زینت دگر
english_translation: Then the cock said to him Be silent do not grieve for God will give you something else instead of this

persian_text: زان رحم بیرون شدن بر تو درشت میگریزی از زهارش سوی پشت
english_translation: Tis grievous to you to go forth from the womb you are fleeing from her pubes towards her back

persian_text: گفت با اینها مرا صد حجت است لیک جمعاند و جماعت قوت است
english_translation: He said I have a hundred arguments against these fellows but they are united and a united party is strength

persian_text: گوشمال محنت بیزینهار داغها بر داغها چندین هزار
english_translation: The chastisement of pitiless tribulation brands on brands so many thousands

persian_text: هین مقابل شو تو و خصم و بگو پاسخ خصم و بکن دفع عدو
english_translation: Hark come face to face with thy adversary and reply to thy adversary and rebut thy opponent

persian_text: آب و آتش آمد ای جان امتحان نقد و قلبی را که آن باشد نهان
english_translation: Water and fire O soul are the test for the pure and the base coin that is hidden

persian_text: ننگرند اندر تب و قولنج و سل راه ندهند این سببها را به دل
english_translation: They do not look at fever and dysentery and consumption they do not admit these causes into their heart

persian_text: من روم سوی قناعت دل قوی تو چرا سوی شناعت میروی
english_translation: I march with stout heart towards contentment why art thou betaking thyself to revilement

persian_text: مینبخشایی تو بر آتشپرست آنکه نپرستد ترا او چون برست
english_translation: Thou hast no pity on the fireworshipper how has he been saved who does not worship thee

persian_text: آتشت را هیزم فرعون نیست ورنه چون فرعون او شعلهزنیست
english_translation: Thy fire hath not Pharaohs fuel otherwise it is one that throws out flames like Pharaoh

persian_text: در گذر از فضل و از جهدی و فن کار خدمت دارد و خلق حسن
english_translation: Abandon eminence and energy and skill what matters is service and a goodly disposition

persian_text: اندکی گفتیم زان بحثای عتل ز اندکی پیدا بود قانون کل
english_translation: We have told a little of that disputation O fierce debater from a little the principle of the whole is evident

persian_text: من ز گفت هر دو حیران گشتمی در دل افتادی مرا بیم و غمی
english_translation: I used to be dismayed by the talk of both a terror and pain would come into my heart

persian_text: صبح کاذب کاروانها را زدست که به بوی روز بیرون آمدست
english_translation: The false dawn has waylaid caravans which have set out in hope of the daybreak

persian_text: پیش خلق ایشان فراز صد کهاند گام خود بر چرخ هفتم مینهند
english_translation: In the sight of the people they are higher than a hundred mountains and plant their footsteps on the Seventh Heaven

persian_text: گفت خلقان چون ببینند آسمان من ببینم عرش را با عرشیان
english_translation: Zayd said When people see the sky I behold the Empyrean with those who dwell there

persian_text: مصطفی مه میشکافد نیم شب ژاژ میخاید ز کینه بو لهب
english_translation: Mustafá splits the moon at midnight Abú Lahab from hatred talks drivel

persian_text: تا ز رنج آن جهانی وارهد بر خود این رنج عبادت مینهد
english_translation: And in order that he may be delivered from the pain of that world lays upon himself this pain of serving God

persian_text: بحر جان و جان بحر ار گویمش نیست لایق نام نو میجویمش
english_translation: Tis not fitting if I call it the Sea of the Soul and the Soul of the Sea I am seeking a new name for it

persian_text: سنگها در آستین بودش شتاب خرد کردش پیش او بود آن صواب
english_translation: He had stones in his sleeve he quickly reduced it to dust that seemed to him the right course

persian_text: گر زیادت میشود زین رو بود نه از برای بوش وهای و هو بود
english_translation: If increase is accruing  it is for this reason it is not for the sake of vainglory and noise

persian_text: فرصت آن پشه راندن هم نبود از نهیب حملهی گرگ عنود
english_translation: There was no opportunity even of driving away those gnats because of dread of an attack by the contumacious wolf

persian_text: وارثی نا بوده یک خوی تراای فلک سجدهکنان کوی ترا
english_translation: There exists no heir to one disposition of thine O thou to whose abode Heaven is bowing in worship

persian_text: در شب مهتاب مه را بر سماک از سگان و عوعو ایشان چه باک
english_translation: On a moonlight night what cares the moon in Simák for dogs and their barking

persian_text: اقتدا کردند آن شاهان قطار در پی آن مقتدای نامدار
english_translation: Those kings followed his leadership in a row behind that renowned exemplar

persian_text: در نگر در صنعت پارهزنی کو همیدوزد کهن بی سوزنی
english_translation: Consider the art of a Tailor who sews old rags without a needle

persian_text: ای بسا ناورده استثنا به گفت جان او با جان استثناست جفت
english_translation: How many a one has not pronounced the saving clause and yet his soul is in harmony with the soul of it

persian_text: فربهش کن آنگهش کشای قصاب زانک بیبرگاند در دوزخ کلاب
english_translation: Fatten him then kill him O Butcher for the dogs in Hell are without food

persian_text: هست بیرون قطرهی خرد و بزرگ در صدف آن در خرد است و سترگ
english_translation: Outside it is a drop of water small or great within the shell it is a small or big pearl

persian_text: خاصه هر شب جمله افکار و عقول نیست گردد غرق در بحر نغول
english_translation: In particular every night all thoughts and understandings become naught plunged in the deep Sea

persian_text: هین جواب خویش گو با کردگار ما کییم ای خواجه دست از ما بدار
english_translation: Hark answer for thyself to the Creator Who are we Sire keep thy hands off us

persian_text: همچو آن ابلیس و ذریات او با خدا در جنگ و اندر گفت و گو
english_translation: Like that Iblís and his progeny in battle and argument with God

persian_text: ناگهان در خانهاش گاوی دوید شاخ زد بشکست دربند و کلید
english_translation: Suddenly a cow ran into his house she butted with her horns and broke the bolt and key

persian_text: در خبر بشنو تو این پند نکو بیم جنبیکم لکم اعدی عدو
english_translation: Hearken to this good counsel in the Traditions Your worst enemy is between your two sides

persian_text: دیده منزلها ز اصل و از اساس چون قلاووز آن خبیر و رهشناس
english_translation: Having perceived the stages from the source and foundation possessed of experience and knowing the way like guides

persian_text: اصل و سرچشمهی خوشی آن است آن زود تجری تحتها الأنهار خوان
english_translation: That that is the source and fountainhead of joy quick recite  beneath which flow the rivers

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر به یک صاحب ریا صل إنک لم تصل یا فتی
english_translation: The Prophet said to a hypocrite Pray for indeed thou hast not prayed  my man

persian_text: دخلها و میوهها جمله ز غیب حق فرستادست بیتخمین و ریب
english_translation: Without surmise or doubt God hath sent all produce and fruits from the Unseen

persian_text: خم شکسته آب از او ناریخته صد درستی زین شکست انگیخته
english_translation: The jar is shattered the water is not spilled from it from this shattering have arisen a hundred soundnesses

persian_text: خاص خاص مخزن سلطان ویست بلک اکنون شاه را خود جان ویست
english_translation: He is the most privileged of the Sultans treasury nay he is now to the King life itself

persian_text: آفتاب از ذرهای شد وام خواه زهرهای از خمرهای شد جامخواه
english_translation: the sun sought to borrow from a mote a Zuhra begged for a cup from a small jar

persian_text: تا تو میبینی عزیزان را بشر دان که میراث بلیس است آن نظر
english_translation: So long as thou art regarding the holy as men know that that view is an inheritance from Iblís

persian_text: قوم معکوساند اندر مشتها خاکخوار و آب را کرده رها
english_translation: The folk are perverted in their appetites eating earth and have left the water

persian_text: اسپ سرکش را عرب شیطانش خواند نی ستوری را که در مرعی بماند
english_translation: The Arabs called a restive horse a devil  not the beast of burden that stayed in the pasture

persian_text: اختر ار با دیو همچون عقربست مشتری را او ولی الاقربست
english_translation: To the devil the stars are as a scorpion to the buyer it is the next of kin

persian_text: دیدههاشان را به سحر میدوختند تا چنین جوهر به خس بفروختند
english_translation: Their eyes have been sealed by a enchantment so that they have sold such a pearl for rubbish

persian_text: ای بسا کز وی نوازش دیدهایم در گلستان رضا گردیدهایم
english_translation: Oh many is the time I have received kindness from Him and walked in the rosegarden of approval

persian_text: یا چو چشمهی سوزنی راهم بدی که ز بیرونم رحم دیده شدی
english_translation: Or would there be for me a path as the eye of a needle so that the womb might become visible to me from outside

persian_text: جبر چه بود بستن اشکسته را یا بپیوستن رگی بگسسته را
english_translation: What is jabr To bind up a broken or tie a severed vein

persian_text: بد نماند چون اشارت کرد دوست کفر ایمان شد چون کفر از بهر اوست
english_translation: When the Beloved has signified  tis evil no more infidelity becomes faith when the infidelity is for His sake

persian_text: شهوت او را که دم آمد ز بنای مبدل شهوت عقبیش کن
english_translation: O changer make its lust which is the tail to be entirely lust for the world hereafter

persian_text: دل قوی دار و بکن حمله برو او بگرداند ز تو در حال رو
english_translation: Keep a stout heart and rush at it and immediately it will turn its face away from you

persian_text: یا که بود آن سعی چون سعی جهود یا جزا وابستهی میقات بود
english_translation: Either his labour has been like the labour of the Jews or the recompense has been connected with the appointed season

persian_text: هست در خانهی فلانی رو بجو نام خانه و نام او گفت آن عدو
english_translation: It is in soandsos house go and seek itthe enemy named the house and mentioned his name

persian_text: این سخن پایان ندارد هوش دار گوش سوی قصهی خرگوش دار
english_translation: This topic hath no end Give heed Attend to the story of the hare

persian_text: ای لعمرک مر ترا حق عمر خواند پس خلیفه کرد و بر کرسی نشاند
english_translation: O Laamruk God pronounced unto thee life then He made thee Vicegerent and seated thee on the throne

persian_text: لیک موسی را مقدم داشتند ساحران او را مکرم داشتند
english_translation: Yet gave Moses the precedencethe magicians held him in honour

persian_text: بر سری جغدانش بر سر میزنند پر و بال نازنینش میکنند
english_translation: To crown all the owls attack it and tear its lovely wingfeathers and plumes

persian_text: دام آدم خوشهی گندم شده تا وجودش خوشهی مردم شده
english_translation: The ear of wheat became a trap for Adam so that his existence became the wheatear of mankind

persian_text: هر که او عاقل بود از جان ماست روح او و ریح او ریحان ماست
english_translation: Whoso is intelligent he is our soul his breeze and wind is our sweet basil

persian_text: وان فضای خرق اسباب و علل هست ارض اللهای صدر اجل
english_translation: But the spacious realm where means and causes are torn to shreds is the earth of God O most honourable sire

persian_text: خرس چون فریاد کرد از اژدها شیر مردی کرد از جنگش جدا
english_translation: When the bear cried out for help against the dragon a valiant man removed it from the claws

persian_text: چون کساد هدیه آنجا شد پدید شرمساریشان همی واپس کشید
english_translation: When the worthlessness of the gift became apparent there  shamefacedness was drawing them back

persian_text: هرکه دوراندازتر او دورتر وز چنین گنجست او مهجورتر
english_translation: The farther one shoots the farther away and more separated is he from a treasure like this

persian_text: گرد برگشت و طلب کرد آن زمان تا بیابد ز آن نهان گشته نشان
english_translation: Then he went round and searched in order to find the trace of that one who had become hidden

persian_text: صد هزاران قرن پیشین را همین مستی هستی بزد ره زین کمین
english_translation: From this ambush this same intoxication of existence waylaid a hundred thousand generations of old

persian_text: مسندت من بودم از من تاختی بر سر منبر تو مسند ساختی
english_translation: I was thy support thou hast run away from me thou hast devised a place to lean against upon the pulpit

persian_text: سایه شاهان طلب هر دم شتاب تا شوی ز آن سایه بهتر ز آفتاب
english_translation: Hasten every moment to seek the shadow of kings that by means of that shadow you may become superior to the sun

persian_text: خسته از ره جملهی شب بیعلف گاه در جان کندن و گه در تلف
english_translation: Killed by the journey without fodder all the night long now at the last gasp and now perishing

persian_text: آنک تنها خوش رود اندر رصد با رفیقان بیگمان خوشتر رود
english_translation: Beyond doubt he who cheerfully goes alone into the customhouse will go more cheerfully with companions

persian_text: والدین و ملک را بگذاشتند راه معشوق نهان بر داشتند
english_translation: They left their parents and kingdom they took the way to the hidden beloved

persian_text: همچو سنگ منجنیقی آمدی آن سخن بر شیشه خانهی او زدی
english_translation: Those words would come like a stone shot by a mangonel and strike upon his glass house

persian_text: رب اوزعنی لشکر ما اری لا تعقب حسرة لی ان مضی
english_translation: O my Lord prompt me to give thanks for that which I see  do not let me feel any subsequent regret if it shall pass away

persian_text: بت پرستان چون که خو با بت کنند مانعان راه بت را دشمنند
english_translation: Since idolaters are devoted to the idol they are foes to them that prevent their access

persian_text: لیک از نور محمد من کنون نیستم این شهر فانی را زبون
english_translation: But now through the Light of Mohammed I am not subject to this city that is passing away

persian_text: یوم لا یخزی النبی راست دان نور یسعی بین ایدیهم بخوان
english_translation: Know aright on the Day when He will not put the Prophet to shame read light shall run before them

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر به آواز بلند با توکل زانوی اشتر ببند
english_translation: The Prophet said with a loud voice While trusting in God bind the knee of thy camel

persian_text: مراعات کردن زن شوهر را و استغفار کردن از گفتهی خویش
english_translation: How the wife paid regard to her husband and begged God to forgive her for what she had said

persian_text: تو ز ضعف خود مکن در من نگاه بر تو شب بر من همان شب چاشتگاه
english_translation: Do not regard me from your infirmity to you tis night to me that same night is morningtide

persian_text: پس جهاد اکبر آمد عصر دزد تا بگوید که چه دزدیده است مزد
english_translation: The Greater Jihád  then consists in squeezing the thief in order that he may tell what he has stolen and what he has carried off

persian_text: چون روا دارم که مثل این گهر که نیاید در بها گردد هدر
english_translation: How should I deem it allowable that a priceless pearl like this should go to waste

persian_text: عشق را صد ناز و استکبار هست عشق با صد ناز میآید به دست
english_translation: Love hath a hundred disdains and prides Love is gained by means of a hundred blandishments

persian_text: جنس مایی پیش ما اولیتری به که زان تن وا رهی و زانتری
english_translation: Thou art my congener thou art more suited with me tis better that thou shouldst escape from that body and that moisture

persian_text: گفت آن تاویل باشد یا قیاس در صریح امر کم جو التباس
english_translation: He replied That would be an interpretation or an inference do not seek to confuse the plain meaning of the command

persian_text: حکم خذها لا تخف دادت خدا تا به دستت اژدها گردد عصا
english_translation: God gave thee command saying Take it fear not so that the dragon may become a rod in thy hand

persian_text: نیست از عاشق کسی دیوانهتر عقل از سودای او کورست و کر
english_translation: None is more mad than the lover Reason is blind and deaf to his melancholia

persian_text: ای سلیمان در میان زاغ و باز حلم حق شو با همه مرغان بساز
english_translation: O Solomon  amidst the crows and falcons be thou the clemency of God sort with all the birds

persian_text: آن خیال نور میترساندش بر شب ظلمات میچفساندش
english_translation: That fancy of the light terrifies it and causes it to become attached to the night of gloom

persian_text: همچو اهل نفس و اهل آب و گل در جهان بنشسته با اصحاب دل
english_translation: like carnal earthlyminded people in the world when they sit with spiritual folk

persian_text: و آن کسان که تشنه بر گردش دوان گشتهاند این دم نمایم من عیان
english_translation: And those who have been made to run athirst round it I will show clearly at this moment

persian_text: ای علی که جمله عقل و دیدهای شمهای واگو از آن چه دیدهای
english_translation: O Alí thou who art all mind and eye relate a little of that which thou hast seen

persian_text: عاقلی گفتش که بگذارای فتی جوزها خود تشنگی آرد ترا
english_translation: A sensible person said to him Leave off O youth truly the walnuts will bring thirst to you

persian_text: گر بخواهد سوی محسوسات رفت ور بخواهد سوی ملبوسات رفت
english_translation: If it will they turn to sensuous things and if it will they turn to things clothed

persian_text: که شناسا کردشان علم اله چونک بره و میش وقت صبحگاه
english_translation: For the Divine Knowledge has made them cognisant  as the lamb and the ewe at the hour of dawn

persian_text: تو مراقب باش بر احوال خویش نوش بین در داد و بعد از ظلم نیش
english_translation: Keep a careful watch over your own behaviour observe that the honey is in justice and that after injustice comes the sting

persian_text: تا کشیدت اندر این انواع حال که نبودت در گمان و در خیال
english_translation: Until He drew you into these various states  which were not in your thought or fancy

persian_text: جسم احمد را تعلق بد بدآن این تغیر آن تن باشد بدان
english_translation: The body of Ahmad was liable to that  know that this corruption belongs to the body

persian_text: که ندیده بود دلقک را چنین که ازو خوشتر نبودش همنشین
english_translation: Because he had never seen Dalqak like this for there was no companion more agreeable to him than he

persian_text: ای تقاضاگر درون همچون جنین چون تقاضا میکنی اتمام این
english_translation: O Thou that makest demands within  like the embryo since Thou art making a demand make easy

persian_text: موبه موی هر سگی دندان شده وز برای حیله دم جنبان شده
english_translation: Every hair on every dog becomes a tooth though they wag their tails for the sake of gaining their object

persian_text: پس خزان او را بهار است و حیات یک نماید سنگ و یاقوت زکات
english_translation: Therefore autumn is its Spring and life the stone and the pure ruby appear one

persian_text: آنک در فتوی امام خلق بود گوی تقوی از فرشته میربود
english_translation: That one who in giving legal judgements was the Imám of the people and in piety bore away the ball from the angels

persian_text: در مجاعت پس تو احول دیدهای که یکی را صد هزاران دیدهای
english_translation: In hunger then you are seeing double for you have regarded a single one as a hundred thousand

persian_text: موسی و هارون چو طاووسان بدند پر جلوه بر سر و رویت زدند
english_translation: Moses and Aaron were as peacocks they flapped the wings of display upon thy head and face

persian_text: دلوها وابستهی چرخ بلند دلو او در اصبعین زورمند
english_translation: The buckets are attached to the lofty wheel  his bucket is in two Almighty fingers

persian_text: رفت میکائیل سوی رب دین خالی از مقصود دست و آستین
english_translation: Michael went to the Lord of the Judgement with hand and sleeve empty of the object of his quest

persian_text: عایشه روزی به پیغمبر بگفت یا رسولالله تو پیدا و نهفت
english_translation: One day Áisha said to the Prophet O Messenger of Allah openly and secretly

persian_text: آب چون انبهتر آید در گذر زو کند قشر صور زوتر گذر
english_translation: When the water begins to pass by in fuller volume the husks the ideas pass along it more quickly

persian_text: باز گرد از بحر و رو در خشک نه هم ز لعبت گو که کودکراست به
english_translation: turn back from the Sea and set thy face towards dry land talk only of the plaything for it is better for the child

persian_text: از سلیمان آن نفس چون نفخ صور مردگان را وا رهانید از قبور
english_translation: Like the blast of the trumpet that breath from Solomon delivered the dead from the tombs

persian_text: هر که پایانبینتر او مسعودتر جدتر او کارد که افزون دید بر
english_translation: The more one descries the end the more blessed is he the greater one sees the crop  the more zealously he sows

persian_text: آن غذای خاصگان دولت است خوردن آن بیگلو و آلت است
english_translation: That is the food of the chosen ones of the sovereignty the eating thereof is without throat or instrument

persian_text: پیشوای بد بود آن بز شتاب میبرد اصحاب را پیش قصاب
english_translation: That goat is a bad leader he is taking his followers quickly along to the butcher

persian_text: او نداند قدر هم کاسان بیافت کو بکد و رنج و کسبش کم شتاف
english_translation: Just because he got it easily he does not know its value for he never made haste to work and toil and earn it

persian_text: نه از طریق گوش بل از وحی هوش رازها بد پیش او بی رویپوش
english_translation: Not by way of the ear but by inspiration from Reason to him mysteries were unveiled

persian_text: همچو جوی است او نه او آبی خورد آب از او بر آب خواران بگذرد
english_translation: He is like a riverbed it does not drink any water the water passes through it to the waterdrinkers

persian_text: تن چو اصطرلاب باشد ز احتساب آیتی از روح همچون آفتاب
english_translation: The body is like the astrolabe in respect of calculation  it is a sign of the sunlike spirit

persian_text: خطوتینی بود این ره تا وصال ماندهام در ره ز شستت شصت سال
english_translation: This journey to union was a matter of two steps because of thy noose I have remained sixty years on the way

persian_text: تو ببین پس قصهی فجار را پیش بنگر مرگ یار و جار را
english_translation: Do thou see behind the story of the wicked see before the death of friend and neighbour

persian_text: دم به دم بر آسمان میدار امید در هوای آسمان رقصان چو بید
english_translation: Continually keep thy hope on Heaven dancing like the willow in desire for Heaven

persian_text: میزنم تا در رسد حکم خدا او کند هر خصم از خصمی جدا
english_translation: I will fight till the decision of God comes to pass He separates every adversary from an adversary

persian_text: صبرکردن لقمان چون دید کی داود علیه السلام حلقهها میساخت از سوال کردن با این نیت کی صبر از سوال موجب فرج باشد
english_translation: How Luqmán when he saw David on whom be peace making rings refrained from questioning him with the intention that this act of selfcontrol should be the cause of relief

persian_text: پس رسولش شکر کرد و گفت ما این جفا دیدیم و بود این خود وفا
english_translation: Then the Prophet thanked her and said I deemed this rudeness but it really was kindness

persian_text: از قضای آسمان استاد ما گشت رنجور و سقیم و مبتلا
english_translation: By the destiny of Heaven our master has become ill and sick and afflicted

persian_text: دست بر بالای دستست ای فتی در فن و در زور تا ذات خدا
english_translation: Hand is above hand O youth in skill and in strength up to the Essence of God

persian_text: گفت موسیای خداوند حساب نقش کردی باز چون کردی خراب
english_translation: Moses said O Lord of the Reckoning Thou didst create the form how didst Thou destroy it again

persian_text: ای یرانا لانراه روز و شب چشمبند ما شده دید سبب
english_translation: O who daily and nightly seest us and whom we see not regarding the secondary cause has muffled our eyes

persian_text: گر چه عقلت سوی بالا میپرد مرغ تقلیدت به پستی میچرد
english_translation: Although your intellect is flying upward the bird of your conventional notions is feeding below

persian_text: و ز سبک داری فرمانهای او و ز فراغت از غم فردای او
english_translation: And of holding light His commands and of being free from care for His Tomorrow

persian_text: گر نبودی حاجت عالم زمین نافریدی هیچ ربالعالمین
english_translation: If the earth had not been needed by the world the Lord of all beings would not have created any

persian_text: ور تو اندر نگذری اکرام حق بگذراند مر ترا از نه طبق
english_translation: And if you do not relinquish them  the grace of God will enable you to pass beyond the nine tiers

persian_text: آنچ اصلست و کشندهی آن شی استگر نمیماند بوی هم از وی است
english_translation: That which is the origin and bringeron of that thingif it does not resemble it still it is from it

persian_text: ننگ درویشان ز درویشی ما روز شب از روزی اندیشی ما
english_translation: The poor feel shame at our poverty day is turned to night by our anxiety about our daily portion

persian_text: قوم گفتند ای صحابی عزیز چون نسوزید و منقی گشت نیز
english_translation: The party said O venerable Companion  how didnt it burn and how too did it become cleansed

persian_text: میزنم نعره درین روضه و عیون خلق را یا لیت قومی یعلمون
english_translation: Amidst these gardens and fountains I am crying to the people Oh would that my folk did but know

persian_text: ما که کورانه عصاها میزنیم لاجرم قندیلها را بشکنیم
english_translation: We who blindly brandish staffs cannot help breaking lamps

persian_text: چون که صوفی بر نشست و شد روان رو در افتادن گرفت او هر زمان
english_translation: When the Súfí mounted and got going he began to fall on his face every time

persian_text: کوزهی آن تن پر از آب حیات کوزهی این تن پر از زهر ممات
english_translation: The pot of that body is filled with the Water of Life the pot of this body is filled with the poison of death

persian_text: او ز آتش ورد احمر آورد از زیانها سود بر سر آورد
english_translation: He brings red roses from the fire from losses he brings gain to the surface

persian_text: چون فراموش خودی یادت کنند بنده گشتی آنگه آزادت کنند
english_translation: When you are forgetful of self you are remembered  you have become a slave  then you are set free

persian_text: کرد بیچاره بسی فریاد کرد هم موکل را به دانگی شاد کرد
english_translation: The helpless Kurd made a great outcry he also gladdened the officer with a dáng

persian_text: گفت هر چند این جزای کار تست که نباشی در وفای ما درست
english_translation: He said Albeit this is the retribution for thy deed in that thou art not firm in keeping faith with me

persian_text: بعد ماهی خلق گفتند ای مهان از امیران کیست بر جایش نشان
english_translation: After a month the people said O chiefs which of amírs is designated in his place

persian_text: اختیارش زید را قدیش کند بیسگ و بیدام حق صیدش کند
english_translation: His freewill makes him a fetter for Zayd God makes him His prey without dog or snare

persian_text: ای فغان از یار ناجنسای فغان همنشین نیک جویید ای مهان
english_translation: Oh alas alas for the sorrow caused by a base friend O sirs seek ye a good companion

persian_text: آب دریا را حکم سازید حق تا که ماند کی برد زین دو سبق
english_translation: God made the water of the sea His arbiter that it might be left which of these two should prevail

persian_text: گازری بود و مر او را یک خری پشت ریش اشکم تهی و لاغری
english_translation: there was a washerman who had an ass with a sore on its back and emptybellied and lean

persian_text: همچو مطبوخ است و حب کان را خوری تا به دیری شورش و رنج اندری
english_translation: It is like decoctions and pills which you swallow and for a long time you are in disturbance and pain

persian_text: دیدن درویش جماعت مشایخ را در خواب و درخواست کردن روزی حلال بیمشغول شدن به کسب و از عبادت ماندن و ارشاد ایشان او را و میوههای تلخ و ترش کوهی بر وی شیرین شدن به داد آن مشایخ
english_translation: How a dervish saw in dream a company of Shaykhs and begged for a daily portion of lawful food without being occupied with earning and being incapacitated from devotional service and how they directed him and how the sour and bitter mountainfruit became sweet to him through the bounty of those Shaykhs

persian_text: چون طمع خواهد ز من سلطان دین خاک بر فرق قناعت بعد ازین
english_translation: Since the Sultan of the Judgement desires covetousness from me dust on the head of contentment henceforth

persian_text: مرد ز آن گفتن پشیمان شد چنان کز عوانی ساعت مردن عوان
english_translation: The man became as sorry for that speech as at the hour of death a tyrannical officer for his tyranny

persian_text: خواب چه بود بر درختان میروم میوههاشان میخورم چون نگروم
english_translation: How can it be a dream I go up the trees I eat their fruit how should I not believe

persian_text: چون ندارد جان جاوید او شقیست جرات او بر اجل از احمقیست
english_translation: Since he does not possess the everlasting soul he is damned his boldness in death is the result of folly

persian_text: مور بر دانه بدان لرزان شود که ز خرمنهای خوش اعمی بود
english_translation: The ant trembles for a grain because it is blind to the goodly threshingfloors

persian_text: سجدهگاه لامکانی در مکان مر بلیسان را ز تو ویران دکان
english_translation: Thou art the spaceless Object of worship in space the devils have their shop destroyed by thee

persian_text: میخرد از مالت انبانی نجس میدهد نور ضمیری مقتبس
english_translation: He buys a dirty bag from your goods and gives an inner light that borrows

persian_text: دست میمالید بر اعضای شیر پشت و پهلو گاه بالا گاه زیر
english_translation: He was rubbing his hand on the limbs of the lion back and side now above now below

persian_text: نیست دستوری وگر نه ریختی گرد از دریای راز انگیختی
english_translation: There is not permission  otherwise he would have poured out and would have raised dust from the sea of the mystery

persian_text: از علمهای گدایان ترس چیست کان علمها لقمهی نان را رهی است
english_translation: What is there to fear from the flags of beggarsfor those flags are a means for a mouthful of bread

persian_text: لطف کنای رازدان رازگو آنچ بازت صید کردش بازگو
english_translation: Show kindness O thou who knowest and canst tell the mystery declare that which thy falcon has made its prey

persian_text: عشقها داریم با این خاک ما زانک افتادست در قعدهی رضا
english_translation: We have great affections towards this earth because it lies in the posture of acquiescence

persian_text: توانگری پنهان کنی در ذل فقر طوق دولت بسته اندر غل فقر
english_translation: Thou dost conceal riches in the lowliness of poverty Thou dost fasten the necklace of wealth to the iron collar of poverty

persian_text: صد کراهت در درون تو چو خار کی بود انده نشان ابتشار
english_translation: A hundred loathings like thorns are in thy heart how should grief be the sign of rejoicing

persian_text: انبیا گویند روز چاره رفت چاره آنجا بود و دستافزار زفت
english_translation: The prophets say The day for remedy is past the remedy and the strong implement were there

persian_text: گر عصاهای خدا را بشمرم زرق این فرعونیان را بر درم
english_translation: If I should enumerate the staves of God I should tear to pieces the hypocrisy of these followers of Pharaoh

persian_text: که ببینم رزق میآید به من تا قوی گردد مرا در رزق ظن
english_translation: Saying I will see whether the daily bread will come to me that my belief in the daily bread may become firm

persian_text: صنعت زراد او کم دیده بود درعجب میماند وسواسش فزود
english_translation: He had not seen the armourers handicraft  he remained in astonishment and his curiosity increased

persian_text: گرگ درندهست نفس بد یقین چه بهانه مینهی بر هر قرین
english_translation: Assuredly thy wicked carnal soul is a rapacious wolf why art thou laying the blame on every comrade

persian_text: هست آن بستان نشاندن همعرض گشت جوهر کشت بستان نک غرض
english_translation: Similarly the planting of the garden is the accident the produce became the substance take behold the object

persian_text: همچو شهزاده رسی در یار خویش پس برون آری ز پا تو خار خویش
english_translation: Like the prince thou wilt attain unto thy Beloved then thou wilt draw out from thy foot the thorn of self

persian_text: یا به زخم من رگ جانت برد یا که همچون من به زندانت برد
english_translation: Either it will sever the vein of thy life by my stroke or it will bring thee into a prison as me

persian_text: بر قضا هر کو شبیخون آورد سرنگون آید ز خون خود خورد
english_translation: When space makes an assault upon nonspatiality it falls headlong and drinks its own blood

persian_text: هر کجا شمع بلا افروختند صد هزاران جان عاشق سوختند
english_translation: Wheresoever the candle of tribulation hath been lighted hundreds of thousands of loving souls are burnt

persian_text: چون بکرد آن منع دلشان زان مقال در هوس افتاد و در کوی خیال
english_translation: when he uttered that prohibition their hearts were thrown by his speech into vain desire and into the quarter of phantasy

persian_text: بخل من لله عطا لله و بس جمله للهام نیم من آن کس
english_translation: My stinginess is for Gods sake my bounty is for Gods sake alone I belong entirely to God I do not belong to any one

persian_text: زانک این تن شد مقام چار خو نامشان شد چار مرغ فتنهجو
english_translation: Inasmuch as this body is the abode of four dispositions they are named the four mischiefseeking birds

persian_text: در همه عالم اگر مرد و زنند دم به دم در نزع و اندر مردنند
english_translation: whether men or women in the whole world are continually in the deathagony and are dying

persian_text: زانک پیلم دید هندستان به خواب از خراج اومید بر ده شد خراب
english_translation: Forasmuch as my elephant has dreamed of Hindustán abandon hope of the tax the village is ruined

persian_text: رو بدو کرد و بگفتش ای امیر ملک دل به یا چنان ملک حقیر
english_translation: He turned his face towards him and said to him O Amír is the kingdom of the heart better or such a despicable kingdom

persian_text: آنک در ذاتش تفکر کردنیست در حقیقت آن نظر در ذات نیست
english_translation: that One whose Essence is an object of thought in reality the speculation is not concerning the Essence

persian_text: آن خداوندی که دادندت عوام باز بستانند از تو همچو وام
english_translation: The dominion which the vulgar have given to thee they will take back from thee as a debt

persian_text: شاد از وی شو مشو از غیر وی او بهارست و دگرها ماه دی
english_translation: Rejoice in Him do not rejoice in aught except Him He is the spring and other things the month of December

persian_text: سوی حوضت آورد بهر وضو واندر اندازد ترا در قعر او
english_translation: it will take you to the tank to perform the ritual ablution and will cast you to the bottom thereof

persian_text: ای دهلهای تهی بی قلوب قسمتان از عید جان شد زخم چوب
english_translation: O empty drums without hearts your share in the festival of the spirit is blows of the stick

persian_text: هر که با خود بهر حق باشد به جنگ تا شود معنیش خصم بو و رنگ
english_translation: Whoso is at war with himself for Gods sake to the end that his reality may become the opponent of scent and colour

persian_text: هر خیالی را خیالی میخورد فکر آن فکر دگر را میچرد
english_translation: Every phantasy is devouring another phantasy thought feeds on another thought

persian_text: خاص گفتندی که سوی چشمتان مینماید او ترشای امتان
english_translation: The elect would say To your eyes O peoples he appears to be sour

persian_text: حکایت آن واعظ کی هر آغاز تذکیر دعای ظالمان و سختدلان و بیاعتقادان کردی
english_translation: Story of the preacher who at the beginning of every exhortation used to pray for the unjust and hardhearted and irreligious

persian_text: چون تهی گشت و وجود او نماند باز جانش را خدا در پیش خواند
english_translation: When he was emptied and his existence remained not God called the falcon his soul into His presence

persian_text: از قدحگر در عطش آبی خورید در درون آب حق را ناظرید
english_translation: If in thirst ye drink some water from a cup ye are beholding God within the water

persian_text: جمع گردد بر وی آن جمله بزه کو سری بودست و ایشان دمغزه
english_translation: All their guilt is collected on him for he has been a head  while they are the root of the tail

persian_text: تو عسل ما سرکه در دنیا و دین دفع این صفرا بود سرکنگبین
english_translation: Thou art honey we are vinegar in this world and in religion the removing this bile is oxymel

persian_text: من هم او را میشفیع آرم به تو حال اوای حالدان با من بگو
english_translation: Him I bring to plead with Thee tell me his plight O Thou who knowest plight

persian_text: شد کبوتر ایمن از چنگال باز گوسفند از گرگ ناورد احتراز
english_translation: The dove became secure from the talons of the hawk the sheep took no precaution against the wolf

persian_text: گفت لا حول این چه افزون گفتن است از قدیم این کارها کار من است
english_translation: Good gracious he replied why this saying overmuch These things have been my care since long ago

persian_text: آن یکی در مرغزار و جوی آب و آن یکی پهلوی او اندر عذاب
english_translation: The one is in meadows and waterbrooks while the other one beside him is in torment

persian_text: مرد بالغ گشت آن بچگی بمرد رومیی شد صبغت زنگی سترد
english_translation: a man grows up his childhood dies he becomes a Greek he washes out the dye of the Ethiopian

persian_text: سنگهاش اندر حدث جای تباه میدمید از سنگها دود سیاه
english_translation: A corrupted place its stones in filth from the stones floated up black smoke

persian_text: گر هزاران بنده باشندت گواه بر نسنجد شرع ایشان را به کاه
english_translation: if thousands of slaves bear witness on thy behalf the law does not assign to them the weight of a straw

persian_text: آن یکی پرسید اشتر را که هی از کجا میآیی ای اقبال پی
english_translation: A certain man asked a camel Hey whence comest thou O thou whom fortune attends

persian_text: کز ذنب پرهیز کن هین هوشدار تا نگردی تو سیهرو دیگوار
english_translation: Saying Avoid the Tail hark take heed lest thou become black of face like a cookingpot

persian_text: آن دل چون سنگ را ما چند چند نرم گفتیم و نمیپذرفت پند
english_translation: How oft how oft have we spoken gently to our stony hearts and they would not accept the counsel

persian_text: تا نخوانی لا و الا الله را در نیابی منهج این راه را
english_translation: Until you recite  not  and except Allah you will not find the plain track of this Way

persian_text: بر کف من نه شراب آتشین وانگه آن کر و فر مستانه بین
english_translation: Put the fiery wine in my hand and then behold the pomp and glory that is enjoyed by the drunken

persian_text: در درون هر دو از راه نهان هر زمان گفتی خیال میهمان
english_translation: In the hearts of them both by the hidden way the phantom of the guest was saying continually

persian_text: روز موسی پیش حق نالان شده نیم شب فرعون گریان آمده
english_translation: In the daytime Moses was making lament to God at midnight Pharaoh would begin to weep

persian_text: قصهی بازرگان که طوطی محبوس او او را پیغام داد به طوطیان هندوستان هنگام رفتن به تجارت
english_translation: The story of the merchant to whom the captive parrot gave a message for the parrots of India on the occasion of his going to trade

persian_text: هین بکن در دفع آن خصم احتیاط هر که میزایید میکشت از خباط
english_translation: Hark take precautions to repel that enemy in his craziness he would fain kill every child that was born

persian_text: برفشانم بر شما چندان عطا که بدرد پردهی جود و سخا
english_translation: I will scatter over you so many gifts that the veil of bounty and munificence will be rent

persian_text: اینچنین باده همیبرد آن غلام سوی قصر آن امیر نیکنام
english_translation: The slave was carrying a wine of this sort to the palace of the Amír of good renown

persian_text: پاکی این حوض بیپایان بود پاکی اجسام کم میزان بود
english_translation: The purity of this tank is infinite the purity of bodies is of little weight

persian_text: گفت رمزی ز آن بگوای پادشاه کز برای من بگفت آن دین تباه
english_translation: He said O King utter some hint of what that miscreant said about me

persian_text: ز آنکه محدود است و معدود است آن آینهی دل را نباشد حد بدان
english_translation: Because those are bounded and numbered know that the mirror of the heart hath no bound

persian_text: آنکه حق پشتش نباشد از ظفر وای اگر گربش نماید شیر نر
english_translation: he that hath not God for his victorious support alas if the fierce lion seem to him a cat

persian_text: هر نفس نو میشود دنیا و ما بیخبر از نو شدن اندر بقا
english_translation: Every moment the world is renewed and we are unaware of its being renewed whilst it remains

persian_text: و آن کبوترشان ز بازان نشکهد باز سر پیش کبوترشان نهد
english_translation: And their dove is not scared by hawks the hawk lays his head before their dove

persian_text: لطف شاهنشاه جان بیوطن چون اثر کرده ست اندر کل تن
english_translation: how the imperial grace of the homeless Spirit has produced effects on the whole body

persian_text: تشبیه بند و دام قضا به صورت پنهان به اثر پیدا
english_translation: A comparison the bonds and snares of Destiny though outwardly invisible are manifest in their effects

persian_text: ای عدو آفتابی کز فرش میبلرزد آفتاب و اخترش
english_translation: O enemy of the Sun at whose glory His sun and stars tremble

persian_text: آن غم آمد ز آرزوهای فضول که بدان خو کرده است آن صید غول
english_translation: That anxiety arises from the wanton desires to which that prey to the ghoul has become habituated

persian_text: من که خصمم هم منم اندر گریز تا ابد کار من آمد خیزخیز
english_translation: I who am the adversary  tis I that am in flight  rising and departing is my occupation for ever

persian_text: آن فلان شب حاضر آ ای شاهپیل تا درون چشمه یابی زین دلیل
english_translation: Come and be present on the suchandsuch a night O king elephant in order that within the spring thou mayst find the proof of this

persian_text: امر سلطان به بود پیش شما یا که این نیکو گهر بهر خدا
english_translation: In your eyes is the command of the sovereign or this goodly pearl superior For Gods sake

persian_text: مثل شیطان بر در رحمان
english_translation: Parable of the Devil at the door of the Merciful God

persian_text: گفت و گو بسیار گشت و خلق گیج در سر و پایان این چرخ پسیج
english_translation: There was much debate and the people became perplexed as to the beginning and end of this wellordered celestial sphere

persian_text: از بزرگی تخت کز حد میفزود نقل کردن تخت را امکان نبود
english_translation: There was no possibility of transporting the throne because of its hugeness which exceeded bounds

persian_text: ترسم ار خامش کنم آن آفتاب از سوی دیگر بدراند حجاب
english_translation: If I keep silence I fear that that Sun will rend the veil and from another quarter

persian_text: همچو آن شیبان که از گرگ عنید وقت جمعه بر رعا خط میکشید
english_translation: Just as Shaybán Ráí  because of the froward wolf used to draw a line round his flock at the hour of the Friday prayers

persian_text: مر و را آزاد کردی از نخست لیک خشنودی لقمان را بجست
english_translation: He would have set him free at the first but he sought to content Luqmán

persian_text: او عجب مانده که ذوق این ز چیست و آن عجب مانده که این در حبس کیست
english_translation: He remains in wonderment saying Wherefore is this mans delight and the other remains in wonderment saying In whose prison is this man

persian_text: رو ترش کردی و جاروبی به کف چیست آن خر برگسسته از علف
english_translation: You have made your face sour and a broom in your hand what is the ass having turned away from his fodder

persian_text: سایههایی که بود جویای نور نیست گردد چون کند نورش ظهور
english_translation: The reflexions that are seeking the Light are naughted when His Light appears

persian_text: یک بدست از جمع رفتن یک زمان مکر دیو است بشنو و نیکو بدان
english_translation: To go for one moment a single span apart from the community is the Devils guile Hearken and know well

persian_text: از پی تقلید و معقولات نقل پا نهاده بر جمال پیر عقل
english_translation: For the sake of blind conformity and traditional ideas they set their feet on the camels of Reason this venerable Guide

persian_text: در یکی گفته که استادی طلب عاقبت بینی نیابی در حسب
english_translation: In one he said Seek a master  you will not find foresight as to the end among the qualities derived from ancestors

persian_text: گفت استاد احولی را کاندر آ رو برون آر از وثاق آن شیشه را
english_translation: The master said to a squinteyed  Come on go fetch that bottle out of the room

persian_text: جنبش باد نفس کاندر لبست تابع تصریف جان و قالبست
english_translation: The movement of the wind of the breath which is on the lips follows the course directed by the spirit and the body

persian_text: خود گرفته ستت تو چون کفتار کور این گرفتن را نبینی از غرور
english_translation: Indeed He has chastised you like the blind hyena from selfdeception you do not see the chastisement

persian_text: گر چه در خشکی هزاران رنگهاست ماهیان را با یبوست جنگهاست
english_translation: Although there are thousands of colours on dry land fishes are at war with dryness

persian_text: گر در آمیزد تو گویی طامع است ور نه گویی در تکبر مولع است
english_translation: If he mix  you say he is covetous and if not you say he is excessively given to pride

persian_text: در گنه او از ادب پنهانش کرد ز آن گنه بر خود زدن او بر بخورد
english_translation: From respect he concealed it in the sin by casting the sin upon himself he ate fruit

persian_text: صورت عاشق چو فانی شد درو پس در آب اکنون کرا بیند بگو
english_translation: since the lovers image has disappeared in Him  whom now should he behold in the water Tell

persian_text: پیل باور کرد از وی آن خطاب چون درون چشمه مه کرد اضطراب
english_translation: The elephant believed that speech of his  when the Moon in the spring showed disturbance

persian_text: ز آنکه با جامه درون سو راه نیست تن ز جان جامه ز تن آگاه نیست
english_translation: Because with your clothes there is no way inside the body is ignorant of the soul the clothes of the body

persian_text: تمثیل لوح محفوظ و ادراک عقل هر کسی از آن لوح
english_translation: Comparison of the Guarded Tablet  and the perception there from by every individuals mind of his daily fate

persian_text: که چه خواهم خورد مستقبل عجب لوت فردا از کجا سازم طلب
english_translation: Thinking I wonder what I shall eat in the future whence shall I seek food for tomorrow

persian_text: گفت من کردم جوامردی بپند تا رهانم من ترا زین خشک بند
english_translation: He says I bestowed counsel generously that I might deliver you from this sterile bondage

persian_text: صد خلیفه گشته کمتر از مگس پیش چشم آتشینش آن نفس
english_translation: To his fiery eye a hundred Caliphs seemed at that moment less than a gnat

persian_text: تا که آید لطف بخشایشگری سرخ گردد روی زرد از گوهری
english_translation: In order that the grace of a Benefactor may come and that thy pale face may be reddened by a jewel

persian_text: این جهان پر ز نقل و پر ز نان چون دهان باز آن تمساح دان
english_translation: Know that this world full of dessert and bread is like the open mouth of the crocodile

persian_text: گفت شیخا وقت رحم و رقت است ناامیدم وقت لطف این ساعت است
english_translation: O Shaykh he cried it is the time for mercy and pity I am in despair now is the time for kindness

persian_text: دختران را لعبت مرده دهند که ز لعب زندگان بیآگهند
english_translation: A dead doll is given to girls for they are ignorant of the play of living

persian_text: خلق گفتند این مسلمان راستگوست وین فروشندهی دعاها ظلمجوست
english_translation: The people said This Moslem speaks the truth and this prayermonger is one who seeks to act unjustly

persian_text: هر حریصی هست محرومای پسر چون حریصان تگ مرو آهستهتر
english_translation: O son every greedy person is deprived  do not thou run like the greedy more slowly

persian_text: دوستی جاهل شیرینسخن کم شنو کان هست چون سم کهن
english_translation: Do not listen to the friendliness of the fairspoken ignorant man for it is like old poison

persian_text: راند حق این آب را در جوی تو آفرین بر دست و بر بازوی تو
english_translation: God turned this water into thy stream Blessing on thy hand and arm

persian_text: سستی او هست چون سستی مست که اندر آن سستیش رشک رستمست
english_translation: His weakness is like the weakness of the intoxicated for in his weakness he is the envy of a Rustam

persian_text: سایه خواب آرد ترا همچون سمر چون بر آید شمس انشق القمر
english_translation: The shadow like chat in the nighthours brings sleep to thee when the sun rises the moon is cloven asunder

persian_text: گفت ای احمق بر این بر دوختی از طبیبی تو همین آموختی
english_translation: O fool he exclaimed you have stuck at this this is all that you have learned of medicine

persian_text: اختران تافته بر چار طاق لحظهلحظه مبتلای احتراق
english_translation: Stars shining in the four quarters are from time to time afflicted with burning

persian_text: زان همیخوردند چون از صید شیر هر یکی از خوردنش چون پیل سیر
english_translation: They were eating thereof as a lion of his prey each of them surfeited like an elephant with eating it

persian_text: بیهشان را وادهد حق هوشها حلقهحلقه حلقهها در گوشها
english_translation: God gives back to the senseless ones their senses troop after troop with rings in their ears

persian_text: گر همیخواهی سلامت از ضرر چشم ز اول بند و پایان را نگر
english_translation: If you desire to be safe from harm close your eye to the beginning and contemplate the end

persian_text: زودشان در یاب و استغفار کن همچو ابری گریههای زار کن
english_translation: Quickly overtake them and ask pardon of God weep lamentably like a cloud

persian_text: دستبسته گبر و همچون گربهای خسته کرده حلق او بیحربهای
english_translation: The pinioned infidel as a cat had wounded his throat without a lance

persian_text: گر چنین بودی همه عالم بدین یک دعا املاک بردندی بکین
english_translation: If it were so by means of a single prayer of this kind the whole world would carry off possessions by force

persian_text: گر جهان پیشت بزرگ و بیبنی است پیش قدرت ذرهای میدان که نیست
english_translation: If the world appears to you vast and bottomless know that to Omnipotence it is not an atom

persian_text: تا بداند کان حلل عاریه بود پرتوی بود آن ز خورشید وجود
english_translation: That he may know that those robes were a loan twas a ray from the Sun of Being

persian_text: اهل چین و روم چون حاضر شدند رومیان از بحث در مکث آمدند
english_translation: When the Chinese and the Greeks presented themselves the Greeks were more skilled in the knowledge

persian_text: موزه بربودی و من درهم شدم تو غمم بردی و من در غم شدم
english_translation: Thou didst carry off the boot and I was perturbed thou tookst away my grief and I was aggrieved

persian_text: بو نبردی تو دل اندر جنس خویش کی بری تو بوی دل از گرگ و میش
english_translation: You have not smelt the heart in your own kind how should you smell the heart in wolf and sheep

persian_text: ناگهان رفت او ولیکن چونک رفت میببایستم شدن در پی بتفت
english_translation: He went off suddenly but seeing that he went I ought to have gone after him in hot haste

persian_text: قامت تو بر قرار آمد بساز سایهات کوته دمی یکدم دراز
english_translation: Your stature is normally invariable your shadow is now short now long

persian_text: سنبل و لاله و سپرغم نیز هم با هزاران ناز و نفرت خوردهام
english_translation: I have eaten hyacinth and anemone and sweet basil too with a thousand disdains and disgusts

persian_text: زهره دارد آب کز امر صمد گردد او با کافران آبی کند
english_translation: Will the water dare to turn aside from the command of the Lord and bestow refreshment on the unbelievers

persian_text: کافران دیدند احمد را بشر چون ندیدند از وی انشق القمر
english_translation: The infidels regarded Ahmad as a man since they did not see in him the moon was cleft asunder

persian_text: پند موسی نشنوی شوخی کنی خویشتن بر تیغ پولادی زنی
english_translation: you hearken not to the counsel of Moses and show disrespect you dash yourself against a sword of steel

persian_text: پرتو شیخ آمد و منهل ز شیخ فیض شادی نه از مریدان بل ز شیخ
english_translation: It is the Shaykhs reflexion and its source is in the Shaykh the overflow of joy is not from the disciples nay it is from the Shaykh

persian_text: گریه و خنده غم و شادی دل هر یکی را معدنی دان مستقل
english_translation: Weeping and laughter and sorrow and joy of heartknow that each one has an independent source

persian_text: بهر جان خویش جو زیشان صلاح هین مدزد از حرف ایشان اصطلاح
english_translation: Seek good for thy soul from them  beware do not steal mystical expressions from their language

persian_text: در عمارت هستی و جنگی بود نیست را از هستها ننگی بود
english_translation: In the state of cultivation there is existence and strife  the nonexistent is ashamed of existent things

persian_text: شد پشیمان خواجه از گفت خبر گفت رفتم در هلاک جانور
english_translation: The merchant repented of having told the news and said I have gone about to destroy the creature

persian_text: آدمی دیدهست باقی گوشت و پوست هرچه چشمش دیده است آن چیز اوست
english_translation: Man is eye the rest is flesh and skin whatsoever his eye has beheld he is that thing

persian_text: همچنین شیبان راعی میکشید گرد بر گرد رمه خطی پدید
english_translation: Likewise Shaybán the shepherd used to draw a visible line round his flock

persian_text: گفت این باری یقین شد پیش عام که جهان در امر یزدانست رام
english_translation: He said This at least is evident to the vulgar that the world is subject to the command of God

persian_text: چونک بجهد در فتد اندر میان در میان هر دو کوه بیامان
english_translation: As soon as he leaps he falls midway between the two pitiless mountains

persian_text: بینوا از نان و خوان آسمان پیش او ننداخت حق یک استخوان
english_translation: without portion of the bread and viands of Heaven God did not throw a single bone to him

persian_text: ای ز تو ویران دکان و منزلم چون ننالم چون بیفشاری دلم
english_translation: O Thou by whom my shop and dwelling is ruined how shall not I wail when Thou rackest my heart

persian_text: آن دعای کهنهام شد مستجاب روزی من بود کشتم نک جواب
english_translation: That ancient prayer of mine was answered  She was my portion of daily bread I killed her Behold the answer

persian_text: گر سیه کردی تو نامهی عمر خویش توبه کن زانها که کردستی تو پیش
english_translation: If you have blackened the scroll of your life repent of the deeds you did formerly

persian_text: تازه کن ایمان نه از گفت زبانای هوا را تازه کرده در نهان
english_translation: Refresh thy faith not with talk of the tongue O thou who hast secretly refreshed thy desire

persian_text: قسم او خاکستگر دیگر بهار میر کونی خاک چون نوشی چو مار
english_translation: Its food is earth whether in winter or in spring thou art the lord of creation how is it thou eatest earth like the snake

persian_text: مغربی را مشرقی کرده خدای کرده مغرب را چو مشرق نورزای
english_translation: God made the Maghribí a Mashriqí He made the place of sunset lightproducing like the place of sunrise

persian_text: گاه نقشش دیو و گه آدم کند گاه نقشش شادی و گه غم کند
english_translation: Now He makes the picture thereon the Devil now Adam now He makes the picture thereon joy now grief

persian_text: جسم ما جوز و مویز استای پسرگر تو مردی زین دو چیز اندر گذر
english_translation: Our body is walnuts and raisins O son if you are a man relinquish these two things

persian_text: تهلکهست این صبر و پرهیزای فلان خوش بکوبش تن مزن چون دیگران
english_translation: O suchandsuch this selfrestraint and abstinence is destruction give him a good blow do not keep quiet like the others

persian_text: گر نه موشی دزد در انبار ماست گندم اعمال چل ساله کجاست
english_translation: If there is no thievish mouse in our barn where is the corn of forty years works

persian_text: نی ولیکن یار ما زین آگهست زانک از دل سوی دل لا بد رهست
english_translation: No but our Friend is acquainted with this  because inevitably there is a way from heart to heart

persian_text: چون که شکر گام کرد و ره برید لاجرم ز آن گام در کامی رسید
english_translation: When he has given thanks for the track and has traversed the way of necessity by means of that track he arrives at a goal

persian_text: عقل موسی چون شود در غیب بند عقل موشی خود کی استای ارجمند
english_translation: Inasmuch as the intellect of Moses becomes tied up in the mysterious  who is the intellect of a mouse O excellent

persian_text: در وضو هر عضو را وردی جدا آمدست اندر خبر بهر دعا
english_translation: In the ritual ablution a separate form of prayer for each member of the body has been handed down in Tradition

persian_text: هیچ پنهان مینشد از وی ضمیر بود بر مضمون دلها او امیر
english_translation: No mental conception was hidden from him he was ruler over the contents of hearts

persian_text: گوش را رهن معرف داشتن آیت محجوبیست و حزر و ظن
english_translation: To confine ones attention to the announcer is a mark of being debarred and of conjecture and opinion

persian_text: موج میزد در دلش عفو گنه که ز هر دل تا دل آمد روزنه
english_translation: Forgiveness of the sin was surging in his heart for as much as there is a window from each heart to heart

persian_text: باز حیوان را چو استعداد نیست عذر او اندر بهیمی روشنیست
english_translation: Again since the animal does not possess capacity its excusability in the bestial state is a thing evident

persian_text: معنی ما شاء الله کان
english_translation: The meaning of whatever God willed came to be

persian_text: بر همان بو میخوری این خشک را بعد از آن کامیخت معنی با ثری
english_translation: In the same hope thou are eating this dry stuff after the spirit has become mingled with clay

persian_text: که منم آن اصل اصل هوش و مست بر صور آن حسن عکس ما بدست
english_translation: Saying I am the ultimate origin of sobriety and intoxication the beauty in forms is reflected from Me

persian_text: صورت پنهان و آن نور جبین کرده چشم انبیا را دوربین
english_translation: The hidden Form and the Light of that Brow have made the eyes of the prophets farseeing

persian_text: کو گشاد رقعههای آسمان کو نهاد بقعههای خاکدان
english_translation: How can the wide expanse of the celestial domains be compared with the character of the terrestrial regions

persian_text: در دهای ساقی یکی رطلی گران خواجه را از ریش و سبلت وا رهان
english_translation: Hand a heavy goblet O cupbearer deliver the Khwája from his beard and moustache

persian_text: از پدر چون خواستندش دادران تا برندش سوی صحرا یک زمان
english_translation: When the brothers begged their father to give him to them that they might take him to the country for a while

persian_text: نقش یوسف کرد و حور خوش سرشت نقش عفریتان و ابلیسان زشت
english_translation: He painted Joseph and fairformed houris he painted ugly afreets and devils

persian_text: چون نبودی بد گمان در حق او چون نهادی سر چنانای زشترو
english_translation: How wert not thou suspicious in regard to him  Why didst thou lay thy head like that O ugly one

persian_text: جای دیگر خاک را چون بوی کرد گفت خاک مخزن شاهیست فرد
english_translation: When he smelt the earth in this other place he said Tis the earth of the treasury of a peerless king

persian_text: صورتی کو فاخر و عالی بود او ز بیتالله کی خالی بود
english_translation: How should the form  which is splendid and sublime be absent from the House of God

persian_text: که به عمر خود نخوردستم شراب خوشتر آید از شرابم زهر ناب
english_translation: Saying I have never drunk wine in my life rank poison would please me better than wine

persian_text: تو نیایی و نگویی مر مرا که خرت را میبرند ای بینوا
english_translation: you would not come and say to me They are taking away your ass O poor man

persian_text: تا جهان گه صابر است و گه شکور بوستان گه حله پوشد گاه عور
english_translation: So that the world is now patient now thankful the garden now puts on a robe and again is bare

persian_text: جمله رنجوران دوا دارند امید نالد این رنجور کم افزون کنید
english_translation: All the sick hope to be cured but this sick one sobs crying Increase my sickness

persian_text: تا که باز آید به من عقلم دمی که فتادم در عجایب عالمی
english_translation: That my wits may once come back to me for I am fallen into a marvellous state

persian_text: دام خود را سختتر یابند و بس کی نماید قوتی با باد خس
english_translation: they only find their snare more grievous  how can straws show any power against the wind

persian_text: نه ز گلحنها برو ننگی بماند نه ز گلشنها برو رنگی بماند
english_translation: No disgrace remained with it from the ashpits no colour remained with it from the rosegardens

persian_text: شیر مستی کز صفت بیرون بود از بسیط مرغزار افزون بود
english_translation: The furious Lion who is beyond description is too great for the wide expanse of the meadow

persian_text: ابر راگر هست ظاهر رو ترش گلشن آرندهست ابر و شورهکش
english_translation: If the cloud apparently has a sour face the cloud is the bringeron of the rosegarden and the destroyer of the nitrous soil

persian_text: ای خنک آن را کزین ملکت بجست که اجل این ملک را ویرانگرست
english_translation: Oh happy he that escaped from this kingdom for Death makes this kingdom desolate

persian_text: چون یقین گشتش رخ او زرد شد از مسلمانی دل او سرد شد
english_translation: When she became sure  her face turned pale and her heart grew cold to Islam

persian_text: پس بفرمودش خداای ذولباب چون بپرسیدی بیا بشنو جواب
english_translation: Then God spake unto him saying O thou who possessest the most excellent  since thou hast asked  come hear the answer

persian_text: صد متاع خوب عرضه میکنند واندرون دل عوضها میتنند
english_translation: They offer a hundred fine articles of merchandise and within their hearts they are intent on compensations

persian_text: خود که را زهره بدی تا او ز خود بر اسیر حکم حق تیغی زند
english_translation: Who indeed would have the stomach of himself to wield a sword against him that is a thrall to the decree of God

persian_text: میرهند ارواح هر شب زین قفس فارغان، نه حاکم و محکوم کس
english_translation: The spirits are set free every night from this cage independent neither ruling nor ruled by anyone

persian_text: آنک گل آرد برون از عین خار هم تواند کرد این دی را بهار
english_translation: He who brings forth roses from the very midst of thorns is also able to turn this winter into spring

persian_text: او برهنه آمد و عریان رود وز غم دزدش جگر خون میشود
english_translation: Bare he came and naked he goes and his heart is bleeding with anxiety on account of the thief

persian_text: شه به جای حاجبان واپیش رفت پیش آن مهمان غیب خویش رفت
english_translation: The king himself instead of the chamberlains went forward to meet his guest from the Invisible

persian_text: گرچه او محو حقست و بیسرست لیک کار من از آن نازکترست
english_translation: Though he is lost in God and headless  yet my case is more delicate than that

persian_text: مثل آوردن اشتر
english_translation: Parable of the camel

persian_text: مرغ جانها را در این آخر زمان نیستشان از همدگر یک دم امان
english_translation: In these latter days the soulbirds have no security from each other for a moment

persian_text: حق آن شه که ترا صاف آفرید کرد چندان مشعله در تو پدید
english_translation: By the truth of that King who created thee pure and displayed so many cressets in thee

persian_text: باد کشتی را به گردابی فگند گفت کشتیبان به آن نحوی بلند
english_translation: The wind cast the boat into a whirlpool the boatman spoke loud to the grammarian

persian_text: آنچ گفتندم ز بد از صد یکیست بر من این کشفست ار کس را شکیست
english_translation: The evil they spoke of me is a hundredth part  this is clearly known to me if any one has a doubt

persian_text: در تگ جو هست سرگینای فتیگر چه جو صافی نماید مر ترا
english_translation: There is dung in the bed of the stream my man though to you the stream appears pure

persian_text: آن یکی با شمع برمیگشت روز گرد بازاری دلش پر عشق و سوز
english_translation: That person was going about in a bazaar in the daytime with a candle his heart full of love and ardour

persian_text: ورنه ادهموار سرگردان و دنگ ملک را برهم زدندی بیدرنگ
english_translation: Otherwise dizzy and dumbfounded like Adham without delay they would have dashed their sovereignty to pieces

persian_text: این سخن پایان ندارد ای قباد حرص ما را اندرین پایان مباد
english_translation: This topic hath no end O emperor may there be no end to our desire for this

persian_text: شیخ کاو ینظر بنور الله شد از نهایت وز نخست آگاه شد
english_translation: The Shaykh who has become seeing by the light of God has become acquainted with the end and the beginning

persian_text: تا یکی یاری ز یاران رسول در دلش انکار آمد ز آن نکول
english_translation: so that one of the Prophets Companions conceived in his heart dislike of that recoilment

persian_text: روان شدن شهزادگان در ممالک پدر بعد از وداع ایشان شاه را
english_translation: How the princes having bidden the King farewell set out on a journey through their fathers empire

persian_text: همچنان آن ماجراها باز رفت زود زد آن مدعی تشنیع زفت
english_translation: Thus the questions in dispute came up again the plain tiff at once uttered violent reproaches

persian_text: بر امید عز ده روزهی خدوک گردن خود کردهاند از غم چو دوک
english_translation: In the hope of a ten days glory of annoyance they have made their necks from anxiety as a spindle

persian_text: آنچنان که وقت زادن حامله ناله دارد خواجه شد در غلغله
english_translation: Even as a woman big with child keeps wailing at the time of parturition so the Khwája began to cry out clamorously

persian_text: لاجرم گفت آن رسول ذو فنون رمز نحن الاخرون السابقون
english_translation: In consequence the allaccomplished Prophet said symbolically We are the hindmost and the foremost

persian_text: گشت آمن او ز خصمان و ز نیش رفت و میپیچید در سودای خویش
english_translation: He became secure from rivals and annoyance he went and wrapped himself in his melancholy madness

persian_text: گفت المرء مع محبوبه لا یفک القلب من مطلوبه
english_translation: said A man is with him whom he loves the heart is not severed from its object of desire

persian_text: من بدین وقت معینای دلیر مینگردم از محاکات تو سیر
english_translation: at this appointed time O brave  I never become weary of conversing with you

persian_text: او فضولی بوده است از انقباض کرد بر مختار مطلق اعتراض
english_translation: He has been meddlesome by distress he has interfered with Him who chooseth  the Absolute One

persian_text: کرد عمران خویش پر خشم و ترش رفت چون دیوانگان بیعقل و هش
english_translation: Imrán feigned to be wrathful and grim he went senseless and witless like madmen

persian_text: خانهی دل بین ز غم ژولیده شد بیکناس از توبهای روبیده شد
english_translation: Behold the house of the heart it was disordered by cares without sweeper it was swept by a repentance

persian_text: هر که بیرون بود ز آن خط جمله را پارهپاره میگسست اندر هوا
english_translation: it was dashing to pieces in the air all who were outside of the line

persian_text: که استخوان و اجزاء سرگین همچو نان نقل زاغان آمدست اندر جهان
english_translation: For in this world the dessert of the crows was bones and pieces of dung like bread

persian_text: مادران بچگان برون انداختند تا همه ناله و نفیر افراختند
english_translation: Mothers cast out their children that all might raise wailing and distressful cries

persian_text: قصه کوته کن که قاضی شد شکار از مقال و از جمال آن نگار
english_translation: cut the tale short the cadi fell a prey to the words and beauty of the fair woman

persian_text: هیچ عاشق خود نباشد وصلجو که نه معشوقش بود جویای او
english_translation: No lover in sooth is seeking union without his loved one seeking him

persian_text: روز و شب ظاهر دو ضد و دشمنند لیک هر دو یک حقیقت میتنند
english_translation: Day and night outwardly are two contraries and enemies but they both attend on one truth

persian_text: ما ندیدیم اینکه آن نقش است و کف کف ز دریا جنبد و یابد علف
english_translation: And this we did not perceive that those are form and foam the foam is moved and fed by the Sea

persian_text: چون رسول روم این الفاظتر در سماع آورد شد مشتاقتر
english_translation: When the ambassador of Rúm admitted these fresh words into his hearing  he became more full of longing

persian_text: عاقبت پیدا شود آثار این چون علامتها رسیدای نازنین
english_translation: In the end the effects of this will be made manifest forasmuch as the signs have appeared O beloved

persian_text: این دهانش نکتهگویان با جلیس و آن دگر با حق به گفتار و انیس
english_translation: This mouth of him is speaking on subtle points to those sitting beside him while the other is in discourse with God and intimate

persian_text: روز دیگر وقت دیوان و لقا پس سلیمان گفت عزراییل را
english_translation: Next day at the time of conference and meeting Solomon said to Azrael

persian_text: هر که را پرسید کردش ریشخند کاین که جوید جز مگر مجنون بند
english_translation: Every one whom he asked made a mock of him saying Who would search after this unless perhaps a madman in confinement

persian_text: هشت کرت کژ بکرد آن مهترش راست میشد تاج بر فرق سرش
english_translation: Eight times did that prince make it awry and did it become straight on the crown of his head

persian_text: کاژدهای مردهای آوردهام در شکارش من جگرها خوردهام
english_translation: Saying I have brought a dead dragon I have suffered agonies in hunting it

persian_text: گر تو برگردی و بر گردد سرت خانه را گردنده بیند منظرت
english_translation: If thou whirl round and thy head whirl round thy sight sees the house whirling round

persian_text: نفس فرعونیست هان سیرش مکن تا نیارد یاد از آن کفر کهن
english_translation: The carnal soul is a follower of Pharaoh beware do not satisfy it lest it remember its ancient infidelity

persian_text: کور گفتش آخر آن یاران تو بر کهند این دم شکاری صیدجو
english_translation: The blind man said to it  Why at this moment your friends are hunting and seeking prey on the mountain

persian_text: این بنا ز آب و گل مرده بدست وان بنا از طاعت زنده شدست
english_translation: This edifice has been of dead water and earth while that edifice has arisen from living piety

persian_text: پادشاهی را خدا کشتی کند تا به حرص خویش بر صفها زند
english_translation: God makes a king to be an ark  to the end that he by selfishness may assault the ranks

persian_text: تا بندهی آن گواهیای شهید تو ازین دهلیز کی خواهی رهید
english_translation: Until thou give that testimony O witness how wilt thou escape from this court

persian_text: تا بیابی بوی خلد از یار من چون محمد بوی رحمن از یمن
english_translation: So that you may feel the scent of Paradise from my Friend as Mohammed the scent of the Merciful from Yemen

persian_text: گر هنر بودی دلش را ز اختبار خوار کی بودی ز صورت آن حمار
english_translation: How should the ass be debased by his form if his spirit had possessed the virtue from experience

persian_text: گر بگویم از فراق چون شرار تا قیامت یک بود از صد هزار
english_translation: If I should speak of separation like sparks of fire till the Resurrection twould be one out of a hundred thousand

persian_text: شب غلط بنماید و مبدل بسی دید صایب شب ندارد هر کسی
english_translation: Night causes many a thing to appear wrong and changed  not every one has the seeing correctly by night

persian_text: بر سر دیوار عالیگر رویگر دو گز عرضش بود کژ میشوی
english_translation: if you walk on the top of a high wall you will stagger even if its width be two ells

persian_text: حرف درویشان بدزدیده بسی تا گمان آید که هست او خود کسی
english_translation: He has stolen many an expression used by dervishes in order that he himself may be thought to be a personage

persian_text: حکایت خرگوشان
english_translation: Story of the hares

persian_text: و آن صف پیش از ضعیفی بصر تاب نارد روشنایی بیشتر
english_translation: And that front rank from weakness of sight cannot support the light that is more advanced

persian_text: چون الف چیزی ندارم ای کریم جز دلی دلتنگتر از چشم میم
english_translation: Like alif I possess nothing O Gracious One except a heart more constricted with anguish than the eye of mim

persian_text: جرعهای بر ماه و خورشید و حمل جرعهای بر عرش و کرسی و زحل
english_translation: a draught on the moon and the sun and Aries a draught on the Throne and the Footstool and Saturn

persian_text: وهم آن گاه است کان پوشیده است این تحری از پی نادیده است
english_translation: Imagination occurs at the time when that is hidden this searching is after the unseen

persian_text: این عوان در حق غیری سود شد لیک اندر حق خود مردود شد
english_translation: This policeman became a profit in respect of another but he became reprobate in respect of himself

persian_text: ذرهای ز آن آفتاب آرد پیام آفتاب آن ذره را گردد غلام
english_translation: a mote bring a message from yonder Sun the sun would become a slave to that mote

persian_text: تا نگیرد آن کفنخواه آن صله تا نهان نکند ازو آن دهدله
english_translation: Lest the purveyor of the graveclothes should seize the gift of money and lest that perfidious rascal should hide it from him

persian_text: این نخواهد شد بروزی و دو روز مهلتم ده تا چهل روز تموز
english_translation: this will not be done in a day or two give me time till the forty days of Tamúz

persian_text: کرامات و نور شیخ عبدالله مغربی قدس الله سره
english_translation: The miraculous gifts and illumination of Shaykh Abdullah Maghribí may God sanctify his spirit

persian_text: گفتگر چه هر سعادت از خداست در میان ما و تو بس فرقهاست
english_translation: He said Though every felicity is from God there are many differences between me and thee

persian_text: بل عدو خویش را هر جانور خود بداند از نشان و از اثر
english_translation: Nay every animal indeed knows its own enemy by sign and mark

persian_text: گر به خلوت آیی ای سرو سهی از ستمکاری شو شرحم دهی
english_translation: if you will come to my private house O cypressslender one and describe to me the injurious behaviour of your husband

persian_text: آن بنای انبیا بی حرص بود زان چنان پیوسته رونقها فزود
english_translation: That edifice of the prophets was without greed  hence the splendours increased so uninterruptedly

persian_text: گر حشیش آب و هوایی میخورد معدهی حیوانش در پی میچرد
english_translation: If the herbage is drinking pure water afterwards an animals belly will feed on it

persian_text: او میانجی شد میان دشمنان اتحادی شد میان پر زنان
english_translation: He became an arbitrator between enemies he became a oneness between the creatures that fly with wings

persian_text: این هم از تاثیر آن بیماریست زهر او در جمله جفتان ساریست
english_translation: This also is from the effect produced by that sickness its poison pervades all associated

persian_text: زانک واقف بود آن خاتون پاک از غیوری رسول رشکناک
english_translation: Because that chaste lady was aware of the resentfulness of the jealous Prophet

persian_text: دشمنان او را ز غیرت میدرند دوستان هم روزگارش میبرند
english_translation: Foes tear him to pieces from jealousy even friends take his lifetime away

persian_text: من ندیدم جز شقاوت در لامگر تو دیدستی رسان از من سلام
english_translation: I have not seen aught but misery in the vile if thou hast seen  convey the salaam from me

persian_text: چیست نامش گفت نامش بوالحسن حلیهاش وا گفت ز ابرو و ذقن
english_translation: What is his name He replied His name is Bu l Hasan and described his featureshis eyebrows and chin

persian_text: اینکه تو کردی دو صد مادر نکرد عیسی از افسونش با عازر نکرد
english_translation: Two hundred mothers never did this which thou hast done  Jesus by his spells never did to Ázar

persian_text: از پدر او را خفی میداشتش پنج ماهه گشت کودک یا که شش
english_translation: She kept it hidden from her father the child was five or six months old

persian_text: باز دل را که پی تو میپرید از عطای بیحدت چشمی رسید
english_translation: From Thy infinite bounty there came an eye to the falconsoul that was flying for Thy sake

persian_text: دزد راگر عفو باشد جان برد کی وزیر و خازن مخزن شود
english_translation: If a robber be pardoned he saves his life how should he become a vizier and keeper of the treasury

persian_text: چون اشارتهاش را بر جان نهی در وفای آن اشارت جان دهی
english_translation: When you take His signs to heart you will devote your life to fulfilling that indication

persian_text: ابر ما را شد عدو و خصم جان که کند مه را ز چشم ما نهان
english_translation: The cloud is our mortal enemy and adversary because it hides the Moon from our eyes

persian_text: پیش او یکسان شده بد خاک و زر زر چه باشد که نبد جان را خطر
english_translation: Earth and gold were alike in his eyes What of gold his life had no value

persian_text: گر بیابد آلت فرعون او که بامر او همیرفت آبجو
english_translation: If it obtain the means of Pharaoh by whose command the water of the river would flow

persian_text: کاو اگر زهری خورد شهدی شود تو اگر شهدی خوری زهری بود
english_translation: For if he drink poison it turns to honey if you eat honey it is poison

persian_text: با زبان حال زر گوید که باشای مزور تا بر آید روز فاش
english_translation: With the tongue of its state the gold says Wait O tinselled one till day rises clear

persian_text: چشم دیوانه بهارش دی نمود زان طرف جنبید کو را خانه بود
