english_translation: Wilt not thou bring thy headache to the court of God saying Take the blackness away give back the radiance

persian_text: ز آنکه گفتندش که فرمان آن تستگر تو میخواهی عصا بفکن نخست
english_translation: Because they said to him Tis for thee to command thou wishest do thou cast down thy rod first

persian_text: آنچنان کرد و از آن افزون که گفت او بخفت و بخت و اقبالش نخفت
english_translation: Thus He did and more than He said he slept  but his fortune and prosperity slumbered not

persian_text: شد خر نفس تو بر میخیش بند چند بگریزد ز کار و بار چند
english_translation: The ass your fleshly soul has gone off tie it to a peg How long will it run away from work and burden how long

persian_text: دل به دست او چو موم نرم رام مهر او گه ننگ سازد گاه نام
english_translation: In his hand the heart is submissive like soft wax his seal makes now shame now fame

persian_text: با قضا هر کو قراری میدهد ریشخند سبلت خود میکند
english_translation: Any one who offers to make a settlement with destiny is mocking at his own moustache

persian_text: آن خدا را میرسد کو امتحان پیش آرد هر دمی با بندگان
english_translation: To God belongs that  who brings forward a test for His servants at every moment

persian_text: قلتی کان از قناعت وز تقاست آن ز فقر و قلت دونان جداست
english_translation: The penury which arises from contentment and piety is distinct from the poverty and penury of the base

persian_text: جادهی شاهست آن زین سو روان وآن از آنسو صادران و واردان
english_translation: Tis the Kings highway departing and arriving one going in this direction another in that direction

persian_text: چونک در ملکش نباشد حبهای جز پی گنگل چه جوید جبهای
english_translation: Since there is not a mite in his possession how should he seek a coat except in jest

persian_text: کای کریمی که در آن لیل وحش گنج رحمت بنهی و چندین چشش
english_translation: O Gracious One who dost deposit in lonesome Night the treasure of Mercy and all these delicious experiences

persian_text: تا که میخوردست و یا بنگ و حشیش خلق درماندند اندر بیهشیش
english_translation: To see whether he had drunk wine or eaten beng or hashish the people remained in despair at his insensibility

persian_text: خود حسد نقصان و عیبی دیگر است بلکه از جمله کمیها بدتر است
english_translation: indeed envy is another defect and fault nay it is worse than all inferiorities

persian_text: سوی خود کن این خفاشان را مطار زین خفاشیشان بخرای مستجار
english_translation: Make these bats to fly towards Thee and redeem them from this batlike disposition O Thou whose protection is implored

persian_text: چون بجد مشغول باشد آدمی او ز دید رنج خود باشد عمی
english_translation: When a man is busy in earnest he is blind to the sight of his pain

persian_text: از شهی تو چه کم گردد اگر برغلام و بنده اندازی نظر
english_translation: What diminution of your sovereignty will occur if you cast looks on your slave and servant

persian_text: آن گدا گوید خدا از بهر نان متقی گوید خدا از عین جان
english_translation: The beggar says God for the sake of bread the devout man says God from his soul

persian_text: ای فسانه گشته و محو از وجود چند افسانه بخواهی آزمود
english_translation: O thou who hast become a story and dead to existence how long wilt thou wish to make trial of stories

persian_text: پس یهودان مال بردند و خراج که مکن رسوا تو ما راای سراج
english_translation: Then the Jews brought the property and landtax saying Do not put us to shame O Lamp

persian_text: گفت مانا این امام ما ز درد بوالفضولانه مناجاتی بکرد
english_translation: He said It would seem that this Imám of ours by grief has meddlesomely offered an orison

persian_text: هر ملک میگفت ما را پیش از این الفتی میبود بر گرد زمین
english_translation: Every angel was saying  Before this we had a friendship on the face of the earth

persian_text: روی از نقاش رو میتافتی چون ز نقشی انس دل مییافتی
english_translation: You were averting your face from the Painter of the face since you were gaining hearts delight from a picture

persian_text: لیک مرکب را نگه میدار از آن کو بدزدید آن قبایت را نهان
english_translation: But always keep the steed from him who secretly stole your coat

persian_text: همچو آن اصحاب کهف از باغ جود میچرم ایقاظ نی بل هم رقود
english_translation: like the Fellows of the Cave I shall browse on the orchard of Bounty not awake nay they are asleep

persian_text: از نظرگه گفتشان شد مختلف آن یکی دالش لقب داد این الف
english_translation: On account of the place of view their statements differed one man entitled it dál another alif

persian_text: برد خر را روبهک تا پیش شیر پارهپاره کردش آن شیر دلیر
english_translation: The little fox brought the ass into the presence of the lion the courageous lion tore him to pieces

persian_text: و آن امیران دگر اتباع تو کرد عیسی جمله را اشیاع تو
english_translation: And those other amírs are thy followers Jesus hath made all of them thy assistants

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر به گوش چاکرم کاو برد روزی ز گردن این سرم
english_translation: The Prophet said in the ear of my servant that one day he would sever this head of mine from my neck

persian_text: تیزچشم آمد خرد بینای پیش که خدایش سرمه کرد از کحل خویش
english_translation: The intellect is keeneyed possessed of foresight for God hath powdered it with His own collyrium

persian_text: مرغ اندر دام دانه کی خورد دانه چون زهرست در دام ار چرد
english_translation: How should the bird in the trap eat the grain The grain in the trap is like poison  if he feed

persian_text: کار آن دارد که حق افراشته ست آخر آن روید که اول کاشته ست
english_translation: That which God has raised has use what He has at first sown at last grows

persian_text: نیم عمر از آرزوی دلستان نیم عمر از غصههای دشمنان
english_translation: Half of life in desire for a charming friend half of life in anxieties caused by foes

persian_text: ای اخاف الله ما لی منه عون اذهبوا انی اری ما لاترون
english_translation: That is I fear God I have no help from Him Get ye gone Verily I see what ye see not

persian_text: رو که اکنون دست در دولت زدی در فکندی خود به بخت سرمدی
english_translation: Go for now thou hast grasped felicity thou hast thrown thyself into everlasting fortune

persian_text: رهزده و رهزن یقین در حکم و داد در چه بعدند و در بس المهاد
english_translation: Assuredly by the decree and just dispensation  the waylaid and the waylayer are in the pit of farness and in an evil restingplace

persian_text: رنجور شدن هلال و بیخبری خواجهی او از رنجوری او
english_translation: How this Hilál fell ill and how his master was unaware of his being ill

persian_text: تا نشد پر بر سر دریا چو طشت چون که پر شد طشت در وی غرق گشت
english_translation: Until they become full like bowls on the top of the sea when the bowl is filled it sinks therein

persian_text: پس ز دفع این جهان و آن جهان ماندهاند این بیرهان بیاین و آن
english_translation: Therefore through being rejected by this world and by that world these lost ones have been left without either this or that

persian_text: گر بگوید فقه فقر آید همه بوی فقر آید از آن خوش دمدمه
english_translation: If he speak theology it all turns to poverty the scent of poverty comes from that man of sweet and beguiling discourse

persian_text: داس بگرفت و مر آن را میبرید پس ندا از غیب در گوشش رسید
english_translation: He took the sickle and was cutting that  then a voice from the Unseen reached his ear

persian_text: صورتی کردت درون جسم او داد در حملش ورا آرام و خو
english_translation: He bestowed on thee a form within her body He gave ease to her during pregnancy and accustomed her

persian_text: تو بعکسی پیش کوران بهر جاه با حضور آیی نشینی پایگاه
english_translation: Thou art contrariwise for the sake of position thou comest with reverence before them that are blind  and sittest in the vestibule

persian_text: این مثل بشنو که شب دزدی عنید در بن دیوار حفره میبرید
english_translation: Hear this parablehow a wicked thief was cutting a hole at the bottom of a wall

persian_text: از ضمیر او بدانست آن جلیل هم ز نور دل بلی نعم الدلیل
english_translation: The venerable knew his secret thought by the light of the heart yea tis an excellent guide

persian_text: غرهی او حاکم درندگان نعرهی او مانع چرندگان
english_translation: Its roar is the magistrate of the tearing animals its shout is the preventer of the browsing animals

persian_text: نور چشم و مردمک در دیدهات از چه ره آمد به غیر شش جهت
english_translation: The light of the eye and pupil by what other way than the six directions doth it come into your eye

persian_text: گر پدر زد مر پسر را و بمرد آن پدر را خونبها باید شمرد
english_translation: If a father strikes his son and he dies the father must pay the bloodprice

persian_text: برج بادی ابر سوی او برد تا بخارات وخم را بر کشد
english_translation: The aerial sign wafts the clouds towards it that they may sweep away the pestilential vapours

persian_text: گفت شاهش کین پسر از دست رفت گفت اینک آمدم درمان زفت
english_translation: The king said to him This boy has passed out of control  He said Look you I am come as a potent remedy

persian_text: اینچنین تهدیدها آن دیو دون آرد و بر خلق خواند صد فسون
english_translation: Such menaces does the vile Devil employ and he chants a hundred spells over the people

persian_text: مضاحک گفتن درزی و ترک را
english_translation: How the tailor told laughable jests the Turk

persian_text: و آنک صد فرسنگ زان سو بود او چونک بد یعقوب میبویید بو
english_translation: While he that was a hundred leagues from that quarter was smelling the perfume since he was Jacob

persian_text: دم مزن تا بشنوی زان آفتاب آنچ نامد درکتاب و در خطاب
english_translation: Do not speak so that thou mayst hear from the Sun that which came not into book or into allocution

persian_text: کاندر این چشم منیر بیزوال از حقایق راه کی یابد خیال
english_translation: For how should a phantom find the way into this illumined eye which never ceases from the realities

persian_text: باز گفتش من عدوی تو نیام لطف باشدگر بیایی در پیام
english_translation: He answered him  I am not your enemy it would be a kindness if you would come after me

persian_text: گشت فرد از کسوهی خوهای خویش شد برهنه جان به جانافزای خویش
english_translation: He has become separated from the garment of his own dispositions his spirit has gone naked to Him who gives it increase of spirituality

persian_text: قصد من آنست که آید بانگ آب هم ببینم بر سر آب این حباب
english_translation: My purpose is that the sound of the water should come  also that I should see these bubbles on the surface of the water

persian_text: که دعایی همتی تا وا رهم تا ازین بند نهان بیرون جهم
english_translation: A prayer A benediction that I may be delivered and that I may escape from this hidden chain

persian_text: گر ز دلق و پوستین بگذشتمی کی چنین تخم ملامت کشتمی
english_translation: If I had omitted the cloak and sheepskin how should I have sown such seeds of blame

persian_text: که چه میخواهی ز خون مومنان کشته میگردند زین حرب گران
english_translation: To say What do you wish by shedding the blood of true believers They are being killed in this grievous war

persian_text: پس ز مکتب آن یکی صدری شده ماهگانه داده و بدری شده
english_translation: Then by school that child rose to the top paid monthly fees  and became perfect

persian_text: معده طبلیخوار همچون طبل کرد قسم هژده آدمی تنها بخورد
english_translation: He made his voracious belly like a drum he consumed singly the portion of eighteen persons

persian_text: از همه طاعات اینت بهتر است سبق یابی بر هر آن سابق که هست
english_translation: Of all acts of devotion this is the best for thee thou wilt gain precedence over every one that has outstripped

persian_text: نعمتت دادم بگو شکرت چه بود دادمت سرمایه هین بنمای سود
english_translation: I gave thee bounty tell  what were thy thanks I gave thee capital come show the interest

persian_text: چون رسیدم سوی یک ساحل بگام بود بیگه گشته روز و وقت شام
english_translation: When I came on foot to a certain shore the day had turned late and twas eventide

persian_text: خون خوری در چارمیخ تنگنا در میان حبس و انجاس و عنا
english_translation: dost thou drink blood on the gibbet of this narrow place in the midst of confinement and filth and pain

persian_text: گفت یارش کاندر آ ای جمله من نی مخالف چون گل و خار چمن
english_translation: His friend said to him Come in O thou who art entirely myself not different like the rose and thorn in the garden

persian_text: ای غلام اکنون تو پر بین مشک خود تا نگویی درشکایت نیک و بد
english_translation: O slave now behold thy waterskin full  that thou mayst not say good or bad in complaint

persian_text: این محک قرآن و حال انبیا چون منحک مر قلب را گوید بیا
english_translation: The Qurán and the state of the Prophets are this touchstone they like a touchstone say to the counterfeit coin Come

persian_text: ذکر و فکری فارغ از رنج دماغ کردمی با ساکنان چرخ لاغ
english_translation: With a memory and thought free from brainsickness I would frolic with the dwellers in Heaven

persian_text: گفت من گفتم که عهد آن خسان خام باشد خام و سست و نارسان
english_translation: I said cried the lion that the promise of those vile ones would be vainvain and frail and unfulfilled

persian_text: مال دادم بستدم عمر دراز در جزا زیرا که بودم پاکباز
english_translation: I gave the riches away and received long life in return because I was utterly selfsacrificing

persian_text: حکایت آن شخص کی در عهد داود علیه السلام شب و روز دعا میکرد کی مرا روزی حلال ده بیرنج
english_translation: Story of the person who in the time of David on whom be peace used to pray night and day crying Give me a lawful livelihood without trouble

persian_text: مرغ گفتش نی نصیحت کردمت که مبادا بر گذشتهی دی غمت
english_translation: The bird said to him Did not I admonish thee saying Let there be no grief in thee for what passed yesterday

persian_text: پس موکلهای دیگر روز حشر هم تواند آفرید از بهر نشر
english_translation: then He can also create on the Day of Judgement other overseers for the purpose of unfolding

persian_text: ما کهایم اندر جهان پیچ پیچ چون الف او خود چه دارد هیچ هیچ
english_translation: Who are we In this tangled world what indeed hath He like alif Nothing nothing

persian_text: من که خروبم خراب منزلم هادم بنیاد این آب و گلم
english_translation: I who am carob  am the ruin of the abode I am the destroyer of the building of this water and clay

persian_text: قول و فعل بیتناقض بایدت تا قبول اندر زمان بیش آیدت
english_translation: Thou must have words and acts that are not selfcontradictory in order that thou mayst meet with immediate acceptance

persian_text: در بیان آنک ترک الجواب جواب مقرر این سخن کی جواب الاحمق سکوت
english_translation: Showing that  Omission to reply is a reply confirms the saying that silence is the reply to the fool

persian_text: مرغ پر نارسته چون پران شود لقمهی هر گربهی دران شود
english_translation: When an unfledged bird begins to fly it becomes a mouthful for any rapacious cat

persian_text: گر محب حق بود لغیره کی ینال دائما من خیره
english_translation: Whether he love God for something other than He that he may continually partake of His good

persian_text: گفت با اینها ندارم مشورت که یقین سستم کنند از مقدرت
english_translation: He said I will not consult these  for assuredly they will make me weak in power

persian_text: گفت این نبود دگر باره دوید مانده گشت و غیر آن پیر او ندید
english_translation: He said This is not he and ran once more He became tired out and saw none but the old man

persian_text: گویم آنگه که بپرسند از بطون کاشکی بحرم روان بودی درون
english_translation: At the time when they ask me of my inward state I say Would that the Sea were flowing within me

persian_text: کرامات شیخ شیبان راعی قدس الله روحه العزیز
english_translation: The miracles of Shaybán Ráí may God sanctify his venerable spirit

persian_text: پس مگو دنیا به تزویرم فریفت ورنه عقل من ز دامش میگریخت
english_translation: Do not say then The World deceived me by its imposture otherwise my reason would have fled from its snare

persian_text: شب که شاه از قهر در قیرش کشید ننگ دارد از هزاران روز عید
english_translation: The night which the King in his wrath covered with pitch holds in disdain a thousand days of festival

persian_text: از نبی بشنو ضلال رهروان که چشان کرد آن بلیس بد روان
english_translation: Hear from the Qurán the perdition of the wayfarers what the evilsouled Iblís did unto them

persian_text: در ره اسلام و بر پول صراط در سر آید همچو آن خر از خباط
english_translation: In the Way of Islam and on the bridge Sirát he will fall upon his head from giddiness like that ass

persian_text: بر لب جو صوفیی بنشسته بود دست و رو میشست و پاکی میفزود
english_translation: On the riverbank a Súfí was seated washing his hands and face and cleansing himself more and more

persian_text: از جهان دو بانگ میآید به ضد تا کدامین را تو باشی مستعد
english_translation: From the World are coming two cries in opposition  for which thou art adapted

persian_text: سهم آن مار سیاه زشت زفت چون بدید آن دردها از وی برفت
english_translation: As soon as he saw the horror of that black ugly big snake those griefs departed from him

persian_text: گر بخواهد عین کشتی را به خو او کند طوفان توای نورجو
english_translation: He if He will makes a very ark in character to be a flood for you O seeker of light

persian_text: باز گفتی حزم سوء الظن تست هر که بد ظن نیست کی ماند درست
english_translation: Then he would say Prudence consists in your thinking evil how shall he that thinks no evil remain unhurt

persian_text: گرفتار شدن باز میان جغدان به ویرانه
english_translation: The falcon getting trapped among owls in the ruins

persian_text: وقت لاف غزو مستان کف کنند وقت جوش جنگ چون کف بیفنند
english_translation: The drunken make a froth when there is talk of war when war is raging they are as unskilled as froth

persian_text: گر ز که بستانی و ننهی بجای اندر آید کوه زان دادن ز پای
english_translation: If you take away from a mountain and do not put in the place  the mountain will be demolished by that giving

persian_text: آن شه شطرنج دل را مات کرد از بهشتش سخرهی آفات کرد
english_translation: And checkmated that king of the spiritual chessboard and made him from Paradise a thrall to calamities

persian_text: برتر از نوبت ملوک باقیاند دور دایم روحها با ساقیاند
english_translation: Beyond Vicissitude are the kings everlasting their spirits are circling with the Cupbearer perpetually

persian_text: عکس راز مرد حق دانید روز عکس ستاریش شام چشم دوز
english_translation: Know that day is the reflexion of the mystery of the man of God while eyesealing night is the reflexion of his occultation

persian_text: میل معشوقان نهانست و ستیر میل عاشق با دو صد طبل و نفیر
english_translation: The desire of loved ones is hidden and veiled the desire of the lover is with a hundred drums and trumpets

persian_text: بهر فرجه شد یکی تا گلستان فرجهی او شد جمال باغبان
english_translation: A certain man went to the rosegarden to take his pleasure and found it in the beauty of the gardener

persian_text: نقش گل در زیربینی بهر لاغ بوی گل بر سقف و ایوان دماغ
english_translation: The form of the rose is beneath the nose for idle pleasures sake the scent of the rose is on the roof and palace of the brain

persian_text: شیر اندر آتش و در خشم و شور دید کان خرگوش میآید ز دور
english_translation: The lion incensed and wrathful and frantic saw the hare coming from afar

persian_text: خود بدانی چون بر من آمدی که تو بی من نقش گرمابه بدی
english_translation: When thou hast come to me thou thyself wilt know that without me thou wert a picture in the bathhouse

persian_text: این مثل از خود نگفتم ای رفیق سرسری مشنو چو اهلی و مفیق
english_translation: I have not invented this parable O comrade do not hear it silly if thou art worthy and restored to thy senses

persian_text: خیر تو این است جامع میروی تا چه باشد شر و وزرت ای غوی
english_translation: This is thy good thou art going to the mosque what must thy evil and burden be O misguided one

persian_text: اینچنین کژ بازیی در جفت و طاق با نبی میباختند اهل نفاق
english_translation: The Hypocrites played against the Prophet such a crooked game at odd and even

persian_text: بیجنایت بیگنه بیبیش و کم ملحدان جایز ندارند این ستم
english_translation: Without guilt without sin without anything great or small the heretics hold not such oppression allowable

persian_text: پشهی مرده هما را شد شریک چون نشاید زنده همراز ملیک
english_translation: The dead gnat has become the consort of the humá how is the living one not fit to be the confidant of the King

persian_text: از کدامین بند میجویی خلاص وز کدامین حبس میجویی مناص
english_translation: From what chain art thou seeking release and from what imprisonment art thou seeking to escape

persian_text: شرع بیتقلید میپذرفتهاند بیمحک آن نقد را بگرفتهاند
english_translation: They have received the Law without mechanical imitation  they have taken that coin without the touchstone

persian_text: پس چراگوید دعا الا مگر در دعا بیند رضای دادگر
english_translation: Why then should he invoke  unless perchance he see the pleasure of the Judge in invocation

persian_text: آشفتن آن غلام از نارسیدن جواب رقعه از قبل پادشاه
english_translation: How the slave was indignant because no reply to his letter arrived from the king

persian_text: گفت خواری قیامت صعبترگر نداری پاس من در خیر و شر
english_translation: He said The contumely of the Resurrection is more grievous if thou do not pay regard to me in good and evil

persian_text: آب را هر دم کند پوشیده او آن هوا خندان و گریان عقل تو
english_translation: Every moment they cover the water  that desire is laughing and thy intellect is weeping

persian_text: همچو من از مستی شهوت ببر مستی شهوت ببین اندر شتر
english_translation: Be cut off like me from the intoxication of lust look at the intoxication of lust in the camel

persian_text: نقصان اجرای جان و دل صوفی از طعام الله
english_translation: The reduction of the allowance of Gods food for the soul and heart of the Súfí

persian_text: آن یکی مرغی گرفت از مکر و دام مرغ او را گفت ای خواجهی همام
english_translation: A certain man caught a bird by guile and trap the bird said to him O noble sire

persian_text: آنکه کالانعام بد بل هم اضلگر چه پر مکر است آن گنده بغل
english_translation: he that was like the cattle nay more lostthough that stinkard is full of cunning

persian_text: نفی ضد کرد از بهشت آن بینظیر که نباشد شمس و ضدش زمهریر
english_translation: He who hath no like banished contraries from Paradise saying Neither sun nor its contrary intense cold shall be there

persian_text: تو نمییاری برین در آمدن من نمیآرم ز در بیرون شدن
english_translation: Thou canst not come to this door nor can I go forth from the door

persian_text: نعرهها زد خلق جمع آمد برو گبر گویان ایهاالناس احذروا
english_translation: He uttered shrieks the people gathered round him the infidel was crying O people beware

persian_text: آن خسان که در کژیها ماندهاند انبیا را ساحر و کژ خواندهاند
english_translation: The wretches who have remained in unrighteous qualities have called the prophets magicians and unrighteous

persian_text: در زن و فرزند من دستی زند بستن این دزد سودم کی کند
english_translation: He may lay hands upon my wife and child how would it profit me to bind this thief

persian_text: اصل صد یوسف جمال ذوالجلالای کم از زن شو فدای آن جمال
english_translation: The beauty of the Almighty is the source of a hundred Josephs O you who are less than a woman devote yourself to that beauty

persian_text: میکشد حق راستان را تا رشد قسم باطل باطلان را میکشد
english_translation: The truth draws the righteous to righteousness the false class draws the false

persian_text: داند و پوشد بامر ذوالجلال که نباشد کشف راز حق حلال
english_translation: He knows and by command of the Almighty he conceals  for it would not be lawful to divulge the secret of God

persian_text: خندیدن جهود و پنداشتن کی صدیق مغبونست درین عقد
english_translation: How the Jew laughed and imagined that the Siddíq had been swindled in this bargain

persian_text: هین مدو اندر پی نفس چو زاغ کو به گورستان برد نه سوی باغ
english_translation: Beware Do not run in pursuit of the crowlike fleshly soul for it carries to the graveyard not towards the orchard

persian_text: چونک پردهی غیب برخیزد ز پیش تو ببینی روی دلالان خویش
english_translation: When the curtain over the Unseen is raised from before thee thou wilt behold the faces of thy brokers

persian_text: سر این آن بود کز حق خواستم لاجرم بنمود راه راستم
english_translation: The secret of this was that I besought God consequently He showed me the right way

persian_text: گفت کل عمرتای نحوی فناست ز آنکه کشتی غرق این گردابهاست
english_translation: O grammarian said he your whole life is naught because the boat is sinking in these whirlpools

persian_text: گر ترا من گفتمی اوصاف مار ترس از جانت بر آوردی دمار
english_translation: Had I told you the qualities of the snake terror would have made you give up the ghost

persian_text: حرس تازد بیهده سوی سراب عقل گوید نیکبین کان نیست آب
english_translation: Greed runs in vain towards the mirage reason says Look carefully it is not water

persian_text: این فرستادن مرا پیش تو میر هست برهانی که بد مرسل خبیر
english_translation: This sending of me to thee O prince is a clear evidence that the Sender was aware

persian_text: زهر قاتل صورتش شهدست و شیر هین مرو بیصحبت پیر خبیر
english_translation: deadly poison in appearance he is honey and milk Beware do not go save in company with a wise preceptor

persian_text: فاطمه مدح است در حق زنان مرد را گویی بود زخم سنان
english_translation: Fátima is praise in regard to women if you address it to a man tis the blow of a spearhead

persian_text: جز روان پاک او را شرق نه در طلوعش روز و شب را فرق نه
english_translation: It hath no Orient but the pure spirit in its rising there is no difference between day and night

persian_text: وقت مرگ از رنج او را میدرند او بدان مشغول شد جان میبرند
english_translation: At the hour of death he is torn with pain he becomes preoccupied with that and his spirit is taken away

persian_text: جمله شاهان پست، پست خویش را جمله خلقان مست، مست خویش را
english_translation: All kings are prostrate before one who is prostrate before them all people are intoxicated with one who is intoxicated with them

persian_text: طفل یک روزه همیداند طریق که بگریم تا رسد دایهی شفیق
english_translation: The onedayold babe knows the way  I will cry that the kind nurse may come

persian_text: تو ز چشم انگشت را بردار هین و آن گهانی هر چه میخواهی ببین
english_translation: Come lift the finger from thine eye and then behold whatsoever thou wishest

persian_text: پس طبیب آمد بدار و کردنش گفت چاره نیست هیچ از رگزنش
english_translation: Thereupon the physician came to treat him and said There is no resource but to bleed him

persian_text: زین پلیدی بدهد و پاکی برد از یطهرکم تن او بر خورد
english_translation: He gives this filth and gets purity  his body enjoys He will purify you

persian_text: حال او زان روز شد خوب و بدید آن عجایب را که اول میشنید
english_translation: From that day his state became excellent and he saw those wondrous things which at first he was hearing

persian_text: بینیازی از غم منای امیر ده زکات جاه و بنگر در فقیر
english_translation: Thou O prince art unconcerned with my passion pay the poortax on thy high estate look on poor wretch

persian_text: ور خوری حلوا بود ذوقش دمی این اثر چون آن نمیپاید همی
english_translation: Whereas if you eat halwá  its taste is momentary this effect like the other is not enduring for ever

persian_text: خشمهای خلق بهر آشتیست دام راحت دایما بیراحتیست
english_translation: The angers of mankind are for the sake of Peace restlessness is ever the snare for Rest

persian_text: که بیار آن مطهره اینجا به پیش تا بشویم جمله را با دست خویش
english_translation: And said Bring the pail here that I may wash all with my own hand

persian_text: لیک دلهاشان چون قاسی گشته بود آن گنههاشان عبادت مینمود
english_translation: But since their hearts had been hardened their sins appeared as obedient service

persian_text: نه بهانه کرد و نه تزویر ساخت نه لوای مکر و حیلت بر فراخت
english_translation: He made no excuse nor did he invent falsehood nor lift up the banner of deceit and evasion

persian_text: گر کشانم بحث این را من بساز تا سال و تا جواب آید دراز
english_translation: If I develop the investigation of this methodically so that question and answer become lengthy

persian_text: باز پیغمبر به تکذیب صریح قد کذبتم گفت با ایشان فصیح
english_translation: Again the Prophet giving the lie direct said to them in plain terms Ye have lied

persian_text: تو ز کرمنا بنیآدم شهی هم به خشکی هم به دریا پا نهی
english_translation: Thou art a king in virtue of  We have ennobled the sons of Adam thou settest foot both on the dry land and on the Sea

persian_text: نه حیاتش چون حیات او بود نه مماتش چون ممات او بود
english_translation: Neither is his life like his life nor is his death like his death

persian_text: صورت از معنی چو شیر از بیشه دان یا چو آواز و سخن ز اندیشه دان
english_translation: Know that form springs from spirit as the lion from the jungle or as voice and speech from thought

persian_text: گر جوابش نیست میبندد ستیز بر همان دم تا بهروز رستخیز
english_translation: If it has no reply  it will cling obstinately to the same formula till the Day of Resurrection

persian_text: مکرهای جبریانم بسته کرد تیغ چوبینشان تنم را خسته کرد
english_translation: The tricks of the necessitarians have bound me  their wooden sword has wounded my body

persian_text: دست من بشکسته بودی آن زمان که زدم من بر سر آن خوشزبان
english_translation: Would that my hand had been broken at the moment when I struck on the head of that sweettongued one

persian_text: گفت تایید خدا بود ای مهان ور نه خرگوشی که باشد در جهان
english_translation: O Sirs said he it was Gods aid else who in the world is a hare

persian_text: گر نماید غیر هم تمویه اوست ور رود غیر از نظر تنبیه اوست
english_translation: If other appear  tis His illusion and if other vanish from sight tis His awakening

persian_text: هر نواحی منجنیقی از نبرد همچو کوه قاف او بر کار کرد
english_translation: On every side he brought into hostile action a mangonel like Mount Qáf

persian_text: گفت اندر جو تو کمتر کاه کن گفت لاحول این سخن کوتاه کن
english_translation: The Súfí said Put a little straw in his barley Good gracious Cut short this speech he replied

persian_text: آنک او کف دید در گردش بود وانک دریا دید او بیغش بود
english_translation: He that regards the foam is in movement while he that regards the Sea is devoid of hypocrisy

persian_text: ور ترا شکی و ریبی ره زند تاجران انبیا را کن سند
english_translation: And if any doubt and suspicion waylay you rely upon the traders  the prophets

persian_text: دوستان در قصهی ذو النون شدند سوی زندان و در آن رایی زدند
english_translation: The friends went to the prison about the story of Dhu lNún and expressed an opinion concerning it

persian_text: قسمت حقست روزی دادنی هر یکی را سوی دیگر راه نی
english_translation: The dispensation of God is a bestowal of the allotted portion no ones has access to another

persian_text: باقی اعضا ز فکر آسودهاند وآن صدور از صادران فرسودهاند
english_translation: The rest of the members are undisturbed by thinking while those breasts are consumed by thoughts that return

persian_text: تو که در جان و دلم جا میکنی زین قدر از من تبرا میکنی
english_translation: Thou who dwellest in my soul and heart wilt thou declare thyself to be quit of me for this amount

persian_text: یک عرابی بار کرده اشتری دو جوال زفت از دانه پری
english_translation: A certain Arab of the desert loaded a camel with two big sacks one full of grain

persian_text: فارغم از طمطراق و از ریا قل تعالوا گفت جانم را بیا
english_translation: I am done with pomp and ostentation Say come ye He said to my soul Come

persian_text: گر خبر خواهی از این دیگر خروج سوره بر خوان و السما ذات البروج
english_translation: If you desire information about this second outbreak read the chapter of the Qurán  By Heaven which hath the signs

persian_text: آن شنیدی داستان بایزید که ز حال بوالحسن پیشین چه دید
english_translation: Have you heard the story of Báyazídwhat he saw beforehand of the state of Bu lHasan

persian_text: گفت آری گفت پسای روح پاک هرچه خواهی میکنی از کیست باک
english_translation: He said Yea Said the other Then O pure Spirit thou doest whatsoever thou wilt of whom hast thou fear

persian_text: مه همه کفست معطی نورپاش ماه راگر کف نباشد گو مباش
english_translation: The moon is entirely a bounteous lightdiffusing hand if the moon have no hand  let it have none

persian_text: بط عاقل گویدش ای باز دور آب ما را حصن و امنست و سرور
english_translation: the wise duck says to him Away O falcon The water is our stronghold and safety and joy

persian_text: ناز آن ابله کشیم و صد چو او نه ز عشق رنگ و نه سودای بو
english_translation: I endure the disdain of that foolish and a hundred like her neither from love of colour nor passion for scent

persian_text: خوشتر از تجرید از تن وز مزاج نیستای فرعون بی الهام گیج
english_translation: There is nothing sweeter than to be stripped of the body and the temperament O stupid uninspired Pharaoh

persian_text: گر عمر نامی تو اندر شهر کاش کس بنفروشد به صد دانگت لواش
english_translation: If your name is Umar nobody in the town of Kásh will sell you a roll of bread for a hundred dángs

persian_text: چون بمردم از حواس بو البشر حق مرا شد سمع و ادراک و بصر
english_translation: Inasmuch as I am dead to the senses of the father of mankind  God has become my hearing and perception and sight

persian_text: خاک زن در دیدهی حس بین خویش دیدهی حس دشمن عقل است و کیش
english_translation: Throw dust on your senseperceiving eye the sensuous eye is the enemy of intellect and religion

persian_text: هست دوزخ همچو مرگ و خاک گور هست کوثر بر مثال نفخ صور
english_translation: Hell is like death and the earth of the grave Kawthar resembles the blast of the trumpet

persian_text: خار دان آن را که خرما دیدهای ز آنکه بس نان کور و بس نادیدهای
english_translation: Know that the thorn is that which thou because thou art very greedy and very blind hast deemed to be a date

persian_text: و آن دگر در نعل او میجست سنگ و آن دگر در چشم او میدید زنگ
english_translation: And another searched for the stone in his shoe and another looked at the dirt in his eye

persian_text: تا بگوید چون ز چاه آیی به بام جان که یا بشرای هذا لی غلام
english_translation: So that when thou comest from the well to the roof the Soul will say Oh good news for met This is a youth for me

persian_text: بندهای میشد سیه با اشتری راویه پر آب چون هدیهبری
english_translation: A negro slave was going with a camel the waterskin filled with water like one bearing a gift

persian_text: مسجد و اصحاب مسجد را نواز تو مهی ما شب دمی با ما بساز
english_translation: Show favour to the mosque and its founders Thou art the moon we are the night comply with us for a moment

persian_text: این دم ار یارانت با تو ضد شوند وز تو برگردند و در خصمی روند
english_translation: If at this time your friends become hostile to you and turn aside from you and quarrel

persian_text: عاشقان را هر نفس سوزیدنی ست بر ده ویران خراج و عشر نیست
english_translation: To lovers there is a burning at every moment tax and tithe are not on a ruined village

persian_text: کیمیایی که ازو یک ماثری بر دخان افتاد گشت آن اختری
english_translation: the Philosophers Stone from which a single impression fell on the vapour and it became a star

persian_text: خاک بر قانون نفیر آغاز کرد داد سوگندش بسی سوگند خورد
english_translation: The Earth according to rule began lamenting loudly she adjured him she swore many an oath

persian_text: که در این کشتی حرمدان گمشدست جمله را جستیم نتوانی تو رست
english_translation: A bag of valuables said he has been lost in this ship We have searched the whole company you cannot escape

persian_text: گفت اینها را بهل بیهیچ شک این بود حق من و نان و نمک
english_translation: He said Let these things be without any doubt  is it due to me and to the bread and salt

persian_text: لا ابالی مر کسی را شد مباح کش زیان نبود ز غدر و از صلاح
english_translation: I reck not is permitted to that One who loses nothing by perfidy and by probity

persian_text: آن دو گفتندش نصیحت در سمر که مکن ز اخطار خود را بیخبر
english_translation: The two admonished him in converse saying Do not ignore the dangers

persian_text: ور نمودی عیب و کژ کم باختی یک طبیبی داروی او ساختی
english_translation: And if thou hadst shown the ailment and hadst not played false some physician would have prepared a remedy for it

persian_text: گفت موسی این مرا دستور نیست بندهام امهال تو مامور نیست
english_translation: Moses said This is not permitted to me I am the slave  the giving of time to thee is not commanded

persian_text: گفت اگر دیوست من بخشیدمش ور بلیسی کرد من پوشیدمش
english_translation: And said If he is the Devil I forgive him and if he has done a satanic deed I cover it up

persian_text: اینچنین آتشکشی اندر دلش دیدهی کافر نبیند از عمش
english_translation: the infidels eye because of dimness does not see a similar fireextinguisher in his heart

persian_text: طفل راگر نان دهی بر جای شیر طفل مسکین را از آن نان مرده گیر
english_translation: If you give a babe bread instead of milk take it that the poor babe will die of the bread

persian_text: چون بدانی حد خود زین حدگریز تا به بیحد در رسی ای خاکبیز
english_translation: When thou knowest the definition of thyself flee from this definition that thou mayst attain to Him who hath no definition O sifter of dust

persian_text: چونک کرد الحاح بنمود اندکی هیبتی که که شود زومند کی
english_translation: When he persisted  he displayed a little the awful majesty by which a mountain would be reduced to dust

persian_text: بهر آن است امتحان نیک و بد تا بجوشد بر سر آرد زر زبد
english_translation: The testing of good and bad is in order that the gold may boil and bring the scum to the top

persian_text: میکشید از بیهشیاش در بیان اندکاندک از کرم صدر جهان
english_translation: The Sadri Jahán from kindness was drawing him little by little from senselessness into clear expression

persian_text: همره جانت نگردد ملک و زر زر بده سرمه ستان بهر نظر
english_translation: Sovereignty and gold will not accompany thy spirit on its journey give thy gold away get collyrium for thy sight

persian_text: گریهی او نه از غمست و نه از فرح روح داند گریهی عین الملح
english_translation: His weeping is neither from sorrow nor from joy the spirit knows the weeping of the fountain of beauties

persian_text: گر ز گوری خشت بر تو اوفتاد عاقلان از گور کی خواهند داد
english_translation: If bricks from the grave have fallen on you how should reasonable persons seek redress from the grave

persian_text: بایزید ار این بود آن روح چیست ور وی آن روحست این تصویر کیست
english_translation: If Báyazíd is this  what is that spirit And if he is that spirit who is this image

persian_text: فایدهی اول سماع بانگ آب کاو بود مر تشنگان را چون رباب
english_translation: The first advantage is hearing the noise of the water which to thirsty men is as a rebeck

persian_text: لوت خوردند و سماع آغاز کرد خانقه تا سقف شد پر دود و گرد
english_translation: They ate the viands and began the samá  the monastery was filled with smoke and dust up to the roof

persian_text: بعد از آنت جان احمد لب گزد جبرئیل از بیم تو واپس خزد
english_translation: After that the spirit of Ahmad will bite thy lip  and Gabriel will creep back in fear of thee

persian_text: چون نخواهی من کفیلم مر ترا جنت الماوی و دیدار خدا
english_translation: When thou desirest nothing  I am thy surety for the Garden of resort and the vision of God

persian_text: پای داری چون کنی خود را تو لنگ دست داری چون کنی پنهان تو چنگ
english_translation: You have feet why do you make yourself out to be lame you have hands why do you conceal the fingers

persian_text: چونک کردندی سرش سوی یمن پیل نر صد اسپه گشتی گامزن
english_translation: whenever they turned its head towards Yemen the fierce Elephant would begin to stride with the speed of a hundred horses

persian_text: دانش پیشه ازین عقل ار بدی پیشهی بیاوستا حاصل شدی
english_translation: If knowledge of a trade were from this intellect any trade would be acquired without a master

persian_text: این مثال آورد ابلیس لعین تا که شد ملعون حق تا یوم دین
english_translation: The accursed Iblís used this comparison so that he fell under Gods curse till the Day of Judgement

persian_text: مقالت برادر بزرگین
english_translation: The discourse of the eldest brother

persian_text: ای بسا ظلمی که بینی از کسان خوی تو باشد در ایشانای فلان
english_translation: Oh many an iniquity that you see in others is your own nature in them O reader

persian_text: ور به خرگه بگذرد بیگانه رو حمله بیند از سگان شیرانه او
english_translation: But if any one having the face of a stranger pass by the tent he will see the dogs rushing at him like lions

persian_text: فرع دید آمد عمل بیهیچ شک پس نباشد مردم الا مردمک
english_translation: Without any doubt action is a branch of seeing  therefore Man is nothing but the little man

persian_text: بینهایت گردد آبش بعد از آن پر شود از کوزهی من صد جهان
english_translation: after that its water will become without end a hundred worlds will be filled from my jug

persian_text: ایمنی بگذار و جای خوف باش بگذر از ناموس و رسوا باش و فاش
english_translation: Let safety go and dwell in the place of fear  leave reputation behind and be disgraced and notorious

persian_text: تا رسی اندر نفوس زوجت کای شه مسحور اینک مخرجت
english_translation: That thou mayst attain unto the souls that are wedded for lo this O ensorcelled prince is thy way of escape

persian_text: سال کردن از عیسی علیهالسلام کی در وجود از همهی صعبها صعبتر چیست
english_translation: How they asked Jesus on whom be peace saying O Spirit of God what is the hardest thing to bear of all the hard things in existence

persian_text: الصلا گفتیم ای اهل رشاد کین زمان رضوان در جنت گشاد
english_translation: We cry welcome O people of righteousness for now Rizwan hath opened the gate of Paradise

persian_text: چون که گردانید سر سوی زمین در کمی و خشکی و نقص و غبین
english_translation: When it has turned its head towards the earth in decrease and dryness and failure and disappointment

persian_text: غیر دنیا پس چه باشد آخرت کت کند زینجا و باشد رهبرت
english_translation: What then is other than the present world The next world which will take you away from here and be your guide

persian_text: حرف درویشان و نکتهی عارفان بستهاند این بیحیایان بر زبان
english_translation: These shameless persons have attached to their tongues the speech of dervishes and the deep sayings of gnostics

persian_text: کیمیای مرگ و جسکست آن صفت مرگ گردد زان حیاتت عاقبت
english_translation: That quality is the elixir of death and woe thereby thy life is at last turned into death

persian_text: لیک بعضی زین صدا کرتر شدند باز بعضی صافی و برتر شدند
english_translation: But some have become more deaf to this echo some again have become purified and have mounted higher

persian_text: ور شوم نومید نومیدی من عین صنع آفتاب استای حسن
english_translation: And if I become despairing my despair is the objective manifestation of the Suns work O goodly

persian_text: روز بر جست و دو چشم کور دید نور فایض از دو چشمش ناپدید
english_translation: At day he jumped up and found two blind eyes from both his eyes the overflowing light had vanished

persian_text: چون ملایک مانع آن میشدند بر ملایک خفیه خنبک میزدند
english_translation: When the angels were opposing that  they were secretly clapping their hands at the angels

persian_text: دست چون بر وی زد او از جا بجست گفت هی تو کیستیای سگپرست
english_translation: Cum manum ei injiceret exsiluit puer eho inquit tu quis homo es O canis cultor When his hand touched him he jumped up from his place hey he asked who are you O worshipper of dogs

persian_text: هر که را بدهی و بکنی آن کرم او سلیمان است و آن کس هم منم
english_translation: To whomsoever Thou wilt give  and Thou wilt confer that bounty he is Solomon and I also am he

persian_text: ور نداری پا بجنبان خویش را تا ببینی هر کم و هر بیش را
english_translation: And if thou hast no foot  bestir thyself that thou mayst behold every less and more

persian_text: گر نبودی شب همه خلقان ز آز خویشتن را سوختندی ز اهتزاز
english_translation: If there were no night on account of cupidity all people would consume themselves by the agitation

persian_text: هر چه او درخواست از نان یا سبوس چربکی میگفت و میکردش فسوس
english_translation: Whatever he asked for from bread to bran he was mocking and deriding him

persian_text: چشم داند فرق کردن رنگ را چشم داند لعل را و سنگ را
english_translation: The eye can distinguish colours the eye knows ruby and stone

persian_text: رجوع به داستان آن کمپیر
english_translation: Return to the tale of the old woman

persian_text: خواست صوفی تا دو سه مشتش زند سبلت و ریشش یکایک بر کند
english_translation: The Súfí was about to give him two or three blows with his fist and tear out his moustache and beard piecemeal

persian_text: تو کجایی تا که خندان چون چمن گویی بستان آن و ده چندان ز من
english_translation: Where art thou that laughing like the garden thou mayst say Receive that and ten times as much from me

persian_text: منازعت چهار کس جهت انگور که هر یکی به نام دیگر فهم کرده بود آن را
english_translation: How four persons quarrelled about grapes which were known to each of them by a different name

persian_text: بایزید اندر سفر جستی بسی تا بیابد خضر وقت خود کسی
english_translation: Báyazíd on his journey  sought much to find some one that was the Khizr of his time

persian_text: که ببر این را بغلطاق فراخ ز امتحان پیدا شود او را دو شاخ
english_translation: Saying Cut this into a wide undervest  from the trial there appear two horns on him

persian_text: روح را از عرش آرد در حطیم لاجرم کید زنان باشد عظیم
english_translation: She brings the spirit from the highest heaven to the Hatím  great must be the craft of women

persian_text: این سزای آنکه یابد آب صاف همچو خر در جو بمیزد از گزاف
english_translation: This is the punishment deserved by him that finds pure water and like an ass stales impertinently in the stream

persian_text: هر که اندر کار تو شد مرگدوست بر دل تو بی کراهت دوست اوست
english_translation: Whosoever in matter of thine has become deathloving without dislike to thy heart he is beloved

persian_text: دم بخور در آب ذکر و صبر کن تا رهی از فکر و وسواس کهن
english_translation: Swallow your breath in the water of recollection and show fortitude that you may be freed from the old thought and temptation

persian_text: شیر چون وا گشت از چشمه به خور جست در خر دل نه دل بد نه جگر
english_translation: When the lion returned from the spring to eat  he looked in the ass to find the heart there was neither heart nor liver

persian_text: صد هزاران آفرین بر جان او بر قدوم و دور فرزندان او
english_translation: A hundred thousand blessings on his spirit and on the advent and cycle of his sons

persian_text: ای عجب کز آتش بیزینهار بسته میآریمتان تا سبزهزار
english_translation: O wonder that we are bringing you in bonds from the merciless fire to the place abounding in verdure

persian_text: های و هویی کرد شیخ و باز راند کودکان را باز سوی خویش خواند
english_translation: The Shaykh gave a loud cry of jubilation and rode back he again called the children to him

persian_text: ره نمایم همرهت باشم رفیق من قلاووزم درین راه دقیق
english_translation: I will show the way I will be your kind fellowtraveller I am the guide on this intricate path

persian_text: خویشتن را با شما همصف کنند پس گریزند و دل صف بشکنند
english_translation: They put themselves in line with you  then they flee and break the heart of the line

persian_text: آن یکی زد سیلیی مر زید را حمله کرد او هم برای کید را
english_translation: A certain man slapped Zayd on the neck he at once rushed at him with warlike purpose

persian_text: خنجر تیزی تو اندر قصد ما نیش زهر آلودهای در فصد ما
english_translation: Thou art a keen dagger to assail us thou art a poisoned lancet to bleed us

persian_text: پس ملایک را چو ما هم یار دان جلوهگر خورشید را بر آسمان
english_translation: Know then that the angels as we also are helpers displayers of the sun in heaven

persian_text: زان همیپرسی چرا این میکنی که صور زیتست و معنی روشنی
english_translation: You ask Why art thou doing this because the forms are the oil and the essential meaning is the light

persian_text: چون درون خود بدید آن خوشپسر از سیهکاری خود گرد و اثر
english_translation: When the comely youth felt within himself the dust and effects of his wicked behaviour

persian_text: میرود هر روز در حجرهی خلا چارقت اینست منگر درعلا
english_translation: Every day he would go into the private chamber  These are thy shoon do not regard thy eminence

persian_text: دیدهام خود بارها این خواب من که به بغدادست گنجی در وطن
english_translation: I myself have often dreamed that there is a treasure in the dwellingplace at Baghdád

persian_text: هر زمان میدرد این دلق تنت پاره بر وی میزنی زین خوردنت
english_translation: This cloak thy body is always being torn and thou art patching it by this eating and drinking of thine

persian_text: گریهی پر جهل و پر تقلید و ظن نیست همچون گریهی آن متمن
english_translation: A weeping full of ignorance and conformity and opinion is not like the weeping of that trusted one

persian_text: هر کسی بر قوم خود ایثار کرد کاغه پندارد که او خود کار کرد
english_translation: Every one shows favour to his own folk the fool thinks he has really done work

persian_text: پس جز او جمله مقلد آمدند در صفات مردم دیده بلند
english_translation: Therefore all except him are imitators in regard to the attributes of the sublime man of the eye

persian_text: پرتو حق است آن معشوق نیست خالق است آن گوییا مخلوق نیست
english_translation: She is a ray of God she is not that beloved she is creative you might say she is not created

persian_text: وقت دم آهنگر ار پوشید دلق احتشام او نشد کم پیش خلق
english_translation: If the ironsmith wore a patched frock when blowing the bellows his reputation was not impaired in the eyes of the people

persian_text: همچنانک آن جنین را طمع خون کان غذای اوست در اوطان دون
english_translation: Even as in the case of the embryo desire for the blood which is its nourishment in the low abodes

persian_text: هم عرب ما هم سبو ما هم ملک جمله ما یؤفک عنه من أفک
english_translation: We are both the Arab and the jug and the King we are all he that has been turned away from it shall be turned away

persian_text: مشورت میرفت در ایجاد خلق جانشان در بحر قدرت تا به حلق
english_translation: consultation was going on as to bringing mankind into existence their spirits were in the Sea of Omnipotence up to the throat

persian_text: که زیان کردم عسس را از گریز بیست چندان سیم و زر بر وی بریز
english_translation: Saying I caused loss to the nightpatrol by fleeing  scatter oer him twenty times as much silver and gold

persian_text: باز گفتش ای حکیم خوشسخن شمهای از حال خود هم شرح کن
english_translation: He said to him again O fairspoken sage explain a little about your own circumstances as well

persian_text: گوش ما گیر و بدان مجلس کشان کز رحیقت میخورند آن سرخوشان
english_translation: Take hold of our ear and draw us along to the assembly where the joyous revellers drink of Thy wine

persian_text: شه معین دید منزلگاهشان حلیه و نام و پناه و راهشان
english_translation: The King saw distinctly their lodgingplace and their personal appearance and names and refuge and way

persian_text: گفت دلقک را سوی زندان برید چاپلوس و زرق او را کم خرید
english_translation: Take Dalqak to prison he said and pay no attention to his wheedling and hypocrisy

persian_text: سایسی کردی در آخر آن غلام لیک سلطان سلاطین بنده نام
english_translation: The youth served as a groom in the stable but a king of kings and a slave in name

persian_text: گفت او هم از ضرورت کای اسد از چو من لاغر شکارت چه رسد
english_translation: He too of necessity said O lion what will come to thee from such a meagre prey as I am

persian_text: لیک آن صوفی ز مستی دور بود لاجرم در حرص او شبکور بود
english_translation: But that Súfí was far removed from intoxication consequently he was nightblind in greed

persian_text: رنگ سبزت زرد شد ای قرع زود زانک از گلگونه بود اصلی نبود
english_translation: Your green colour soon turned yellow  O pumpkin for it was derived from rouge it was not original

persian_text: جوق مرغان از برون گرد قفص خوش همیخوانند ز آزادی قصص
english_translation: outside round the cage a multitude of birds is sweetly chanting tales of liberty

persian_text: معجزات از بهر قهر دشمنست بوی جنسیت پی دل بردنست
english_translation: Miracles are for the purpose of subjugating the foe the scent of homogeneity is for the winning of hearts

persian_text: گفت پیغامبر که ان فی البیان سحرا و حق گفت آن خوش پهلوان
english_translation: The Prophet said Verily there is a magic in eloquence and that goodly hero spake the truth

persian_text: میزد او بر سر کای بیعقل سر میزد او بر سینه کای بینور بر
english_translation: He smote his head saying O head without understanding He smote his breast saying O bosom without light

persian_text: در حقیقت هر عدو داروی تست کیمیا و نافع و دلجوی تست
english_translation: In reality every foe is your medicine he is an elixir and beneficial and one that seeks to win your heart

persian_text: آن امیران در شفاعت آمدند و آن مریدان در شناعت آمدند
english_translation: Those amírs came for intercession and the disciples came in reproach

persian_text: خلق پرسیدند کای عم رسولای هزبر صفشکن شاه فحول
english_translation: The people asked him saying O uncle of the Prophet O Lion that breakest the ranks  O prince of the champions

persian_text: ای بسا کس رفته تا شام و عراق او ندیده هیچ جز کفر و نفاق
english_translation: How many a one has gone as far as Syria and Iráq and has seen nothing but unbelief and hypocrisy

persian_text: در یکی گفته که عجز خود مبین کفر نعمت کردن است آن عجز هین
english_translation: In one he said Do not regard your weakness that weakness is an act of ingratitude Beware

persian_text: همچنین هر یک به جزوی که رسید فهم آن میکرد هر جا میشنید
english_translation: Similarly whenever any one heard  he understood the part that he had touched

persian_text: دل ز دانشها بشستند این فریق زانک این دانش نداند آن طریق
english_translation: This company washed their hearts of kinds of knowledge because this knowledge does not know that Way

persian_text: مغفرت میخواهد از جرم و خطا از پس آن پرده از لطف خدا
english_translation: he is craving forgiveness for his sins and trespasses from the grace of God behind the veil

persian_text: وآن دگر که برد مروارید خرد وآن دگر که سنگریزه و شبه برد
english_translation: And the other who got the small pearls and the other who got pebbles and worthless shells

persian_text: باز گفتندی که گرچه او شکست چون شکست ما نبود آن زشت و پست
english_translation: again they were saying  Although he was defeated it was not disgraceful and vile like our defeat

persian_text: فکرگاهش کند شد عقلش خرف عمر شد چیزی ندارد چون الف
english_translation: His brain became dull his mind doting his life is goneand like alif he hath nothing

persian_text: این در آن حیران که او از چیست خوش وآن درین خیره که حیرت چیستش
english_translation: This one is amazed at that one and asks wherefore he is happy while that one is astounded at this one and asks why he is amazed

persian_text: آن عصای حزم و استدلال را چون نداری دید میکن پیشوا
english_translation: When you have not the staff of prudence and judgement make the eye your leader

persian_text: پیش قدرت خلق جمله بارگه عاجزان چون پیش سوزن کارگه
english_translation: Before Omnipotence all the people of the court of audience are as helpless as the fabric before the needle

persian_text: هین بیا اینسو بر آوازم شتاب عالم ار برفست روی از من متاب
english_translation: Hey come quickly in this direction towards my voice if the world is snow do not turn thy face away from me

persian_text: آن تاسف و آن فغان و آن نیاز در گذشتی از دو صد ذکر و نماز
english_translation: And that regret and that lamentation and that yearning would have exceeded two hundred litanies and prayers

persian_text: سرکشی از بندگان ذو الجلال دان که دارند از وجود تو ملال
english_translation: If you turn away your head from the servants of the Almighty know that they are disgusted by your existence

persian_text: بیان آنک مجموع عالم صورت عقل کلست
english_translation: Explaining that the whole world is the form of Universal Reason

persian_text: بعد از آن میگفت هینای سابقانگر فزون آیید اندر امتحان
english_translation: After that he was saying Hark O ye that are foremost  if ye prove superior in the trial

persian_text: عقل ما کو تا ببیند غرب و شرق روحها را میزند صد گونه برق
english_translation: Where is our reason that it should perceive the spiritual West and East flashing forth a hundred kinds of splendour
